<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094045 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00072: (2026x) Exposing elements for views

- page_id: `298094045`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094045/2026x+Exposing+elements+for+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:30:58.229+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Once you have[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating views' space='UAF2P'], you can specify what model elements can be displayed by the view using the expose relationship. The exposed elements can be further narrowed down using[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Filtering elements for views' space='UAF2P'].

Elements can be exposed for views only via the Textual Editor. For displaying exposed elements on views, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in views' space='UAF2P'] page.

##### Element exposition kinds and visibility

Expose relationship is a kind of import relationship and is thus declared like an import relationship, but using the keyword *expose* instead of *import*. However, unlike imports, whose visibility can be modified, expose relationships always have *protected* visibility, which means that the elements exposed by a view usage are not publicly visible outside the view usage.

The exposition kind is specified as a specific suffix added to the exposed elements qualified name.

| Exposition kind | Suffix | Exposed elements | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Membership exposition | none | Only the specified namespace (i.e., element import). | part vehicleInformation { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}view 'Vehicle Details' { // membership exposition: expose vehicleInformation; // exposes vehicleInformation} |
| Namespace exposition | ::* | The content of the specified namespace. | part vehicleInformation { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}view 'Vehicle Details' { // namespace exposition: expose vehicleInformation::*; // exposes vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord} |
| Recursive membership exposition | ::** | The specified namespace and its content, including sub-namespaces and their elements. | part vehicleInformation { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}view 'Vehicle Details' { // recursive membership exposition: expose vehicleInformation::**; // exposes vehicleInformation, vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel} |
| Recursive namespace exposition | ::*::** | The content of the specified namespace, including sub-namespaces and their elements. | part vehicleInformation { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}view 'Vehicle Details' { // recursive namespace exposition: expose vehicleInformation::*::**; // exposes vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel} |

##### Exposing elements

To expose elements

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the body of the view element for which you want to expose the element(s).
2. Declare the keyword expose .
3. Specify the qualified name of the membership/namespace you want to import, followed by the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exposing elements for views' space='UAF2P'].
4. (Optional) Specify an element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Filtering elements for views' space='UAF2P'] .
5. Click the Synchronize button.

You can specify multiple *expose* relationships for the same view.

partvehicleInformation{partvehicleSpecs{}partvehicleRecord{itemvehicleModel;}}

view'Vehicle Details':DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv,DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter{exposevehicleInformation::**;// recursive membership exposition of *vehicleInformation*, *vehicleSpecs*, *vehicleRecord*, *vehicleModel*}

#### NOTE: Reviewing exposed elements

Reviewing exposed elements

To review the elements exposed for a specific element, open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space='UAF2P']**Specification** window and check the values of the **Exposed Element**property.

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:01:34.512+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements > (2026x) Managing elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Over time, your model may grow to contain usage elements with repetitive features, thus the use of typing by definition elements would be useful. The Extract Definition command allows you to extract a definition element with the features of the usage element. This improves feature reusability, allowing you to easily generate definition elements that can be used for typing other usage elements.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']On the left: the same attributes *diameter*and *width* are each created for the parts *leftFrontWheel*and *rightFrontWheel*, resulting in duplicated elements that serve the same purpose. 
On the right: once the Extract Definition command is executed on the *rightFrontWheel*element, the definition is extracted with the part's attributes. 
The *rightFrontWheel*element is then defined by the extracted definition, inheriting its attributes. The *leftFrontWheel*part can then be defined by the definition as well.

##### Extracting a definition element from a usage element

Once you execute the Extract Definition command, all owned features, except for the redefined ones, are moved to the definition, which is then set as the type for the usage. All features that have been moved are then inherited instead of being owned by the usage.

To extract a definition element from a usage element

- Right-click a usage element(s) or its symbol(s) and in the shortcut menu select Refactor , then Extract Definition . The appropriate definition element is created.

##### Extracting a usage element

You can also use the Extract Usage command to extract a usage element. This is especially useful when reusing and adapting [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Use case scenario' space='UAF2P'], and states to different contexts.

Once you execute the Extract Usage command:

- The extracted usage is owned by the same package and named after the element from which it was extracted;
- The extracting usage reference subsets the extracted usage;
- All owned features of the extracting usage are moved to the extracted usage, except for the redefined ones;
- All features that have been moved are then inherited instead of owned by the extracting usage.

**Extract Usage**command is only available for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Perform action' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Include use case' space='UAF2P'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exhibit state' space='UAF2P'], unless they reference other elements.

To extract a usage element from a perform action usage, include use case usage, or exhibit state usage element

- Right-click a perform action usage, include use case usage, or exhibit state usage element(s) or its symbol(s), and in the shortcut menu select Refactor , then Extract Usage . The appropriate action, use case, or state usage element is created.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093529 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00074: (2026x) Feature membership

- page_id: `298093529`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093529/2026x+Feature+membership
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:50:44.922+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Relations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use feature memberships to specify element features.

- Feature membership relationship is created automatically in the tool when you create a feature for an element.
  - For element creation procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing elements' space='UAF2P'] page. You can also create features through the creation of a feature membership itself. This is especially useful for element decomposition, such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Part decomposition' space='UAF2P'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Requirement decomposition' space='UAF2P'].
- For element ownership change procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Element ownership change' space='UAF2P'] page.
- The feature membership display can differ based on the related elements. If one of the elements related via a feature membership is a referential usage, it is displayed with a hollow diamond icon, whereas a composite usage is displayed with a filled diamond icon. For procedures on referential usages, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifiers' space='UAF2P'] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifiers' space='UAF2P'] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Specifying feature memberships

###### Specifying feature memberships via the Textual Editor

To specify feature memberships via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, use curly braces after element declaration to specify the element body. Features specified within the element body become related to the owning element via the feature membership. To indicate a referential (non-composite) usage, use the keyword *ref*before the element keyword. For more procedures on referential usages, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifiers' space='UAF2P'] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifiers' space='UAF2P'] page.
2. Click the Synchronize button. part'input unit'{ 
partmouse;// composite usagerefpartmonitor;// referential (non-composite) usage}

###### Specifying feature memberships via the smart manipulator in a view

Creating features via the smart manipulator is only available for a limited selection of elements (e.g., parts, requirements).

To specify feature memberships via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating parts' space='UAF2P'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] the part for which you want to specify a feature membership.
2. In the selected symbol's smart manipulator, click the feature membership icon and do one of the following:
  1. To create a composite feature, click elsewhere in the view pane. The new composite feature of the same type is created.
  2. To designate an existing element as a composite one, click on the element you want to designate as the composite feature.

###### Specifying feature memberships via the view palette

To specify feature memberships via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the **feature membership** button.
2. In a view, click on the element symbol you want to designate as the owner, then the one you want to designate as the owned feature. A feature membership path symbol is created between the elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

If one of the elements related via a feature membership is a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2c31701e-4275-4971-8e9b-c06657bb835a">referential usage, it is displayed with a hollow diamond icon</ac:inline-comment-marker>, whereas a composite usage is displayed with a filled diamond icon. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// referential (non-composite) usage<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Specifying feature memberships via the smart manipulator in a view</h4><p>Creating features via the smart manipulator is only available for a limited selection of elements (e.g., parts, requirements).</p><p>To specify feature memberships via the smart manipulator in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating parts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[display]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the part for which you want to specify a feature membership.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the selected symbol's smart manipulator, click the</span><strong style="text-align: left;"><span> </span></strong><strong>feature membership</strong> <span>icon and do one of the following:</span><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="47f0256c-a451-4389-8d47-12c211b97602">To create a composite feature, </ac:inline-comment-marker><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="47f0256c-a451-4389-8d47-12c211b97602">click elsewhere in the view pane. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093554 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00075: (2026x) Feature typing

- page_id: `298093554`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093554/2026x+Feature+typing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:51:04.851+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Relations > (2026x) Specializations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use thefeature typing relationship tospecify a definition for a usage.

##### Feature typing

###### Defining an element via the Textual Editor

To define an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the usage element that you want to define.
2. Follow the element name with either one of the following:
  1. keyword defined by
  2. textual symbol : (colon)
3. Follow the keyword with the name of the definition element you want to be the defining one.
4. Click the Synchronize button. partdieselEngine:Engine; 
/* or */ 
partdieselEnginedefined byEngine;

###### Defining an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To define an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P'] on an element symbol executing the Set Type command.

###### Defining an element via the view palette

To define an element via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, select feature typing .
2. Draw the line with the arrowhead going from the element you want to be defined to the defining one. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Defining an element via the Create Relationship command

To define an elementvia the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click the element that you want to define.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click Outgoing , and then in the submenu, click feature typing . The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the defining one. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the defining one and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the defining one from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK .

###### Defining an element via the Specification panel

To define an element via the Specification panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] page.

Hold Shift to select multiple elements. </li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Feature typing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the defining one and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the defining one from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Element Picker</strong>.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong>Element Picker</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h4>Defining an element via the Specification panel</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To define an element via the Specification panel</span></p><hr /><ul><li>See the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the feature type via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modifying the feature type via the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094049 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00076: (2026x) Filtering elements for views

- page_id: `298094049`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094049/2026x+Filtering+elements+for+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:25:57.410+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can specify filters for view definitions and/or specific views to narrow down what elements can be displayed by views. Once you have[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating views' space='UAF2P'], specified the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exposing elements for views' space='UAF2P'], you can then optionally filter the exposed elements. Views have the capability to filter elements upon exposition based on certain conditions.Only elements that meet all filter conditions are exposed for the view.

Elements can be filtered for views only via the Textual Editor.

##### Filtering elements for views

Ways to perform element filtering for views:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Filtering elements for views' space='UAF2P'] . You can specify filter conditions directly for the specific view, using the keyword filter .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Filtering elements for views' space='UAF2P'] . You can also filter elements for views through inheritance, e.g., if the view is defined by a view definition that contains filter conditions, the filters are inherited by and applied to the defined view.

###### Specifying filter conditions

- Filter conditions can be used only for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Package' space='UAF2P'] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing and filtering elements' space='UAF2P']) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Views basics' space='UAF2P'] (element exposition) elements.
- Filter condition expression must be model-level evaluable. Any SysML v2 expression can be used for the filter condition specification, but it must return *true/false*.
- @ , istype , and hastype classification operators are commonly used to check if an element has the given metadata annotation.
- Element metaclass metadatas are located in the used projects at Standard Libraries > SysML > Systems . Filter conditions require a qualified name for metadata-based filtering (e.g., @SysML::Systems::PartUsage ).

To specify filter conditions

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the body of the view.
2. Declare the keyword filter followed by filter condition expression, including the needed operator and the qualified name of the element you want to expose .
3. Click the Synchronize button.

packageMetadataDefinitions{metadatadefSafety{attributeisMandatory:Boolean;}}

packageVehicle{ 
privateimportMetadataDefinitions::**; 
partvehicle{ 
partinterior{ 
partalarm; 
partseatBelt[2] {@Safety{isMandatory=true;}} 
partfrontSeat[2]; 
partdriverAirBag{@Safety{isMandatory=false;}} 
} 
} 
}

view'Vehicle Features'{ 
exposeVehicle::**;// exposes package *Vehicle*// you can use multiple filter condition declarations: 
filter@SysML::Systems::PartUsage;// exposes part usage elements from the package *Vehicle* 
filter@MetadataDefinitions::SafetyandMetadataDefinitions::Safety::isMandatory;// exposes elements with *Safety* metadata attribute *isMandatory*value *true*from previously filtered part usage elements exposed from the package *Vehicle* 
}

###### Inheriting filter conditions

**Filters in predefined view definitions:**

- Standard predefined [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbolic views' space='UAF2P']/[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular views' space='UAF2P'] view definitions (except for *RequirementsTable*) do not have any specified filters, meaning, any elements can be exposed by these views. Once you have created such a view, you may want to specify filters to limit the displayed elements.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] contain:
  - Element filters specified in the *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter*and *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter*view definitions, narrowing down what elements can be exposed by the view by discarding various standard library elements and other non-essential elements that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views.
  - Additionally, most of the predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions contain additional filters to narrow down the exposed elements, e.g., *PartsTreeView*view definition contains filters specifying that only part usage elements can be exposed.

**Recommendations**:

- The easiest way to filter exposed elements is to reuse predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions. See *Example 1*.
  - Keep in mind that the renderers specified for the view definitions are also inherited.
- If you only want to discard uncommon library elements from being displayed in a view, you can reuse the *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter*and *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter***view definitions. See *Example 2*.

#### PANEL: Example 1

Example 1

viewdefNonStandardLibraryElementFilter { 
// see the *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter* view definition in the modeling tool to view all filters specified for it 
} 
viewdefTreeView :> EssentialElementsFilter, NonStandardLibraryElementFilter { // the *TreeView*view definition subclassifies the two view definitions inheriting their filters 
renderViews::asTreeDiagram; // the specified renderer will be inherited 
}

viewdefPartsTreeView :> TreeView { // the *PartsTreeView*view definition subclassifies *TreeView* inheriting all filters and renderer 
filterhastypePartUsage; 
}

viewnewView:DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::PartsTreeView{ // the specific view is defined by *PartsTreeView*view definition inheriting all filters and renderer 
exposeVehicle::**; 
}

#### PANEL: Example 2

Example 2

viewdefNonStandardLibraryElementFilter { 
// see the *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter* view definition in the modeling tool to view all filters specified for it 
}

viewnewView: DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter, DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter{ // the specific view is defined by the view definitions inheriting their filters 
exposeVehicle::**; 
}

// the view is defined by the *Interconnection View* view definition (inheriting its renderer) and view definitions specifying filters:viewnewView: DS_Views::SymbolicViews::iv, DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter, DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter{exposeVehicle::**;}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the <em>PartsTreeView </em>view definition subclassifies <em>TreeView</em> inheriting all filters and renderer</span></span><br /><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093127 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00077: (2026x) Find dialog

- page_id: `298093127`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093127/2026x+Find+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:06:51.780+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model management > (2026x) Working with Textual Editor > (2026x) Textual Editor features
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can invoke the Find dialog to search for or replace specific data in the textual notation.

##### Invoking the Find dialog

To invoke the Find dialog

- Use the Ctrl+F shortcut keys to invoke the Find dialog.

##### Searching for data in the textual notation via the Find dialog

To search for data in the textual notation via the Find dialog

1. Invoke the Find dialog.
2. In the Find field, enter the word you are searching for. The found instances are highlighted throughout the notation.

##### Replacing data in the textual notation via the Find dialog

You can use the Find dialog to replace specific data in the textual notation.

To replace data in the textual notation via the Find dialog

1. Invoke the Find dialog.
2. In the Find field, input the word you want to replace.
3. On the left side of the dialog, click the right-facing arrow. The Find dialog is expanded.
4. In the Replace field, input the replacement word.
5. Do one of the following:
  1. To replace the highlighted instance of the word, either click the Replace button on the dialog or Enter on the keyboard.
  2. To replace all instances of the word, either click the **Replace** **All**button on the dialog or Ctrl+Alt+Enter on the keyboard. All instances of the word can also be changed via the **Change All Occurrences**(Ctrl+F2) command.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">You can invoke the Find dialog to search for or replace specific data in the textual notation. </p><h3>Invoking the Find dialog</h3><p style="text-align: left;">To invoke the Find dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>Use the Ctrl+F shortcut keys to invoke the Find dialog.</li></ul><h3>Searching for data in the textual notation via the Find dialog</h3><p>To search for data in the textual notation via the Find dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Invoke the Find dialog.</li><li>In the <strong>Find</strong> field, enter the word you are searching for.<br />The found instances are highlighted throughout the notation.</li></ol><h3>Replacing data in the textual notation via the Find dialog</h3><p>You can use the Find dialog to replace specific data in the textual notation. </p><p>To replace data in the textual notation via the Find dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Invoke the Find dialog.</li><li>In the <strong>Find</strong> field, input the word you want to replace.</li><li>On the left side of the dialog, click the right-facing arrow. The Find dialog is expanded.</li><li>In the <strong>Replace</strong> field, input the replacement word.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ol><li>To replace the highlighted instance of the word, either click the <strong>Replace</strong> button on the dialog or Enter on the keyboard.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To replace all instances of the word, either click the <strong>Replace</strong> <strong>All </strong>button on the dialog or Ctrl+Alt+Enter on the keyboard.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a00e8947-6778-4457-a9d6-a064b6f6f518"><ac:rich-text-body><p>All instances of the word can also be changed via the <strong>Change All Occurrences </strong>(Ctrl+F2) command.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093522 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00078: (2026x) Flow

- page_id: `298093522`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093522/2026x+Flow
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:12:21.484+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Relations > (2026x) Connectors
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A flow is a kind of connection that specifies a transfer from a source output to a target input (and, optionally, the payload). A flow is a transfer that continues while the source and target actions execute. The transferred payload can be anything (attribute value, item, part, etc.).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The image displays the *fuelFlow* flow connectorfrom *fuelTank.fuelOut*to*engine.fuelIn*with the payload *flowingFuel*defined by *Fuel*.Additionally, the connector is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P'].

##### Creating flow usage and definition elements

###### Creating flow usage elements via the Textual Editor

To create flow usage elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the flow keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Flow' space='UAF2P'] (optional), then the from keyword and the name of the source element and output feature. Then follow with the keyword to and the name of the target element and input feature. If the flow has no name and no specialization, the keyword *from*can be omitted.
3. Click the Synchronize button. flow fuelFlow:FuelFlowfromfuelTank.fuelOuttoengine.fuelIn; // named and specialized (feature typing) flowflow:FuelFlowfromfuelTank.fuelOuttoengine.fuelIn; // unnamed specialized(feature typing) flowflow fuelFlowfromfuelTank.fuelOuttoengine.fuelIn;// named unspecializedflowflowfuelTank.fuelOutto engine.fuelIn; // unnamed unspecializedflow can omit the keyword *from*

###### Creating flow usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To create flow usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the flow command.
2. A dropdown menu appears. Select the output element in the menu.
3. Click on the target element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the compatible input element in the menu. A flow usage is created between the elements.

###### Creating flow definition and usage elements via the view palette

To create flow definition and usage elements via the view palette

- To create a flow definition:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **flow** def button to create a flow definition element.
  2. Click in the view pane where you want the flow definition symbol to be created.
- To create a flow usage:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **flow** button to create a flow usage.
  2. In a view, click on the source element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the output element in the menu.
  3. Then click on the target element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the compatible input element in the menu. A flow usage is created between the elements. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **flow**or the **flow****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.

###### Creating flow definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create flow definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the *flow* def keyword.
2. Name the element, then specify the flow definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the flow definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or specialization of the needed element.
3. Click the Synchronize button. flow def FuelFlow{ 
ref :>>payload:Fuel; 
 end portfuelIn:FuelIn; 
 end portfuelOut:FuelOut; 
}

###### Creating flow definition elements via the Create Element command

To create flow definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, click flow def for flow definition.

###### Auto-creating flow definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create a flow definition element via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='UAF2P']page.

##### Setting the payload for the flow

While the payload name and sign are displayed on the flow in a view by default, you can hide them using the **Show Payload** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space='UAF2P'].

###### Setting the payload for the flow via the Textual Editor

To set the payload for the flow via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor before the keyword*from*within the flow for which you want to set a payload.
2. Declare the keyword of followed by either of the following:
  1. The name of the payload specialized by a definition element.
  2. The name of the definition element you want to set as payload for the flow.
3. Click the Synchronize button. flow:FuelFlowofFuelfromfuelTank.fuelOuttoengine.fuelIn; // specialized(feature typing) flow with payload set as a definition elementflow: FuelFlowof flowingFuel :FuelfromfuelTank.fuelOutto engine.fuelIn; // specialized(feature typing) flow with a named and specialized (feature typing) payloadflow of Fuel from fuelTank.fuelOutto engine.fuelIn; // unspecialized flowwith payload set as a definition element

###### Setting the payload for the flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To set the payload for the flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P'] on a flow element symbol executing the **Set Payload** **** command.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A flow is a kind of connection that specifies a transfer from a source output to a target input (and, optionally, the payload). A flow is a transfer that continues while the source and target actions execute. </em></span><em>def</em> keyword.</li><li>Name the element, then specify the flow definition features in the element body:<ol><li>Specify the flow definition ends by declaring the ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c00fd817-e309-4d8e-839d-5b21596e9da9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>flow def</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelFlow</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">{</span><br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   ref</span> :&gt;&gt;<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">payload</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Fuel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br style="text-align: left;" />   end port<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelIn</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br style="text-align: left;" />   end port<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating flow definition elements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create flow definition elements via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, click <strong>flow def</strong> for flow definition.</li></ol><h4>Auto-creating flow definition elements via the Extract Definition command</h4><p><span>To auto-create a flow definition element via the Extract Definition command</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extract Definition and Usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul><h3>Setting the payload for the flow</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b2449c9-18d1-417d-a7a1-208643954cb0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>While the payload name and sign are displayed on the flow in a view by default, you can hide them using the <strong>Show Payload</strong> <ac:link ac:anchor="The Symbol Style dialog"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To set the payload for the flow via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">within the flow for which you want to set a payload.</span></p></li><li>Declare the keyword <em>of </em>followed by either of the following:<ol><li>The name of the payload specialized by a definition element. </li><li>The name of the definition element you want to set as payload for the flow. </li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0ebeef5-7016-4332-ba47-0dc7e6fe8373"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093618 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00079: (2026x) Frame concern

- page_id: `298093618`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093618/2026x+Frame+concern
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-11T19:20:54.061+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Requirements > (2026x) Concerns
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A framed concern usage is a subrequirement usage, which will automatically be considered a required constraint of its containing requirement definition or usage.

There are two ways concerns can be framed:

50%

- by referencing a concern defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the frame keyword (unnamed element) or the frame concern keyword (named element). 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - See the procedures:
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Frame concern' space='UAF2P'] :
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Frame concern' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Frame concern' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Frame concern' space='UAF2P']

50%

- by containing an owned frame concern usage, i.e., the framed concern is the frame concern usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the frame concern keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='frame concern 1.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Frame concern' space='UAF2P'] :
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Frame concern' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Frame concern' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Frame concern' space='UAF2P']

##### Framing a concern by referencing a concern defined elsewhere in the model

###### Framing concerns via the Textual Editor

To frame concerns via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to frame a concern, do one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed frame concern, declare the frame keyword followed by the name of the framed concern.
  2. For a named frame concern, declare the frame concern keyword, specify the element name, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space='UAF2P'] the framed concern.
2. Specify the concern body.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

concernbrakingConcern{subjectvehicle:Vehicle;stakeholderdriver:Person;attributemaxBrakingDistance:DistanceValue;}

requirementbrakingRequirement{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
actorenvironment:'Driving Environment'; 
attributespeedLimit:SpeedValue; 
attributemaxBrakingDistance:DistanceValue;

framebrakingConcern{ // step 1.a - unnamed usage frames another concern usage via reference subsetting 
:>>maxBrakingDistance=brakingRequirement::maxBrakingDistance; 
}

frame concernbrakingRequirementConcern ::> brakingConcern{ // step 1.b - named usage frames another concern usage via reference subsetting 
 :>> maxBrakingDistance = brakingRequirement::maxBrakingDistance; 
 } 
}

###### Framing concerns via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To frame concerns via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop a concern element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P'] on the needed element symbol, executing the Create Frame Concern command.

###### Framing concerns via the Specification panel

To frame concerns via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop a concern element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler and drop it on the Required Constraint field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the frame concern usage. The appropriate concern is specified for the element.

##### Framing a concern via the owned frame concern usage itself as the framed concern

###### Framing concerns via the Textual Editor1Framing concerns via the Textual Editor

To frame concerns via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to frame a concern, declare the frame concern keyword followed by its name.
2. Specify the concern body.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

requirementbrakingRequirement{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
actorenvironment:'Driving Environment'; 
attributespeedLimit:SpeedValue; 
attributemaxBrakingDistance:DistanceValue;

frameconcernbrakingRequirementConcern{ // named framed concern 
attributemaxBrakingDistance:DistanceValue; 
:>>maxBrakingDistance=brakingRequirement::maxBrakingDistance; 
} 
}

###### Framing concerns via the symbol compartment in a view

To frame concerns via the symbol compartment in a view

1. Do one of the following:
  1. Select a requirement symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the frame concern element.
  2. Select a requirement symbol and on the right side of the frames compartment, click the Create frame concern button. The new feature is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.
2. Specify the concern. The new element is created and displayed in the frames compartment.

###### Framing concerns via the Specification panel2Framing concerns via the Specification panel

To frame concerns via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **frame concern**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **frame concern**compartment section's bar, click the **Create frame concern**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
3. Specify the concern. The element is created in the frames compartment.

subsetting</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span>        :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">maxBrakingDistance</span> = <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">brakingRequirement::maxBrakingDistance</span>;<br />    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Framing concerns via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p>To frame concerns via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="63e06baa-7a5c-4ac1-8ac2-7c4ff55dca4a">Drag and drop a concern element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Drag and Drop handler" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Drag and Drop handler]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the needed element symbol, executing the <strong>Create Frame Concern </strong>command.</li></ul><h4>Framing concerns via the Specification panel</h4><p>To frame concerns via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="828b094e-9d38-497d-a1b9-0a09aaa5a170">Drag and drop a concern element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler and drop it on the <strong>Required Constraint</strong><strong> </strong>field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the frame concern usage. <span><br /></span>The appropriate concern is specified for the element.</li></ul><h3>Framing a concern via the owned frame concern usage itself as the framed concern</h3><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b41e5ef-6b50-426d-996a-1394065931fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Framing concerns via the Textual Editor1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Framing concerns via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To frame concerns via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target" data-uuid="ba583a33-985d-4fe8-bc04-dbb67c93bc68">In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to frame a concern, declare the <em>frame concern </em><span>keyword followed by its name.</span></li><li data-uuid="13bcefc5-adbd-4a8b-8573-717387c12a3c"><span>Specify the concern body.</span></li><li class="auto-cursor-target" data-uuid="16ce83a1-4a40-483a-ba09-bafe6f5a3078"><span>Click the </span><strong>Synchronize </strong><span>button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71efa7a2-2e9e-4d61-8363-d4801b33a1ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Driving Environment'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">frame</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">concern</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">brakingRequirementConcern</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {    <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named framed concern</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">maxBrakingDistance</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">maxBrakingDistance</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">brakingRequirement::maxBrakingDistance</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Framing concerns via the symbol compartment in a view</h4><p>To frame concerns via the symbol compartment in a view</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target" data-uuid="b6b0328e-d9ba-46d2-b694-157f2d2cc03a">Do one of the following:<ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">Select a requirement symbol and click the <strong>Create Element</strong> button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the <strong>frame concern</strong> element.</li><li>Select a requirement symbol and on the right side of the <strong>frames </strong>compartment, click the<span> </span><strong>Create frame concern<span> </span></strong>button.<br />The new feature is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="b83bf122-d4cd-4230-a076-3e5a6a2cc021"><span>Specify the concern.<br /></span>The new element is created and displayed in the<span> </span><strong>frames </strong><span>compartment.</span></li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d414ba3b-9c4a-4caf-a84a-0d155acc1633"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Framing concerns via the Specification panel2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Framing concerns via the Specification panel</h4><p>To frame concerns via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="6c573eb6-94fa-42ff-ba0f-b46c7c65312e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="f5ed96db-58cc-4186-a2ab-de95afb1d6f6"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Frame concern" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>frame concern </strong>element in the shortcut menu.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>frame concern </strong>compartment section's bar, click the <strong>Create frame concern </strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Frame concern" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="d478800f-428f-4a64-9dac-fecbd00a582e">Specify the concern.<br />The element is created in the <strong>frames </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">compartment.</span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093823 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00082: (2026x) Guard conditions

- page_id: `298093823`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093823/2026x+Guard+conditions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:21:32.588+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Actions > (2026x) Controlling actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A succession within the body of an action definition or usage may be given a guard condition represented as a Boolean expression, forming a conditional succession. A conditional succession****asserts that the target action can start only if the guard condition is *true*.The conditional succession shorthand notation is particularly useful for notating several conditional successions outgoing from a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Control nodes' space='UAF2P']*decide* node.

##### Specifying guard conditions for successions

###### Specifying guard conditions for successions via the Textual Editor

To specify guard conditions for successions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor between the succession's source and target and declare the *if*keyword followed by the condition. The target of a conditional succession must be specified as a qualified name or feature chain and cannot be a full action usage declaration, even when the shorthand notation is used.You can specify multiple guards by declaring multiple conditional successions.
2. (Optional) Declare the *else*keyword followed by its succession target. It indicates a succession to be taken if the guards evaluate to *false*.
3. Click the**Synchronize**button.

// ------------ Full conditional succession

actiontransportPassenger{ 
... 
decide decideNode;firstdecideNode; // specifies the succession source 
ifanotherDestinationthenmergeNode; // 'ifanotherDestination' specifies a guard condition, followed by a succession 'thenmergeNode' 
elsedone; // specifies a succession to be taken if the guard condition evaluates to *false* 
}

//------------Shorthandconditionalsuccession

actiontransportPassenger{... 
decidedecideNode; // omits the succession sourceas it is identified from the lexically previous action 'decidedecideNode' 
ifanotherDestinationthenmergeNode; // 'ifanotherDestination' specifies a guard condition, followed by a succession 'thenmergeNode' 
elsedone; // specifies a succession to be taken if the guard condition evaluates to *false* 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A succession within the body of an action definition or usage may be given a guard condition <span>represented as a Boolean expression, forming a conditional succession. A conditional succession<strong> </strong>asserts that the target action can start only if the guard condition is <em>true</em>. </span>The conditional succession shorthand notation is particularly useful for notating several conditional successions outgoing from a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Control nodes" /><ac:link-body><em>decide</em> node</ac:link-body></ac:link>. </p><h3>Specifying guard conditions for successions</h3><h4>Specifying guard conditions for successions via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify guard conditions for successions via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor between the succession's source and target and declare the <em>if </em>keyword followed by the condition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5d45654-d808-4e06-802a-1473bbb0c766"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The target of a conditional succession must be specified as a qualified name or feature chain and cannot be a full action usage declaration, even when the shorthand notation is used.</li><li>You can specify multiple guards by declaring multiple conditional successions.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(Optional) Declare the <em>else </em>keyword followed by its succession target. It indicates a succession to be taken if the guards evaluate to <em>false</em>.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the </span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d297bbee-9a34-46ce-9e00-8c715472d3a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// ------------ Full conditional succession</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transportPassenger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">    ...</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>decide</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">decideNode</span>;   <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">decideNode</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                                          <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span>// specifies the succession <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">source </span></span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">if</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">anotherDestination</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mergeNode</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;      <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span>// '<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">if</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">anotherDestination' <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">specifies a guard condition, followed by a succession '<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mergeNode'</span></span></span></span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">else</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">done</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                                                     <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span>// specifies a succession to be taken if the guard condition evaluates to <em>false</em></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">------------ </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">Shorthand<span> conditional </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">succession<span> </span></span></span></span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transportPassenger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">    ...</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">decide</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">decideNode</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                                         <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span>// omits the succession <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">source </span></span>as it is identified from the lexically previous action '<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">decide</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">decideNode'</span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">if</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">anotherDestination</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mergeNode</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;     <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span>// '<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">if</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">anotherDestination' <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">specifies a guard condition, followed by a succession '<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mergeNode'</span></span></span></span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">else</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">done</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                                                    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span>// specifies a succession to be taken if the guard condition evaluates to <em>false</em></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093827 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00083: (2026x) If action

- page_id: `298093827`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093827/2026x+If+action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:21:39.440+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Actions > (2026x) Controlling actions > (2026x) Structured control actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An if action usage evaluates a condition expression and then performs a then clause action usage if the expression evaluates to *true*, or, optionally, an else clause action usage if the expression evaluates to *false*.

##### Specifying if actions for actions

###### Specifying if actions for actions via the Textual Editor

To specify if actions for actions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the if action.
2. Specify the if action usage:
  1. Do one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed if action usage, declare the keyword if .
    2. For a named if action usage, declare the keyword action , specify the element name, and then the keyword if .
  2. Follow with the condition expression for the if action usage.
  3. Specify a then clause by doing one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed then clause, follow with the body for the then clause.
    2. For a named then clause, declare the keyword action , specify the element name, and follow with the body for the then clause.
3. (Optional) Specify the else if clause action usage:
  1. Do one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed else if action usage, declare the keyword else if .
    2. For a named else if action usage, declare the keyword else , then the keyword action , specify the element name, and then the keyword if .
  2. Follow with the condition expression for the if action usage.
  3. Specify a then clause by doing one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed then clause, follow with the body for the then clause.
    2. For a named then clause, declare the keyword action , specify the element name, and follow with the body for the then clause.
4. (Optional) Specify the else clause action usage:
  1. Do one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed else action usage, declare the keyword else .
    2. For a named else action usage, declare the keyword else , then the keyword action , and specify the element name.
  2. Specify the body for the else clause.
5. Click the Synchronize button.

// unnamed if, then clause, and else clause action usages: 
actiontestAlarm{ 
ifthreat.level==high{// unnamed if action usage followed by the if condition expression and an unnamed then clause action usage 
performsoundAlarm{incause=threat;}// body of the then clause action usage 
}elseifthreat.level==medium{// unnamed else clause action usage which is itself an if action usage, followed by the if condition expression and an unnamed then clause action usage 
actionsendNotification{inmsg=threat;}// body of the else if action usage 
}else{// unnamed else clause action usage 
actionbeginMonitoring{intarget=threat;}// body of the else action usage 
} 
}

// named if, then clause, and else clause action usages: 
actiontestAlarm{ 
actionthreatHighifthreat.level==highactionalarm{// named 'threatHigh' if action usage followed by the if condition expression and a named 'alarm' then clause action usage 
performsoundAlarm{incause=threat;}// body of the 'alarm' then clause action usage 
}elseactionthreatMediumifthreat.level==mediumactionnotify{// named 'sendNotification' else clause action usage which is itself an if action usage, followed by the if condition expression and a named 'notify' then clause action usage 
actionsendNotification{inmsg=threat;}// body of the 'sendNotification' else if action usage 
}elseactionmonitor{// named 'monitor' else clause action usage 
actionbeginMonitoring{intarget=threat;}// body of the 'monitor' else action usage 
} 
}

###### Specifying if actions for actions via the view palette

To specify if actions for actions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Other Actions group, click the if button to create an if action usage element.
2. Click on an action symbol in the view pane where you want to create the if symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the element via the Textual Editor.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093436 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00084: (2026x) Importing and filtering elements

- page_id: `298093436`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093436/2026x+Importing+and+filtering+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:06:08.500+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An import relationship allows one namespace to import memberships from another namespace. The member elements from imported memberships become (unowned) members of the importing namespace in addition to being members of the imported namespace.

##### Element import kinds and visibility

###### Import kinds

The import kind is specified as a specific suffix added to the import's qualified name.

| Import kind | Suffix | Imports | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Membership import | none | Only the specified namespace (i.e., element import). | package 'Vehicle Information' { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}package 'Vehicle Details' { // membership import: private import 'Vehicle Information'; // imports 'Vehicle Information'} |
| Namespace import | ::* | The content of the specified namespace. | package 'Vehicle Information' { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}package 'Vehicle Details' { // namespace import: private import 'Vehicle Information'::*; // imports vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord} |
| Recursive membership import | ::** | The specified namespace and its content, including sub-namespaces and their elements. | package 'Vehicle Information' { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}package 'Vehicle Details' { // recursive membership import: private import 'Vehicle Information'::**; // imports 'Vehicle Information', vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel} |
| Recursive namespace import | ::*::** | The content of the specified namespace, including sub-namespaces and their elements. | package 'Vehicle Information' { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}package 'Vehicle Details' { // recursive namespace import: private import 'Vehicle Information'::*::**; // imports vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel} |

###### Import visibility

The import [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Visibility' space='UAF2P'] is specified as a specific keyword declared prior to the *import* keyword. The import visibility keywords are as follows:

- public - membership is visible outside the namespace.
- private - membership is invisible from outside the namespace.
- protected - membership is invisible outside the namespace, except in inheritance, where it behaves like public.

##### Element import

###### Importing elements

To import elements

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to import the element(s).
2. Specify the import [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing and filtering elements' space='UAF2P'] , declaring the needed keyword.
3. Declare the keyword import .
4. Specify the qualified name of the membership/namespace you want to import, followed by the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing and filtering elements' space='UAF2P'].
5. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing and filtering elements' space='UAF2P'] .
6. Click the Synchronize button.

You can specify multiple*import*relationships for the same view.

package'Vehicle Information'{partvehicleSpecs{}partvehicleRecord{itemvehicleModel;}}

package'Vehicle Details'{ 
privateimport'Vehicle Information'::**;// private recursive membership import of '*Vehicle Information', vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel* 
}

#### NOTE: Reviewing imported elements

Reviewing imported elements

To review the elements imported for a specific element, open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space='UAF2P']**Specification** window and check the values of the **Imported Membership**property.

###### Importing library elements

Import is especially useful for accessing library elements instead of using a qualified name each time.

#### SECTION: Import instead of qualified name

#### COLUMN: Qualified name instead of import

50%

#### PANEL: Qualified name instead of import

Qualified name instead of import

// instead of using a qualified name each time:

package'Employee Data'{ 
partemployeeData{ 
// accessing 'String' and 'Real' data types from the *ScalarValues* library via qualified name: 
itememployeeName:ScalarValues::String; 
itemID:ScalarValues::Real; 
} 
}

#### COLUMN: Import instead of qualified name

50%

#### PANEL: Import instead of qualified name

Import instead of qualified name

// use import to access library elements:

package'Employee Data'{ 
private importScalarValues::**; // importing *ScalarValues* library 
partemployeeData{ 
itememployeeName:String; // no need for qualified name 
itemID:Real; // no need for qualified name 
} 
}

##### Element import filtering

Namespaces have the capability to filter elements upon import based on certain conditions, usually defined in terms of the metadata provided by annotations of those elements. Only elements that meet all filter conditions become imported members of the namespace.

- Filter conditions can be used only for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Package' space='UAF2P'] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing and filtering elements' space='UAF2P']) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Views basics' space='UAF2P'] (element exposition) elements.
- Filter condition expression must be model-level evaluable. Any SysML v2 expression can be used for the filter condition specification, but it must return*true/false*.
- @ , istype , and hastype classification operators are commonly used to check if an element has the given metadata annotation.
- Element metaclass metadatas are located in used projects at Standard Libraries > SysML > Systems . Filter conditions require a qualified name for metadata-based filtering (e.g., @SysML::Systems::PartUsage ).

###### Separately declared filter conditions

To specify separately declared filter conditions

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the body of the package after an import declaration.
2. Declare the keyword filter followed by filter condition expression, including the needed operator and the qualified name of the element you want to import .
3. Click the Synchronize button.

#### PANEL: Separately declared filter conditions

Separately declared filter conditions

packageMetadataDefinitions{metadatadefSafety{attributeisMandatory:Boolean;}}

packageVehicle{ 
privateimportMetadataDefinitions::**; 
partvehicle{ 
partinterior{ 
partalarm; 
partseatBelt[2] {@Safety{isMandatory=true;}} 
partfrontSeat[2]; 
partdriverAirBag{@Safety{isMandatory=false;}} 
} 
} 
}

package'Vehicle Features'{ 
privateimportVehicle::**; // imports package *Vehicle*// you can use multiple filter condition declarations:filter@SysML::Systems::PartUsage;// imports part usage elements from the package *Vehicle*filter@MetadataDefinitions::SafetyandMetadataDefinitions::Safety::isMandatory;// imports elements with *Safety*metadata attribute *isMandatory*value *true*from previously filtered part usage elements imported from the package *Vehicle*}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

namespace.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Element import kinds and visibility </h3><h4><span style="letter-spacing: style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><h4>Import visibility</h4><p>The import <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Visibility" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[visibility]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is specified as a specific keyword declared prior to the <em>import</em> keyword. The import visibility keywords are as follows:</p><ul><li><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">public</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> - membership is visible outside the namespace.</span></li><li><em>private</em> - membership is invisible from outside the namespace.</li><li><em>protected</em> - membership is invisible outside the namespace, except in inheritance, where it behaves like public.</li></ul><h3>Element import</h3><h4>Importing elements</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To import elements</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to import the element(s).</span></li><li>Specify the import <ac:link ac:anchor="Import visibility"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[visibility]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing and filtering elements" /></ac:link>, declaring the needed keyword.</li><li>Declare the keyword <em>import</em>.</li><li>Specify the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">qualified name of the membership/namespace you want to import, followed by the <ac:link ac:anchor="Import kinds"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[appropriate suffix for a recursive/membership/namespace import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing and filtering elements" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">(Optional) </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Element import filtering"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify a filter for the import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing and filtering elements" /></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Details'</span><span
