style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db258086-34ec-4ea1-a064-14f563cef664"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Reviewing imported elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To review the elements imported for a specific element, open its <ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification window"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification window" /><ac:link-body><strong>Specification</strong> window</ac:link-body></ac:link> and check the values of the <strong>Imported Membership </strong>property.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span>Importing library elements</span></h4><p><span>Import is especially useful for accessing library elements instead of using a qualified name each time.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="87754a6c-049d-479c-b354-43e6c90afd90"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69db5776-53c2-46a2-a8a0-9b9a0fd7ffdf"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="deb9aa25-bd7f-488c-b27c-339db1203e0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Qualified name instead of import</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// instead of using a qualified name each time:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> '</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Employee Data'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeData</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        // accessing 'String' and 'Real' data types from the <em>ScalarValues</em> library via qualified name:</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ScalarValues::</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">String</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ID</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ScalarValues::Real</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c26e52dd-43b8-47c4-9728-dbfedf2fb90c"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1eb6a049-d753-4d74-84ed-e805e9ca1c62"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Import instead of qualified name </ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// use import to access library elements:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> '<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Employee Data'</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ScalarValues</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::**;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// importing <em>ScalarValues</em> library</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeData</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">String</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// no need for qualified name</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ID</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Real</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// no need for qualified name</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Element import filtering</h3><p>Namespaces have the capability to filter elements upon import based on certain conditions, usually defined in terms of the metadata provided by annotations of those elements. Only elements that meet all filter conditions become imported members of the namespace.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69834189-7a61-4af4-ac3a-db7ce99b7a9b"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>Filter conditions can be used only for <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (<ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[element import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing and filtering elements" /></ac:link>) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Views basics" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (element exposition) elements.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Filter condition expression must be model-level evaluable. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Any SysML v2 expression can be used for the filter condition specification, but it must return </span><em style="text-align: left;">true/false</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></span></li><li><em>@</em>, <em>istype</em>, and <em>hastype</em> classification operators are commonly used to check if an element has the given metadata annotation.</li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Element metaclass metadatas are located in used projects at </span><em style="text-align: left;">Standard Libraries &gt; SysML &gt; Systems</em><em style="text-align: left;">. </em>Filter conditions require a qualified name for metadata-based filtering (e.g., <em>@SysML::Systems::PartUsage</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">).</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Separately declared filter conditions</h4><p><span>To specify separately declared filter conditions </span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the body of the package after an <em>import</em><em> </em>declaration.</li><li>Declare the keyword<span> </span><em>filter </em>followed by filter condition expression, including the needed operator and the qualified name of the element you want to import<span>.</span></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d35791f4-83c6-41f4-87e5-d3d23b9af69c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Separately declared filter conditions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MetadataDefinitions</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Safety</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isMandatory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Boolean</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>private<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>import<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MetadataDefinitions</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">interior</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">alarm</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">seatBelt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">] { </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">@</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Safety</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isMandatory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span>true<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;} </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">frontSeat</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driverAirBag</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">@</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Safety</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isMandatory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span>false<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;} </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Features'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>private<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>import<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::**;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// imports package <em>Vehicle</em> <br /></span></span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">    // you can use multiple filter condition declarations:<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> @ </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML::Systems::PartUsage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// imports part usage elements from the package <em>Vehicle<br /></em></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> @ </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MetadataDefinitions::Safety</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">and</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">MetadataDefinitions::Safety::isMandatory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span>imports elements with <em>Safety </em>metadata attribute <em>isMandatory </em>value <em>true </em>from previously filtered part usage elements imported from the package <em>Vehicle <br /></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093753 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00085: (2026x) Include use case

- page_id: `298093753`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093753/2026x+Include+use+case
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:20:02.851+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Use cases
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An include use case usage is a use caseusage that is also a kind of perform action usage. A use case definition or usage may contain an include use case usage to specify that the behavior of the containing use case includes the behavior of the included use case. The subject of the included use case is the same as the subject of the containing use case, so the subject parameter of the included use case must have a definition that is compatible with the definition of the containing use case. Actor parameters of the included use case may be bound to corresponding actor parameters of the containing use case as necessary.

An include use case usage can be a feature of:

- a part definition or usage, specifying that the referenced use case is included in the containing part.
- a use case definition or usage, whereby the behavior of the containing use case includes the behavior of the included use case.

There are two ways a use case can be included by another element:

50%

- by referencing the use case usage defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the include keyword (unnamed element) or the include use case keyword (named element). [ATTACHMENT filename='include_symbol.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='include_use_case_symbol_2.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Include use case' space='UAF2P'] :
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Include use case' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Include use case' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Include use case' space='UAF2P']

50%

- by containing an owned include use case, i.e., the included use case is the include use case usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the include use case keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='include_use_case_symbol.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Include use case' space='UAF2P'] :
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Include use case' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Include use case' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Include use case' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Include use case' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Include use case' space='UAF2P']

##### Including a use case by referencing a use case usage defined elsewhere in the model

###### Including use cases via the Textual Editor1Including use cases via the Textual Editor

To include use cases via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to include a use case, do one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed included use case, declare the include keyword followed by the name of the included use case.
  2. For a named one, declare the include use case keyword, specify the element name, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space='UAF2P'] the included use case.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

use case 'drive vehicle';//referenced use case

use case 'provide transportation' {

// unnamed included use case referencing a use case defined elsewhere in the model 
 include 'drive vehicle' {}

// named included use case referencing a use case defined elsewhere in the model 
 include use case enter references 'drive vehicle' {} 
 include use case enter ::> 'drive vehicle' {}

}

###### Including use cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To include use cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag a use case element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P'] and drop it on:
  - the target element symbol that you want to include the use case by referencing it using the **Create Include Use Case** command.
  - the include use case usage symbol that you want to include the use case by referencing it using the **Set Use Case** **Included** command.

###### Including use cases via the Specification panel1Including use cases via the Specification panel

To include use cases via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop a use case element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler and drop it on the **Included Use Case** field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the include use case element. The appropriate use case is specified for the element.

##### Including a use case via the ownedinclude use caseusageitselfas theincluded use case

###### Including use cases via the Textual Editor

To include use cases via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to include a use case, declare the include use case keyword followed by its name.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

usecase'provide transportation'{

// included use case is the defined include use case usage itself contained by the parent 
 include use case 'drive vehicle' {} 
}

###### Including use cases via the Create Element command

To include use cases via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select the include use case element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the included use case's features.

###### Including use cases via the view palette

To include use cases via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Cases group, click the include use case button.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the include use case symbol.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the included use case's features.

###### Including use cases via the symbol compartment in a view

To include use cases via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select a use case symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the **include use case** button. The element is created and displayed in the includes compartment.
  - Select a use case symbol and on the right side of the includes compartment, click the Create include use case button. The element is created and displayed in the includes compartment.

###### Including use cases via the Specification panel

To include use cases via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] for the use case element.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the include use case button in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **includes** compartment section's bar, click the **Create include use case** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element is created in the **includes** compartment.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

A use case definition or usage may contain an include use case usage to specify that the behavior of the containing use case includes the behavior of the included use case. cases via the Textual Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Include use case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Including use cases via the panel1"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Including use cases via the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Include use case" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55cf0080-3662-4a6b-ad60-74d13ed03eaf"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="2b9a4a1a-1bb9-4f16-bddc-a3f09c767f3e">by <strong>containing</strong> an owned include use case, i.e., the included use case is the include use case usage itself.<ul><li>The element symbol is displayed with the <em>include use case </em>keyword.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="include_use_case_symbol.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Include use case" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>See the procedures: <ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Including a use case via the owned include use case usage itself as the included use case"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Include use case" /></ac:link>:<ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Including use cases via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Include use case" /></ac:link> </li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Including use cases via the Create Element command"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Include use case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Including use cases via the view palette"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Include use case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Including use cases via the symbol compartment in a view"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Including use cases via the symbol compartment in a view ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Include use case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Including use cases via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Include use case" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Including a use case by referencing a use case usage defined elsewhere in the model</h3><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6cf8c39f-aa8d-4479-abac-9f6449d6372c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Including use cases via the Textual Editor1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Including use cases via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To include use cases via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to include a use case, do one of the following:<ol><li class="auto-cursor-target"><span>For an unnamed included use case, declare the </span><em>include </em><span>keyword followed by the name of the included use case. </span></li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><span>For a named one, </span><span>declare the </span><em>include use case </em><span>keyword, specify the element name, then <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Reference subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reference subset]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the included use case.</span></li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5974dddc-3ad2-4665-9c7b-1234849130f4"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">use</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">case <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drive vehicle';   </span></span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">//</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> referenced use case</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">use</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">case</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'provide transportation'</span> {</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed included use case referencing a use case defined elsewhere in the model</span><br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">include</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drive vehicle'</span> {}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">included use case</span> referencing a use case defined elsewhere in the model</span><br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">include</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">use</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">case</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enter</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">references</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drive vehicle'</span> {}<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">include</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">use</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">case</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enter</span> ::&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drive vehicle'</span> {}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Including use cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p>To include use cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag a use case element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Drag and Drop handler" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Drag and Drop handler]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and drop it on:<ul><li>the target element symbol that you want to include the use case <span>by referencing it </span>using the<span> <strong>Create Include Use Case</strong> </span>command.<span> </span></li><li>the include use case usage symbol that you want to include the use case <span>by referencing it </span>using the<span> <strong>Set Use Case</strong> <strong>Included </strong></span>command.<span> </span></li></ul></li></ul><h4><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e67949d-2e83-48b0-973b-2267523138c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Including use cases via the Specification panel1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Including use cases via the Specification panel</span></h4><p>To include use cases via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag and drop a use case element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler and drop it on the<span> <strong>Included Use Case</strong></span><strong><span> </span></strong>field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the include use case element. <span><br /></span>The appropriate use case is specified for the element.</li></ul><h3>Including a use case <span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">via the owned </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">include use case </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">usage </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">itself </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">as the </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">included use case</span></h3><h4>Including use cases via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To include use cases via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to include a use case, declare the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">include use case </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">keyword followed by its name.</span></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96b24203-8e6b-4f91-9f5f-db995b0c852a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">use</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">case</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'provide transportation'</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">included use case</span></span> is the defined include use case usage itself contained by the parent</span></span><br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">include</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">use</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">case</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drive vehicle'</span> {}<br />}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Including use cases via the Create Element command</h4><p>To include use cases via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select the <strong>include use case </strong>element.</li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Specify the included use case's features.</li></ol><h4>Including use cases via the view palette</h4><p>To include use cases via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Cases group, click the <strong>include use case </strong><span>button.</span></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the include use case symbol.</li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Specify the included use case's features. </li></ol><h4>Including use cases via the symbol compartment in a view </h4><p>To include use cases via the symbol compartment in a view </p><hr /><ul><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select a use case symbol and click the<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span> </span>button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>include use case </strong></span>button. The element is created and displayed in the <strong>includes</strong> compartment.</li><li>Select a use case symbol and on the right side of the <strong>includes</strong> compartment, click the<span> </span><strong>Create <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">include use case</span><span> </span></strong>button. The element is created and displayed in the <strong>includes</strong> compartment.</li></ul></li></ul><h4>Including use cases via the Specification panel</h4><p>To include use cases via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the use case element.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Include use case" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears. </span>Click the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">include use case </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">button in the shortcut menu. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>includes</strong> compartment section's bar, click the <strong>Create include use case</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Include use case" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The element is created in the <strong>includes</strong> compartment.</span></li></ol></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298092843 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00086: (2026x) Installation and system requirements

- page_id: `298092843`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298092843/2026x+Installation+and+system+requirements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:25:17.455+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

For information regarding installation and system requirements, visit the [Installation, licensing, and system requirements](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=installation-licensing-and-system-requirements&version=2026x) page.

To use SysML v2 features in the modeling tool, the SysML v1 Plugin and Cameo Requirements Modeler must be installed in addition to the SysML v2 Plugin and the SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Prerequisites' space='UAF2P'] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>For information regarding installation and system requirements, visit the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=installation-licensing-and-system-requirements&amp;version=2026x">Installation, licensing, and system requirements</a> page.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c300796-e00f-4b4b-b6e1-a434daa52ba5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To use SysML v2 features in the modeling tool, the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">SysML v1 Plugin and Cameo Requirements Modeler</span> must be installed in addition to the SysML v2 Plugin and the SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Prerequisites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Prerequisites]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093717 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00087: (2026x) Interface

- page_id: `298093717`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093717/2026x+Interface
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:55:05.027+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An interface is a connection whose ends are ports. As such, an interface facilitates the specification and reuse of compatible connections between parts. An interface definition is a kind of connection definition whose ends are restricted to be port definitions, while an interface usage is a kind of connection that is a usage of an interface definition with ends restricted to be port usages. As such, an interface usage connects compatible ports.

- The ends of an interface definition or usage must be port usages.
- The port keyword is optional for interface ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Cross features' space='UAF2P'] .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The image displays the *'fuel interface'* interface connector where *supplierPort*reference subsets *tankAssy.fuelTankPort*connecting to *consumerPort*which reference subsets *eng.engineFuelPort*. 
Additionally, the connector is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P'].

##### Creating interface usage and definition elements

###### Creating interface usage elements via the Textual Editor

To create interface usage elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the interface keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional), then the connect keyword, then state the feature ports explicitly (using the connect...to syntax ) or in a comma-separated list of related features. If the interface has no name and no specialization, the keyword *connect*can be omitted.You can also name interface end ports, associating them to feature ports via[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space='UAF2P'].
3. Click the Synchronize button.

interfacedefFuelInterface{ 
end portsupplierPort:FuelOutPort; 
end portconsumerPort:FuelInPort; 
}

partvehicle{ 
parttankAssy{portfuelTankPort;} 
parteng{portengineFuelPort;}

// unnamed unspecialized interface usage connecting feature ports with explicit declaration:interfacetankAssy.fuelTankPorttoeng.engineFuelPort;//unnamed unspecialized interface usage connecting featureports in a comma-separated list:interface(tankAssy.fuelTankPort,eng.engineFuelPort); //unnamed unspecialized interface usage with named end portsconnecting featureports in a comma-separated list:interface(sPort ::>tankAssy.fuelTankPort, cPort ::>eng.engineFuelPort);

// unnamed specialized (feature typing) interface usage with named end portsconnectingfeatureportswith explicit declaration:interface : FuelInterface connect sPort ::> tankAssy.fuelTankPort to cPort ::> eng.engineFuelPort;

// named unspecializedinterface usage connecting featureports with explicit declarations: 
interface fuelInterface connecttankAssy.fuelTankPort to eng.engineFuelPort;

// named specialized (feature typing) interface usage with named end portsconnectingfeatureportswith explicit declaration:interface 'fuel interface' : FuelInterface connect sPort ::> tankAssy.fuelTankPort to cPort ::> eng.engineFuelPort;// named specialized (feature typing) interface usage with named end portsconnecting featureportsin a comma-separated list:interface'fuel interface':FuelInterfaceconnect(sPort::>tankAssy.fuelTankPort,cPort::>eng.engineFuelPort);}

###### Creating interface usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To create interface usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the interface command.
2. Click on the element you want to connect via an interface. An interface connector is created between the elements with ports on both ends.

###### Creating interface usage elements via the Create Relationship command

To create interface usage elements via the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click one of the elements you want to connect.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click Outgoing , and then in the submenu, click interface . The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK .

###### Creating interface usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create interface usage and definition elements via the view palette

- To create an interface definition:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **interface** def button to create an interface definition element.
  2. Click in the view pane where you want the interface definition symbol to be created.
- To create an interface usage:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **interface** button to create an interface usage.
  2. In a view, click on the first of the two element symbols you want to connect, then on the second. An interface connector is created between the elements with ports on both ends. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **interface, n-ary interface**or the **interface****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.

###### Creating interface definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create interface definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword interface def .
2. Name the element, then specify the interface definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the interface definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] of the needed port.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

interfacedefFuelInterface{ // interface definition 
end portsupplierPort:FuelOutPort; 
end portconsumerPort:FuelInPort; 
}

###### Creating interface definition elements via the Create Element command

To create interface definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element submenu, click interface def for interface definition.

###### Auto-creating interface definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create an interface definition element via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='UAF2P']page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

As such, an interface usage connects compatible ports.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5787418a-803f-4626-9318-7948affdd4fa"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="7e8c53a2-dbb1-41b6-a09f-9612a0b3dd32">The ends of an interface definition or usage must be port usages.</li><li data-uuid="dbfc9b96-fa5e-48ac-a81d-0d304f71faee">The <em>port</em> keyword is optional for interface ends. can also name interface end ports, <span>associating them to feature ports via </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Reference subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reference subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c78fedff-e12b-499d-9543-df7d2552df18"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">interface</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelInterface</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {     </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">supplierPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelOutPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">consumerPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelInPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engineFuelPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> </span></span></p><p><span unspecialized interface usage connecting feature ports with explicit declaration:<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span>interface</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">tankAssy</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">eng</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">interface</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> (</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">tankAssy</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">unnamed unspecialized interface usage <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">feature </span></span>ports in a comma-separated list:<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">interface</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> (<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sPort</span> ::&gt; </span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">tankAssy</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTankPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cPort</span> ::&gt; </span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">eng</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engineFuelPort)</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;    </span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span>// unnamed specialized (feature typing) interface usage <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">with named end ports </span></span>connecting<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">feature </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">ports<span> </span></span></span>with explicit declaration:<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">interface</span> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelInterface</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connect</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sPort</span> ::&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">tankAssy</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTankPort</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cPort</span> ::&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">eng</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engineFuelPort</span>;</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span></span>// named <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">unspecialized </span></span>interface usage connecting <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">feature </span></span>ports <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">with explicit declarations:</span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span></span>interface</span> fuelInterface <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connect</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">tankAssy</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTankPort</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">eng</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engineFuelPort</span>;          </span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named specialized (feature typing) interface usage <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">with named end ports </span></span>connecting<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">feature </span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">ports<span> </span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">with explicit declaration:<br /></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">interface</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'fuel interface'</span> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelInterface</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connect</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sPort</span> ::&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">tankAssy</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTankPort</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cPort</span> ::&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">eng</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engineFuelPort</span>;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">    </span></span>// named specialized (feature typing) <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">interface</span></span> usage <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">with named end ports </span></span>connecting <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">feature </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">ports</span></span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">in a comma-separated list:</span>   <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">interface</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'fuel interface'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelInterface</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connect</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> (</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">tankAssy</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTankPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">eng</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engineFuelPort)</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating interface usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</h4><p>To create interface usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>interface </strong>command.</li><li>Click on the element you want to connect via an interface. An interface connector is created between the elements with ports on both ends.</li></ol><h4>Creating interface usage elements <span>via the Create Relationship command</span></h4><p>To create interface usage elements via the Create Relationship command</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click one of the elements you want to connect.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Relationship</strong>, then in the submenu, click <strong>Outgoing</strong>, and then in the submenu, click <strong>interface</strong>. The <strong>Element Picker </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Element Picker </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements. </li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Interface" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Element Picker</strong>.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong>Element Picker</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating interface usage and definition elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create interface usage and definition elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ul><li>To create an interface definition:<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>interface </strong></span><strong>def </strong><span>button to create an interface definition element.</span></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want the interface definition symbol to be created.</li></ol></li><li>To create an interface usage:<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>interface </strong></span><span>button to create an interface usage.</span></li><li>In a view, click on the first of the two element symbols you want to connect, then on the second. An interface connector is created between the elements with ports on both ends.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769c3a3a-7b27-471d-b151-165748c95ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the <span><strong>interface, n-ary interface </strong></span>or the <span><strong>interface </strong></span><strong>def </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ul><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating interface definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create interface definition elements via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">interface def</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>Name the element, then specify the interface definition features in the element body:<ol><li>Specify the interface definition ends by declaring the <em>end</em> keyword, followed by the end name and/or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ specialization ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>of the needed port.</li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30044d15-f229-4626-9e04-2b2739874b7d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">interface</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelInterface</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {                <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// interface definition</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">supplierPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelOutPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">consumerPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelInPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating interface definition elements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create interface definition elements via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the Create Element submenu, click <strong>interface def</strong> for interface definition.</li></ol><h4>Auto-creating interface definition elements via the Extract Definition command</h4><p><span>To auto-create an interface definition element via the Extract Definition command</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extract Definition and Usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093735 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00088: (2026x) Item

- page_id: `298093735`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An item definition defines identifiable objects that may be acted on over time, such as an input, output, or a stored item, but which do not necessarily perform actions themselves (the latter would be a part element). An item usage is a usage of one or more item definitions. Item usages may be:

- Representations of inputs and outputs (e.g., water, fuel, electrical signals, data).
- Flowing through and/or stored by a system.
- Referencial, referencing discrete items that interact with or pass through the system.

##### Creating item usage and definition elements

###### Creating item usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create item usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *item def*for an item definition.
  2. *item* for an item usage.
2. Name the element.
3. (Optional) Specify the item's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

partdefFuelTank{ 
itemdefFuel;// item definition 
} 
itemdefPerson;// item definition

partdefVehicle{ 
refitemdriver:Person;// referential item 
partfuelTank:FuelTank{ 
itemfuel:Fuel;// continuous item usage stored by a system 
} 
}

###### Creating item usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create item usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. item def to create an item definition element.
  2. item to create an item usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the item's features.

###### Creating item usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create item usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click one of the following:
  1. The **item** def button to create an item definition element.
  2. The **item** button to create an item usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **item**or the **item def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the item symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the item's features.

###### Creating item usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] page.

To create item usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select the element symbol for which you want to create an item and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click item .
  - Select the element symbol for which you want to create an item and on the right side of the items compartment, click the Create item button. The item is created and displayed in the items compartment.

###### Creating item usage elements via the Specification panel

To create item usage elements via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] of the element for which you want to create an item.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **item** element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **items**compartment's section bar, click the **Create item**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The item is created in the **items** compartment.

###### Auto-creating item definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create item definition elements via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='UAF2P']page.

## PAGE 00089: (2026x) Key concepts

- page_id: `298093373`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093373/2026x+Key+concepts

- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page provides descriptions of basic concepts related to the SysML v2 elements that are relevant to know before you begin modeling.

For a comprehensive list and examples of SysML v2 elements, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model composition' space='UAF2P'] section.

The general structure of the model is comprised of the global namespace, which encloses one or more namespaces that contain model elements optionally organized into packages.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Model organization.

##### Key concepts

Below is a list of some of the SysML v2 elements key concepts, along with expanded descriptions following.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P']
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P']
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P']
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P']
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P']
    - Definition specialization : [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P']
    - Usage specializations: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P']

##### Namespace

A namespace is a kind of element that can contain other elements and assign names to them.The contained elements are referred to as member elements, whichare related to a namespace via a membership relationship. It can specify both the name by which the member element is known within the containing namespace and whether the visibility of the element's membership extends beyond the namespace.

**Kinds of namespaces:**

- Global namespace
- Root namespaces
- Packages
- All definition and usage elements

###### Global namespace

A global namespace acts as a container for all root namespaces within a single project, including all SysML model libraries. There is no syntax for the global namespace, thus it is not represented in graphical or textual notation. In the modeling tool, the global namespace is represented by the top-level <project_name> package. Elements within any root namespace can refer to top-level elements of any other root namespace without qualification, as root namespaces themselves are not named. The global namespace essentially encapsulates all root namespaces and their elements, providing a mechanism for cross-reference among them.

###### Root namespace

A root namespace is defined as a namespace without an owner. It contains top-level elements, and every element that is not a root namespace must belong to an ownership tree of a top-level element in some root namespace. The declaration of a root namespace is implicit within SysML, and its content is represented by its top-level elements. Root namespaces are sorted by creation date.The root namespace is automatically named after the initial package created for it and cannot be renamed.For a procedure on root namespace creation, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing elements' space='UAF2P'] page.

###### Package

A package is a specific type of namespace designed for organizing and grouping elements within a model. It serves as a container that can include both structural and behavioral elements, facilitating modular design and reuse. Packages can be used to structure the model in a hierarchical manner, enabling better organization and management of model elements. This approach allows the logical grouping of related elements, improving the clarity and maintainability of the system model.In the modeling tool, a package element can only be created for aroot namespace within the Containment tree or via Textual Editor. For procedures on package management, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Package' space='UAF2P'] page.

##### Elements and relationships

The following concepts apply to all model elements.

###### Elements

- Every model element is assigned an element ID, which remains unchanged from the element's creation until its deletion, even if the element properties change.
- Every model element may have one or more alias IDs relative to one or more namespaces.

###### Relationships

- A relationship is a kind of element that relates two or more model elements.
- Relationships can be:
  - Binary - relating exactly two elements.
  - Non-binary - relating more than two elements.
  - Directed - a relationship that is directed from source to target.
  - Undirected - a relationship within which all elements are targets without any source elements.
- A relationship may also serve as the source or target of other relationships.
- There are several kinds of relationships:
  - Specializations : [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subclassification' space='UAF2P'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Feature typing' space='UAF2P'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subsetting' space='UAF2P'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space='UAF2P'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Redefinition' space='UAF2P']
  - Connectors : [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Connection' space='UAF2P'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Interface' space='UAF2P'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Flow' space='UAF2P'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Succession' space='UAF2P'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Succession flow' space='UAF2P'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Binding' space='UAF2P'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Allocation' space='UAF2P'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Derive requirement' space='UAF2P']
  - Other relationships : [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Annotations' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Dependency' space='UAF2P'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Feature membership' space='UAF2P']

##### Definition and Usage

In SysML v2, definition and usage are the primary constructs facilitating reusability for different kinds of elements (requirements, use cases, interfaces, ports, attributes, etc.).

###### Definition

A Definition element classifies the element kind (requirement, use case, etc.).In the modeling tool's graphical notation, definitions are always represented as rectangles. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the modeling tool's Textual Editor, a definition is represented by the keyword def.

part defVehicle;

###### Usage

A Usage element is a usage of a definition element within a specific context.A usage must be defined/typed by at least one definition that corresponds in kind (requirement usage defined/typed by requirement definition).If the usage is undefined by the user, it is implicitly defined by the appropriate kind defined in the Systems Library.In the modeling tool's graphical notation, usages are always represented as rectangles with rounded corners. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the modeling tool's Textual Editor, usage has no keyword. It is represented simply by typing the element kind (e.g., requirement, use case, etc.).

partengine:Vehicle;

###### Features

A definition can own usage elements, which are referred to as its features.A usage can also own other usage elements as features.

###### Graphical compartment and body

An element may contain other elements, which can be represented in the:

- Graphical compartments (graphical notation). The graphical compartment(s) is displayed in the owning element's symbol. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Body (textual notation). The body is bound by curly braces, e.g., {...}. requirement defMassLimitReq{ 
doc/* The actual mass shall be...*/ 
}

##### Specialization relationships

SysML v2 provides specialization relationships used to specialize definition and usage elements. Specialization lets modelers build specific elements by inheriting features from more general ones. They can then tailor or adjust these features to fit different situations.Below is a list of various types of specialization relationships:

**SubclassificationSubclassification**: used to specialize a definition element. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subclassification' space='UAF2P'] page to learn more.

**Feature TypingFeature typing**: specifies a definition for a usage. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Feature typing' space='UAF2P'] page to learn more.

**SubsettingSubsetting**: used to specialize a usage element. Inherited features can be subsetted by multiple features. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subsetting' space='UAF2P'] page to learn more.

**Reference SubsettingReference subsetting**: is a kind of subsetting relationship. While a subsetting usage can have multiple subsetted usages, a reference subsetting usage can only have one specific subsetted usage. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space='UAF2P'] page to learn more.

**RedefinitionRedefinition**: is a kind of subsetting used to replace an inherited feature by redefining. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Redefinition' space='UAF2P'] page to learn more.
