/></ac:link><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Definition"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Key concepts" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Key concepts" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Features"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Key concepts" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <ac:link ac:anchor="Graphical compartment and body"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Key concepts" /></ac:link> </span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <ac:link ac:anchor="Specialization relationships"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Key concepts" /></ac:link> </span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Definition specialization</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <ac:link ac:anchor="Subclassification"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subclassification]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Key concepts" /></ac:link> </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Usage specializations: <ac:link ac:anchor="Feature Typing"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[feature typing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Key concepts" /></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, <ac:link ac:anchor="Subsetting"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Key concepts" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Reference Subsetting"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reference subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Key concepts" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Redefinition"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[redefinition]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Key concepts" /></ac:link> </span></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><h3>Namespace</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">A namespace is a kind of element that can contain other elements and assign names to them. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The contained elements are referred to as </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">member elements, which</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> are related to a namespace via a membership relationship. It </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">can specify both the name by which the member element is known within the containing namespace and whether the visibility of the element's membership extends beyond the namespace. </span></p><p><strong> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Kinds of namespaces:</span> </strong></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Global namespace </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Root namespaces</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Packages</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">All definition and usage elements</span></li></ul><h4>Global namespace</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">A global namespace acts as a container for all root namespaces within a single project, including all SysML model libraries. There is no syntax for the global namespace, thus it is not represented in graphical or textual notation. In the modeling tool, the global namespace is represented by the top-level &lt;project_name&gt; package. Elements within any root namespace can refer to top-level elements of any other root namespace without qualification, as root namespaces themselves are not named. The global namespace essentially encapsulates all root namespaces and their elements, providing a mechanism for cross-reference among them.</span></p><h4>Root namespace</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">A root namespace is defined as a namespace without an owner. It contains top-level elements, and every element that is not a root namespace must belong to an ownership tree of a top-level element in some root namespace. The declaration of a root namespace is implicit within SysML, and its content is represented by its top-level elements. Root namespaces are sorted by creation date. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The root namespace is automatically named after the initial package created for it and cannot be renamed. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For a procedure on root namespace creation, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></p><h4>Package</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">A package is a specific type of namespace designed for organizing and grouping elements within a model. It serves as a container that can include both structural and behavioral elements, facilitating modular design and reuse. Packages can be used to structure the model in a hierarchical manner, enabling better organization and management of model elements. This approach allows the logical grouping of related elements, improving the clarity and maintainability of the system model. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the modeling tool, a package element can only be created for a </span>root namespace within the Containment tree or via Textual Editor. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For procedures on package management, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></p><h3>Elements and relationships</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The following concepts apply to all model elements.</span></p><h4>Elements</h4><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Every model element is assigned an element ID, which remains unchanged from the element's creation until its deletion, even if the element properties change.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Every model element may have one or more alias IDs relative to one or more namespaces.</span></li></ul><h4>Relationships</h4><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">A relationship is a kind of element that relates two or more model elements.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Relationships can be:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Binary - relating exactly two elements.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Non-binary - relating more than two elements.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Directed - a relationship that is directed from source to target.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Undirected - a relationship within which all elements are targets without any source elements.<br /></span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">A relationship may also serve as the source or target of other relationships.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">There are several kinds of relationships:</span><ul><li><strong>Specializations</strong>: <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Subclassification" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Subclassification]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Feature typing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Feature typing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Reference subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Reference subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefinition]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><strong>Connectors</strong>: <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Connection" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connection]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interface]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Flow" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Flow]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Succession" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Succession]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Succession flow" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Succession flow]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Binding" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Binding]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Allocation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Allocation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Derive requirement" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Derivation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><strong>Other relationships</strong>: <span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Annotations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Annotation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Dependency" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Dependency]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Feature membership" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Feature membership]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul><h3>Definition and Usage</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In SysML v2, definition and usage are the primary constructs facilitating reusability for different kinds of elements (requirements, use cases, interfaces, ports, attributes, etc.).</span></p><h4>Definition</h4><p>A Definition element classifies the element kind <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">(requirement, use case, etc.).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the modeling tool's graphical notation, definitions are always represented as rectangles. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="definition_element.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Key concepts" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the modeling tool's Textual Editor, a definition is represented by the keyword def.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="259e3374-e6b8-4c2a-97a4-b6085775943e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>part def</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle;</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Usage</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">A Usage element is a usage of a definition element within a specific context. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">A usage must be defined/typed by at least one definition that corresponds in kind (requirement usage defined/typed by requirement definition). </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If the usage is undefined by the user, it is implicitly defined by the appropriate kind defined in the Systems Library. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the modeling tool's graphical notation, usages are always represented as rectangles with rounded corners. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="usage_element.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Key concepts" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the modeling tool's Textual Editor, usage has no keyword. It is represented simply by typing the element kind (e.g., requirement, use case, etc.).<br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5be767d-5c27-476d-8c20-b4899171ebf9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>part</span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle;</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Features</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">A definition can own usage elements, which are referred to as its features. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">A usage can also own other usage elements as features. </span></p><h4>Graphical compartment and body</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">An element may contain other elements, which can be represented in the:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Graphical compartments (graphical notation). The graphical compartment(s) is displayed in the owning element's symbol.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Graphical compartment and body.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Key concepts" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Body (textual notation). The body is bound by curly braces, e.g., {...}.<br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f31d5e3-2dd4-43b6-a7e6-dafaa6598d57"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>requirement def</span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MassLimitReq</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{</span><br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">doc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* The actual mass shall be...*/</span><br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><h3>Specialization relationships</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">SysML v2 provides specialization relationships used to specialize definition and usage elements. Specialization lets modelers build specific elements by inheriting features from more general ones. They can then tailor or adjust these features to fit different situations.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> Below is a list of various types of specialization relationships:</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8dac97c5-5839-44e5-aa16-ea6f75e8bda4"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Subclassification</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Subclassification</strong>: used to specialize a definition element. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Subclassification" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Subclassification]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page to learn more.</span></p><p><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3aee5317-26b7-463e-bbf6-32b882f35933"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Feature Typing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Feature typing</span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: specifies a definition for a usage. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Feature typing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Feature typing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page to learn more.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac6a9bce-807a-40aa-a997-11a334c8dff3"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Subsetting</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Subsetting</strong>: used to specialize a usage element. Inherited features can be subsetted by multiple features. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page to learn more.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a6108bf-0e4f-46f0-8abc-8d6e11a48b5d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Reference Subsetting</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Reference subsetting</strong>: is a kind of subsetting relationship. While a subsetting usage can have multiple subsetted usages, a reference subsetting usage can only have one specific subsetted usage. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Reference subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Reference subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page to learn more.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5a3ac4a-7ff2-47e4-a2f5-d011de35c866"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Redefinition</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Redefinition</strong>: is a kind of subsetting used to replace an inherited feature by redefining. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefinition]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page to learn more.</span></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298092857 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00090: (2026x) Libraries & samples

- page_id: `298092857`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298092857/2026x+Libraries+samples
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-08T14:30:57.377+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Modeling tool environment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Libraries

Each SysML v2 project is automatically loaded with used projects:

- Standard Libraries. It contains standard SysML v2 libraries.
- *3DS SysML Customization*. It contains concepts designed for view creation and symbol styles customization.

##### Samples

You can review the SysML v2 project samples provided with the installation, found at:

- <modeling tool installation directory>\samples

##### Opening sample projects

You can use existing sample projects to explore SysML v2 model examples.

To open a sample project

1. In the Main toolbar, select Help , in the dropdown menu, click Show Samples . A new tab opens displaying all available sample projects.
2. Click an appropriate sample project under the **SysML v2** section. Use the shortcut keys Ctrl+F to open the **Find** dialog. Enter the desired keyword to locate specific samples. The sample project is opened.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Libraries</h3><p>Each SysML v2 project is automatically loaded with used projects:</p><ul><li data-uuid="e6ee1f45-dab2-490f-b941-40d682da7168"><em>Standard Libraries.</em> It contains standard SysML v2 libraries.</li><li data-uuid="f3158e11-5154-4233-a7dd-bd1b1b6c75d8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>3DS SysML Customization</em>. It contains concepts designed for view creation and symbol styles customization.</span></li></ul><h3>Samples</h3><p>You can review the SysML v2 project samples provided with the installation, found at:</p><ul><li><em style="text-align: left;">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples</em><em style="text-align: left;"><br /></em></li></ul><h3><span>Opening sample projects</span></h3><p>You can use existing sample projects to explore SysML v2 model examples.</p><p>To open a sample project</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1351e0b8-914d-4dcb-879a-5d84299dbcfa">In the Main toolbar, select <strong>Help</strong>, in the dropdown menu, click <strong>Show Samples</strong>. A new tab opens displaying all available sample projects.</li><li data-uuid="f27f1183-ff4b-421e-80fc-177e78e1efc9"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click an appropriate sample project under the <strong>SysML v2</strong> section.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fb73d0b3-6652-4614-8ccc-62616a1112e4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use the shortcut keys Ctrl+F to open the <strong>Find</strong> dialog. Enter the desired keyword to locate specific samples.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">The sample project is opened.</p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298092868 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00091: (2026x) Main menu

- page_id: `298092868`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298092868/2026x+Main+menu
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T14:26:20.609+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Modeling tool environment > (2026x) User interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The main menu located at the top of the modeling tool window contains the following options:

Currently, multiple commands displayed in the dropdown menus of the main menu options that are listed below are disabled.

| Menu option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| File | Contains basic project management-related commands, e.g., Local Repository, Open Project, Save Project, etc. |
| Edit | Use this menu when working with views in the view pane. The Edit menu contains the commands for selecting, cutting, copying, and pasting symbols. |
| View | Contains commands allowing you to zoom in or zoom out of a view and of your modeling tool. |
| Layout | Use this menu for managing the layout of the symbols on an active view. |
| Options | Use this menu to specify project options and environment options. |
| Tools | Use this menu to access the Patch tool. |
| Analyze | At the moment, the commands provided in this menu are not available in SysML v2. |
| Collaborate | Contains the commands for working with server projects. |
| Window | Allows you to manage the layout and display of various windows in your modeling tool. |
| Help | Among other commands, this menu allows you to open online documentation, manage resources, and access modeling tool-related information. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The main menu located at the top of the modeling tool window contains the following options:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1975375f-47f8-431d-9d2e-40c18bd5e69d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Currently, multiple commands displayed in the dropdown menus of the main menu options that are listed below are disabled.   </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 12.7287%;" /><col style="width: 87.2713%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Menu option</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>File</strong></td><td>Contains basic project management-related commands, e.g., Local Repository, Open Project, Save Project, etc.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Edit</strong></td><td>Use this menu when working with views in the view pane. The <strong>Edit</strong> menu contains the commands for selecting, cutting, copying, and pasting symbols.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>View</strong></td><td>Contains commands allowing you to zoom in or zoom out of a <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">view</span> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Toolbars" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[customize the toolbars]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of your modeling tool.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Layout</strong></td><td>Use this menu for managing the layout of the symbols on an active view.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Options</strong></td><td>Use this menu to specify project options and environment options.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Tools</strong></td><td><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="92ec7cab-860a-4b55-a4af-9668ef5efe6f">Use this menu to access the Patch tool. </ac:inline-comment-marker></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Analyze</strong></td><td><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e15ba95c-43fb-478c-bd3b-fd75caed7fe8">At the moment, the commands provided in this menu are not available in SysML v2. </ac:inline-comment-marker></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Collaborate</strong></td><td><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9b26ee28-9a3e-4e91-a1e7-f31e97fa0f4e">Contains</ac:inline-comment-marker> the commands for working with server projects.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Window</strong></td><td>Allows you to manage the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cae614e9-a028-4893-bc5a-c8551f3630c9">layout</ac:inline-comment-marker> and display of various windows in your modeling tool.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Help</strong></td><td>Among other commands, this menu allows you to open online documentation, manage resources, and access modeling tool-related information.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093386 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00092: (2026x) Managing elements

- page_id: `298093386`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093386/2026x+Managing+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:39:01.983+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page outlines the basic common procedures regarding element management.

#### NOTE: Useful information for element management

Useful information for element management

- For more information on working with the Textual Editor and textual notation, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Textual Editor' space='UAF2P'] section.
- For a comprehensive list of SysML v2 elements, their textual notation keywords, and relevant procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model composition' space='UAF2P'] section.

##### Declaring a root namespace

A root namespace contains the model elements. It does not have textual representation. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P'] page.

To declare a root namespace

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the global namespace (by default, the global namespace name is the same as the project name).
2. In the shortcut menu, select Create Namespace .

##### Creating an element

The procedures below describe the basic options for creating the majority of the model elements. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space='UAF2P'] page.

###### Creating an element via the Textual Editor

To create an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, type the keyword for the element you want to create followed by its name. partflashlight;
2. Click the Synchronize button.

###### Creating an element via the Create Element command

To create an element via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**. The **Create Element**dialog is displayed.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select the element you want to create.
3. Type the name for the newly created element.

###### Creating an element via the view palette

To create an element via the view palette

1. In the View Palette, click on the element you want to create.
2. Hover the cursor over the V iew Pane and click on the location where you want the new element to be created.
3. Type the name for the newly created element.

###### Creating an element via the smart manipulator in a view

Creating an element via the smart manipulator is only available for a limited selection of elements (e.g., ports, connectors).

To create an element via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view , select a symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the command for the needed element.

###### Creating an element via the symbol compartment in a view

To create an element via the symbol compartment in a view

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] page.

###### Creating an element via the Specification panel

To create an element via the Specification panel

- Do one of the following:
  - See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] page.
  - See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] page.

###### Creating an element via the Drag and Drop handler In a view

To create an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- See the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P']page.

###### Auto-creating a definition element via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create a definition element via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='UAF2P']page.

###### Creating a relationship element via the Create Relationship command

To create a relationship element via the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click element(s) or element symbol(s) for which you want to create relationship(s).
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click either Outgoing or Incoming , and then in the submenu, click the relationship you want to create. The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view , click the element symbol. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element(s) and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element(s) from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK . The specified relationship is created between the selected elements.

##### Copying, pasting, and cutting an element

Elements cannot be copied and pasted between separate SysML v2 projects.

###### Copying an element via the Textual Editor

To copy an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, select the element you want to copy and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the selection and in the shortcut menu click the Copy command.
  2. Press Ctrl+C on the keyboard.

###### Copying an element via the Containment tree

To copy an element via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, select an element you want to copy and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the element and in the shortcut menu click the Copy command.
  2. In the main menu, click Edit > Copy .
  3. Press Ctrl+C on the keyboard.
  4. Press Ctrl , then drag and drop the selected element to the element where you want the selected one to be copied and pasted.

###### Copying an element symbol in a view

To copy an element symbol in a view

1. In a view , select an element symbol you want to copy and do one of the following:
  1. In the view toolbar, click the Copy [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
  2. In the main menu, click Edit > Copy .
  3. Press Ctrl+C on the keyboard.

###### Pasting an element via the Textual Editor

To paste an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to paste the copied notation and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click and in the shortcut menu click the Paste command.
  2. Press Ctrl+V on the keyboard.

###### Pasting an element via the Containment tree

To paste an element via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, select an element where you want to paste the copied element and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the element and in the shortcut menu click the Paste command.
  2. In the main menu, click Edit > Paste .
  3. Press Ctrl+V on the keyboard.

###### Pasting an element or its symbol via a view

To paste an element or its symbol via a view

1. In a view , select an element symbol for which you want to paste the copied element symbol and do one of the following:
  1. In the view toolbar, click the Paste [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to paste the copied symbol without creating a new element.
  2. In the main menu, click one of the following:
    1. Click Edit > Paste to paste the copied symbol without creating a new element.
    2. Click Edit > Paste with New Element to paste the copied symbol and create a new element for it.
  3. Press one of the following shortcut keys:
    1. Press Ctrl+V to to paste the copied symbol without creating a new element.
    2. Press Ctrl+E to paste the copied symbol and create a new element for it.

###### Cutting an element via the Textual Editor

To cut an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, select the element you want to cut and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the selection and in the shortcut menu click the Cut command.
  2. Press Ctrl+X on the keyboard.

###### Cutting an element via the Containment tree

To cut an element via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, select an element you want to cut and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the element and in the shortcut menu click the Cut command.
  2. In the main menu, click Edit > Cut .
  3. Press Ctrl+X on the keyboard.

##### Deleting an element

###### Deleting an element via the Textual Editor

To delete an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, select the element keyword and its notational text. partflashlight;
2. On the keyboard, press either one of the following keys:
  1. Delete key.
  2. Backspace key.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

###### Deleting an element via the Containment tree

To delete an element via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, select an element you want to delete and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the element and in the shortcut menu click the Delete command.
  2. In the main menu, click Edit > Delete .
  3. Press Delete key or Ctrl+D on the keyboard.

###### Deleting an element or its symbol via a view

To delete an element or its symbol via a view

1. In a view , select an element symbol you want to delete and do one of the following:
  1. In the view toolbar, click one of the following:
    1. Delete button to delete the element from the model.
    2. Remove from Diagram button to remove the element symbol from the view .
  2. In the main menu, click one of the following:
    1. Click Edit > Delete to delete the element from the model.
    2. Click Edit > Remove from Diagram to remove the element symbol from the view .
  3. Press one of the following shortcut keys:
    1. Press Ctrl+D to delete the element from the model.
    2. Press Delete to remove the element symbol from the view .

###### Deleting an element via the compartment in the Specification panel

To delete an element via the compartment in the Specification panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

element for it.</li></ol></li></ol></li></ol><h4>Cutting an element via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To cut an element via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr </strong>command.</li><li>Press <strong>Ctrl+X</strong> on the keyboard.</li></ol></li></ol><h4>Cutting an element via the Containment tree</h4><p>To cut an element via the Containment tree</p><hr </strong>command.</li><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Edit </strong>&gt; <strong>Cut</strong>.</li><li>Press <strong>Ctrl+X</strong> on the keyboard.</li></ol></li></ol><h3>Deleting an element</h3><h4>Deleting an element via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To delete an element via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298092904 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00093: (2026x) Managing local projects

- page_id: `298092904`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298092904/2026x+Managing+local+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-19T08:29:33.306+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXPAND: Click here to expand the 'Managing local projects' documentation for the 2026x version...

Click here to expand the 'Managing local projects' documentation for the 2026x version...

Local projects are managed via the local repository instead of working with individual files. The projects are stored in the local repository within the modeling tool’s configuration files. The local repository serves as a central storage for all your projects, their dependencies, and standard libraries. This makes it easier to keep track of your models, review your work, and smoothly transition between local and server projects. It also provides a more robust format for exchanging models in air-gapped environments between servers.

When you open a project from the file system, the tool first imports it into the local repository. After the import completes, the tool opens the project copy from the local repository, not the file in your file system. The tool handles sample projects in the same way. As a result, every project you create, open, or import is stored in the local repository, allowing you to view and modify it.

The projects can be saved in an**.mdszip format**, where a single file contains both the project and its used projects. This file is intended only for importing projects into your local repository or server. Once imported, all ongoing work is saved directly in the repository. You only need to export to a file again if you want to share your project or create a backup.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### The Manage Projects dialog

The Manage Project dialog allows you to manage projects in the local repository. It displays all the projects in the local repository and allows you to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'], and[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] your projects.

- The Name column in the Manage Projects dialog displays projects and project categories.
- The Branches column displays the names of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] .
- The Last modified column displays the last project modification date and time.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Manage Projects dialog 2026xOpening the Manage Projects dialog

To open the Manage Projects dialog

- In the main menu, click File > Local Repository . The Manage Projects dialog opens.

##### Managing local projects

###### Creating projects in the local repository 2026xCreating projects in the local repository

Create projects in the local repository to create your models.

To create projects in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the dialog, click the New button. The New Project dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, specify the project name and category, then click OK .

###### Opening projects from the local repository 2026xOpening projects from the local repository

Open projects from the local repository to view and modify them in the tool.

To open projects from the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the dialog, do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed project and click the Open button.
  2. Double-click the needed project.

###### Importing projects to the local repository 2026xImporting projects to the local repository

- The project from the file system is first imported into the local repository, and then opened from there. Therefore, the project file you open and work on in the tool is the project copy in the local repository, not the file in your file system.
- Imported projects are displayed in the local repository using the following structure: <project_name> or <project_name<number>> .

To import projects to the local repository

Do one of the following:

- Open the **Manage Projects**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] . In the dialog, click the Import button. The Open dialog opens. Select the needed file from the file system and click OK .
- In the main menu, click File > Open Project . In the Open dialog, select the project and click Open .
- In the main toolbar, click the Open Project button.
- On the keyboard, press Ctrl+O .
- Drag the project from the open window of your file system to the modeling tool. The project opens.

###### Removing projects from the local repository 2026xRemoving projects from the local repository

Removing the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project, and vice versa. Learn more in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] subsection.

To remove projects from the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the dialog, select the needed project and click the Remove button. In the dropdown menu, select Delete Draft or Delete Project .
3. The question dialog opens. Click the Delete button to confirm the removal of the project.

###### Renaming projects in the local repository 2026xRenaming projects in the local repository

Renaming the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. Learn more in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] subsection.

To rename projects in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the dialog, select the needed project and click the Rename button. The Rename dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, update the project name and click OK .

##### Managing project imports in the local repository 2026xManaging project imports in the local repository

Each originally created or imported project in the local repository may have multiple subsequent imports of the same project. For example, you might [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] a project from the local repository, share it with a colleague who makes changes while you continue working on the original, and then later [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] your colleague’s modified copy back into your local repository. In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the original and the subsequent import of the same project. You can modify both projects independently, and although they are displayed as separate projects, they are treated as distinct but still interdependent instances of the same project.

- The original project is the first instance of the project that exists in the local repository, which can be created in or imported into the local repository.
  - If the original project was created in the local repository, it is displayed as trunk .
  - If the original project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed as <imported_file_name> .
- Subsequent imports of the same project are any later instances of the project that are imported into the local repository.
  - Subsequent imports of the same project originate only by importing a project into the local repository. They are displayed as: <imported_file_name> .
    - If there already is an import with the same name, a number is added: <imported_file_name<number>> .

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project, and vice versa.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.

You can view the project details in the **Manage Projects** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Saving local projects

There are three ways to save a local project:

- **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P']**. Recommended for ongoing work. Uses less disk space than saving as a new version in the local repository.
- **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P']**. Saves the project's new version in the local repository. This option is required if you want to export the project to a file or to create a reusable baseline for other projects. Note that it consumes more disk space than saving as a draft.
- **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P']**. Exports the project as an *.mdszip* file, which can be stored anywhere on your file system and shared with colleagues.

Use **Change Sets** to track project modifications. The latest changes are compared against the most recent project version saved in the local repository.

- The panel legend explains the modification markings.
- Click the Refresh icon to update the display.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Saving projects as drafts in the local repository 2026xSaving projects as drafts in the local repository

To save projects as drafts in the local repository

1. Do one of the following:
  1. In the main menu, click File > Save Project .
  2. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+S.
  3. In the main toolbar, click the Save Project button. Projects saved as drafts are displayed with the asterisk symbol on the project icon in the local repository. [ATTACHMENT filename='save_as_draft.png']

###### Saving projects as new versions in the local repository 2026xSaving projects as new versions in the local repository

To save projects as new versions in the local repository

1. Do one of the following:
  1. In the main menu, click File > Save As New Version .
  2. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+K. The question dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, click the Save As New Version button to confirm. The Save As New Version dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, specify a comment (optional) and click Save .

###### Exporting projects from the local repository as .mdszip files 2026xExporting projects from the local repository as .*mdszip* files

To export projects from the local repository as .*mdszip* files

1. In the main menu, click File > Save Project As . To export a project to a file, its new version must first be saved in the local repository. If it is not, clicking the **Save As Project** command opens the **Save As New Version** dialog. Click **Save** in the dialog to store the new version in the local repository and enable the project export to a file.
2. The Save dialog opens. Select the file location in the file system and click Save .

##### Managing local project categories 2026xManaging local project categories

Project categories in the local repository help you organize and structure your local projects, providing a more efficient project management experience. You can create, delete, rename, and move projects between them. Category management commands are available in the toolbar of the **Manage Projects** dialog and the shortcut menus of categories and projects.

The*Standard Libraries*category is pre-populated with the SysML v2 standard libraries required for modeling. It is created automatically when the SysML v2 Plugin is first launched and cannot be edited or deleted.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The project categories are highlighted in the image.

###### Creating new project categories in the local repository

To create new project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] and do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Create Category [ATTACHMENT filename='create category button.png'] button. The Create Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Create Category command. The Create Category dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, specify the parent category and the name for the new category, then click OK .

###### Deleting project categories in the local repository

To delete project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Delete Category [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The Delete Category question dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Delete Category command. The Delete Category question dialog opens.
2. In the Delete Category dialog, click Yes to confirm the deletion.

###### Renaming project categories in the local repository

To rename project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Rename Category [ATTACHMENT filename='rename category button.png'] button. The Rename Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Rename Category command. The Rename Category dialog opens.
2. In the Rename Category dialog, specify the new name and click OK .

###### Moving projects to other project categories in the local repository 2026xMoving projects to other project categories in the local repository

Moving the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. Learn more in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] subsection.

To move projects to other project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Move To Category [ATTACHMENT filename='move to category button.png'] button. The Move To Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click the project and in the shortcut menu, click the Move To Category command. The Move To Category dialog opens.
2. In the Move To Category dialog, select the needed category and click OK .

###### Managing empty project categories in the local repository

To manage empty project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Show empty categories [ATTACHMENT filename='Show empty categories button.png'] button to enable or disable the display of empty categories.

##### Local repository information

Locally saved projects are stored in the local repository within the modeling tool’s configuration files. You can access this repository in your file system; however, the projects are saved in a format readable only by the modeling tool and should be opened only as described in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P']****procedure.

To access the local depository within the modeling tool’s configuration files in your file system

1. In the modeling tool's main menu, go to Help > About <modeling tool name> .
2. In the Environment tab, click the file path next to Configuration Files . The opened repository contains the localRepository with the local projects (e.g., ...\AppData\Local\.<modeling tool name>\2026x\localRepository ).

Some buttons in the Manage Projects dialog are either disabled or not recommended for usage (e.g., **Clone**, **Version Properties**, **Project History**, **Edit Branches**, **Set Password**, and **Update from Local Project**) as they will be removed in future releases.

#### NOTE: 2026x HotFix1 version

2026x HotFix1 version

This documentation page is for the 2026x HotFix1 version of the SysML v2 Plugin. If you are working with the 2026x version, expand the section above. However, we highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix1 version.

Local projects are managed via the local repository instead of working with individual files. The projects are stored in the local repository within the modeling tool’s configuration files. The local repository serves as a central storage for all your projects, their dependencies, and standard libraries. This makes it easier to keep track of your models, review your work, and smoothly transition between local and server projects. It also provides a more robust format for exchanging models in air-gapped environments between servers.

When you open a project from the file system, the tool first imports it into the local repository. After the import completes, the tool opens the project copy from the local repository, not the file in your file system. The tool handles sample projects in the same way. As a result, every project you create, open, or import is stored in the local repository, allowing you to view and modify it.

The projects can be saved in an**.mdszip format**, where a single file contains both the project and its used projects. This file is intended only for importing projects into your local repository or server. Once imported, all ongoing work is saved directly in the repository. You only need to export to a file again if you want to share your project or create a backup.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### The Manage Projects dialog

The Manage Project dialog allows you to manage projects in the local repository. It displays all the projects in the local repository and allows you to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'], and[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] your projects.

- The Name column in the Manage Projects dialog displays projects and project categories. Additionally, projects are displayed with the information about their [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] .
- The Last modified column displays the last project modification date and time.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Manage Projects dialog

To open the Manage Projects dialog

- In the main menu, click File > Local Repository . The Manage Projects dialog opens.

##### Managing projects in the local repository

###### Creating projects in the local repository

Create projects in the local repository to create your models.

To create projects in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the dialog, click the New button. The New Project dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, specify the project name and category, then click OK .

###### Opening projects from the local repository

Open projects from the local repository to view and modify them in the tool.

To open projects from the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the dialog, do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed project and click the Open button.
  2. Double-click the needed projects.

###### Importing projects to the local repository

- The project from the file system is first imported into the local repository, and then opened from there. Therefore, the project file you open and work on in the tool is the project copy in the local repository, not the file in your file system.
- Imported projects are displayed in the local repository using the following structure: [<file_name>.mdszip imported @<date and time of the import>] .

To import projects to the local repository

Do one of the following:

- Open the **Manage Projects** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] . In the dialog, click the Import button. The Open dialog opens. Select the needed file from the file system and click OK .
- In the main menu, click File > Open Project . In the Open dialog, select the project and click Open .
- In the main toolbar, click the Open Project button.
- On the keyboard, press Ctrl+O .
- Drag the project from the open window of your file system to the modeling tool. The project opens.

###### Removing projects from the local repository

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project. Removing any subsequent import of the same project does not affect the original project. Learn more in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] subsection.

To remove projects from the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the dialog, select the needed project and click the Remove button. The question dialog opens. Click the Delete button to confirm the removal of the project.

###### Renaming projects in the local repository

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. Learn more in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] subsection.

To rename projects in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. Select the needed project and click the Rename button. The Rename dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, update the project name and click OK .

###### Saving projects in the local repository

To save a local project in the local repository

Do one of the following:

1. In the main menu, click File > Save Project .
2. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+S.
3. In the main toolbar, click the Save Project button.

Use the **Change Sets** tab to track project modifications. The latest changes are compared against the latest project version saved in the local repository.

- The panel legend explains the modification markings.
- Click the Refresh icon to update the display.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Exporting projects from the local repository as .*mdszip* files

Export projects from the local repository as *.mdszip* files, which can be stored anywhere on your file system as a backup or shared with colleagues.

To export projects from the local repository as .*mdszip* files

1. In the main menu, click File > Save Project As . To export a project to a file, its changes must first be saved in the local repository. If it is not, clicking the **Save As Project** command opens a question dialog. Click **Save** in the dialog to save changes in the local repository and enable the project export to a file.
2. The Save dialog opens. Select the file location in the file system and click Save .

##### Managing project imports in the local repository

Each originally created or imported project in the local repository may have multiple subsequent imports of the same project. For example, you might [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] a project from the local repository, share it with a colleague who makes changes while you continue working on the original, and then later [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] your colleague’s modified copy back into your local repository. In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the original and the subsequent import of the same project. You can modify both projects independently, and although they are displayed as separate projects, they are treated as distinct but still interdependent instances of the same project.

- The original project**** is the first instance of the project that exists in the local repository, which can be created in or imported into the local repository.
  - If the original project was created in the local repository, it is displayed only by indicating the project name.
  - If the original project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed using the following structure: [<file_name>.mdszip imported @<date and time of the import>] .
- Subsequent imports of the same project are any later instances of the project that are imported into the local repository.
  - Subsequent imports of the same project originate only by importing a project into the local repository. They are displayed using the following structure: [<file_name>.mdszip imported @<date and time of the import>] .

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project. Removing any subsequent import of the same project does not affect the original project.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.

You can view the project details in the **Manage Projects** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Managing project categories in the local repository

Project categories in the local repository help you organize and structure your local projects, providing a more efficient project management experience. You can create, delete, rename, and move projects between them. Category management commands are available in the toolbar of the **Manage Projects** dialog and the shortcut menus of categories and projects.

The*Standard Libraries*category is pre-populated with the SysML v2 standard libraries required for modeling. It is created automatically when the SysML v2 Plugin is first launched and cannot be edited or deleted.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The project categories are highlighted in the image.

###### Creating new project categories in the local repository

To create new project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] and do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Create Category [ATTACHMENT filename='create category button.png'] button. The Create Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Create Category command. The Create Category dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, specify the parent category and the name for the new category, then click OK .

###### Deleting project categories in the local repository

To delete project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Delete Category [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The Delete Category question dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Delete Category command. The Delete Category question dialog opens.
2. In the Delete Category dialog, click Yes to confirm the deletion.

###### Renaming project categories in the local repository

To rename project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Rename Category [ATTACHMENT filename='rename category button.png'] button. The Rename Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Rename Category command. The Rename Category dialog opens.
2. In the Rename Category dialog, specify the new name and click OK .

###### Moving projects to other project categories in the local repository

Moving the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. Learn more in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] subsection.

To move projects to other project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Move To Category [ATTACHMENT filename='move to category button.png'] button. The Move To Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click the project and in the shortcut menu, click the Move To Category command. The Move To Category dialog opens.
2. In the Move To Category dialog, select the needed category and click OK .

###### Managing empty project categories in the local repository

To manage empty project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Show empty categories [ATTACHMENT filename='Show empty categories button.png'] button to enable or disable the display of empty categories.

##### Local repository information

Locally saved projects are stored in the local repository within the modeling tool’s configuration files. You can access this repository in your file system; however, the projects are saved in a format readable only by the modeling tool and should be opened only as described in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P']**procedure.

To access the local depository within the modeling tool’s configuration files in your file system

1. In the modeling tool's main menu, go to Help > About <modeling tool name> .
2. In the Environment tab, click the file path next to Configuration Files . The opened repository contains the localRepository with the local projects (e.g., ...\AppData\Local\.<modeling tool name>\<modeling tool version>\localRepository ).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c85d4841-5927-41bb-a823-97acef86a23d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Click here to expand the 'Managing local projects' documentation for the 2026x version...</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Local projects are managed via the local repository instead of working with individual files. The projects are stored in the local repository within the modeling tool’s configuration files. The local repository serves as a central storage for all your projects, their dependencies, and standard libraries. This makes it easier to keep track of your models, review your work, and smoothly transition between local and server projects. It also provides a more robust format for exchanging models in air-gapped environments between servers.</p><p>When you open a project from the file system, the tool first imports it into the local repository. After the import completes, the tool opens the project copy from the local repository, not the file in your file system. The tool handles sample projects in the same way. As a result, every project you create, open, or import is stored in the local repository, allowing you to view and modify it.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The projects can be saved in an </span><strong style="text-align: left;">.mdszip format</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">, where a single file contains both the project and its used projects. This file is intended only for importing projects into your local repository or server. Once imported, all ongoing work is saved directly in the repository. You only need to export to a file again if you want to share your project or create a backup.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Local_repository_2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><h3>The Manage Projects dialog</h3><p style="text-align: left;">The Manage Project dialog allows you to manage projects in the local repository. It displays all the projects in the local repository and allows you to <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating projects in the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Opening projects from the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[open]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Importing projects to the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing projects from the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Renaming projects in the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rename]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>, and<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Managing local project categories 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[categorize]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> your projects.</p><ul><li data-uuid="99219734-4d83-436d-911a-eaf5b028b7a5">The <strong>Name </strong>column in the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog displays projects and project categories.</li><li data-uuid="2c3c1269-8093-4fc2-8d39-46eb95bebb58">The <strong>Branches </strong>column displays the names of <ac:link ac:anchor="Managing project imports in the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project and their imports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="5aba5b18-c747-455a-8b51-b8a3e8736565">The <strong>Last modified</strong> column displays the last project modification date and time.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Manage_projects_dialog_2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91a30893-caa3-4564-b724-cd19ebc1283a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Opening the Manage Projects dialog 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Opening the Manage Projects dialog</h4><p>To open the Manage Projects dialog</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="57733040-215f-4163-afb9-7e07e17ea5db">In the main menu, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Local Repository</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog opens.</li></ul><h3>Managing local projects </h3><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b8f6e42-115f-418b-bedf-af3c562f8924"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Creating projects in the local repository 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating projects in the local repository</h4><p>Create projects in the local repository to create your models.</p><p>To create projects in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ceb70830-80a3-4443-af08-e7572a7138f6"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="2e2aac46-0cee-466c-b4f2-49d0d6e02e40">In the dialog, click the <strong>New</strong> button. The <strong>New Project </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="7231ad98-76ef-4d26-bbc0-b6397eb29832">In the dialog, specify the project name and category, then click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f94ab0fe-b09e-47b8-9552-dab962797d22"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Opening projects from the local repository 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Opening projects from the local repository</h4><p>Open projects from the local repository to view and modify them in the tool.</p><p>To open projects from the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d9f47daf-25ca-4788-a887-54c57ef2218b"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="8803ead0-b9e6-43d5-97e9-8758915e8c2f">In the dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>Select the needed project and click the <strong>Open</strong> button.</li><li>Double-click the needed project.</li></ol></li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a23a906-84ec-4b4b-b751-c3ce12eb917e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Importing projects to the local repository 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Importing projects to the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f2ed913-d11d-4c41-939a-fdad58daed7b"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="42db5c47-e264-41ce-bd00-ff9794bf89c1">The project from the file system is first imported into the local repository, and then opened from there. Therefore, the project file you open and work on in the tool is the project copy in the local repository, not the file in your file system. </li><li data-uuid="f2fb5d5d-d35a-4b8e-9e97-43f6ac56ef0e">Imported projects are displayed in the local repository using the following structure: <em>&lt;project_name&gt;</em> or <em>&lt;project_name&lt;number&gt;&gt;</em>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To import projects to the local repository</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="bfeb4a13-dd9f-40b0-9f0d-87597cfa8a74"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>. In the dialog, click the <strong>Import</strong> button. The <strong>Open </strong>dialog opens. Select the needed file from the file system and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="3b16a029-693a-4dad-8b49-5a60c0b46b37">In the main menu, click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Open Project</strong>. In the <strong>Open</strong> dialog, select the project and click<strong> Open</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="bb5b7573-5883-4890-a48b-a4cf5b6f5ab0">In the main toolbar, click the <strong>Open Project</strong> button.</li><li data-uuid="f7d34695-e3ce-4e8f-bb58-b5c79aae5b73">On the keyboard, press <strong>Ctrl+O</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="7607d3c8-b1f6-4e35-89b5-636fad86a09c">Drag the project from the open window of your file system to the modeling tool. The project opens.</li></ul><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f3cb942-29b8-4da2-bc44-a57abd4f0f24"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Removing projects from the local repository 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Removing projects from the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cca7795f-56af-47dc-b371-cc6e5bd6c2b8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Removing the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project, and vice versa. Learn more in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Managing project imports in the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing project imports in the local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> subsection.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To remove projects from the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c2c49ae1-b7ba-448f-9aad-437d2bb76f84"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="04dc3dbd-be31-4352-abc5-577c23b62ee1">In the dialog, select the needed project and click the <strong>Remove</strong> button. In the dropdown menu, select <strong>Delete Draft </strong>or <strong>Delete Project</strong>. </li><li data-uuid="3a0b1105-aa29-4a4e-8208-65cb32ed6d02">The question dialog opens. Click the <strong>Delete</strong> button to confirm the removal of the project.</li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="14332723-17e5-45fb-b843-d868569e83a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Renaming projects in the local repository 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Renaming projects in the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b73cec7-d808-487d-b907-a8dd4a7138e9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Renaming the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.
````
