Learn more in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Managing project imports in the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing project imports in the local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> subsection.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To rename projects in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="e0201773-2bc2-4346-8610-e5bd9909e548"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="47f30f31-5b13-4f28-ad36-4cad51f91b65">In the dialog, select the needed project and click the <strong>Rename</strong> button. The <strong>Rename </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="0b0aa0db-4542-419a-a146-ff34a0ab7f83">In the dialog, update the project name and click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1fc547e5-7cc7-46f3-89c4-25dfe36b32a5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Managing project imports in the local repository 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Managing project imports in the local repository</h3><p><span>Each originally created or imported project in the local repository may have multiple subsequent imports of the same project. For example, you might <ac:link ac:anchor="Exporting projects from the local repository as .mdszip files 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[export]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> a project from the local repository, share it with a colleague who makes changes while you continue working on the original, and then later <ac:link ac:anchor="Importing projects to the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> your colleague’s modified copy back into your local repository. In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the original and the subsequent import of the same project. You can modify both projects independently, and although they are displayed as separate projects, they are treated as distinct but still interdependent instances of the same project. </span></p><ul><li data-uuid="18ce2ded-4596-41ec-b287-fe66fdd88b21">The <strong>original project </strong>is the first instance of the project that exists in the local repository, which can be created in or imported into the local repository. <ul><li>If the original project was created in the local repository, it is displayed as <em>trunk</em>. </li><li>If the original project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed as <em>&lt;imported_file_name&gt;</em><span>.</span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="87491fba-9328-49fb-9233-daf476f1fef4"><strong>Subsequent imports of the same project</strong> are any later instances of the project that are imported into the local repository.<ul><li>Subsequent imports of the same project originate only by importing a project into the local repository. They are displayed as: <em>&lt;imported_file_name&gt;</em>.<ul><li>If there already is an import with the same name, a number is added: <em>&lt;imported_file_name&lt;number&gt;&gt;</em>.</li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23eef6fe-aa62-4b4e-a7ac-c2375bd7d749"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="fc668ca0-f745-414f-89b7-0f6dc913f0d9"><ac:link ac:anchor="Renaming projects in the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Renaming]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.</li><li data-uuid="9053d56f-4c5c-4d73-84d4-5dfeb23a9771"><ac:link ac:anchor="Removing projects from the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Removing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project, and vice versa.</li><li data-uuid="c0f1fe7e-a52b-47e0-8342-995d7691f4a8"><ac:link ac:anchor="Moving projects to other project categories in the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Moving]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can view the project details in the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_instances_2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Saving local projects</h3><p>There are three ways to save a local project:</p><ul><li data-uuid="0e423b42-c603-4a73-8f4c-8053fff63767"><p><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Saving projects as drafts in the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[As a draft]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link></strong>. Recommended for ongoing work. Uses less disk space than saving as a new version in the local repository.</p></li><li data-uuid="76429502-7f5e-4ecb-9fca-626d5efd809b"><p><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Saving projects as new versions in the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[As a new version]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link></strong>. Saves the project's new version in the local repository. This option is required if you want to export the project to a file or to create a reusable baseline for other projects. Note that it consumes more disk space than saving as a draft.</p></li><li data-uuid="8eed1e1e-f2ac-4c60-9361-5161ab8994eb"><p><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Exporting projects from the local repository as .mdszip files 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[As an exported file]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link></strong>. Exports the project as an <em>.mdszip</em> file, which can be stored anywhere on your file system and shared with colleagues.</p></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1074d31-938f-43d9-82a5-4710cb6ca8d9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use <strong>Change Sets</strong> to track project modifications. The latest changes are compared against the most recent project version saved in the local repository.</p><ul><li data-uuid="49138934-55a9-4314-8a8b-00fec36631f8">The panel legend explains the modification markings.</li><li data-uuid="a5184059-9efb-4850-af29-1097c444137c">Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> icon to update the display.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Change Sets.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="830145cc-d6f5-43b7-9af5-d9f1f7c81d33"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Saving projects as drafts in the local repository 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Saving projects as drafts in the local repository</h4><p>To save projects as drafts in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d4c1bf44-a9dc-4e38-be4c-4f9c5e397e3d">Do one of the following:<ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Save Project</strong>.</li><li>On the keyboard, press Ctrl+S. </li><li>In the main toolbar, click the <strong>Save Project </strong>button.<br />Projects saved as drafts are displayed with the asterisk symbol on the project icon in the local repository.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_as_draft.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dc961119-c0e0-4bb3-8423-8b834b989695"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Saving projects as new versions in the local repository 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Saving projects as new versions in the local repository</h4><p>To save projects as new versions in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c33e0819-0ccb-4ca4-9165-f11bc07c3a97">Do one of the following:<ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Save As New Version</strong>.</li><li>On the keyboard, press Ctrl+K.<br />The question dialog opens. </li></ol></li><li data-uuid="6e753e82-e85b-4110-9bbd-7cd9d631c2d3">In the dialog, click the <strong>Save As New Version </strong>button to confirm. The <strong>Save As New Version </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="1df4a740-e22b-45e0-894d-27ea546d92f3">In the dialog, specify a comment (optional) and click <strong>Save</strong>.</li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c5a94d82-fd21-4745-8bdc-185628597fa3"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Exporting projects from the local repository as .mdszip files 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Exporting projects from the local repository as .<em>mdszip</em> files</h4><p>To export projects from the local repository as .<em>mdszip</em> files</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d969a07c-cd8e-4b39-b14f-5cfce34d6b92">In the main menu, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Save Project As</strong>. <ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3afcd461-f40b-4799-bb11-1109833648f2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To export a project to a file, its new version must first be saved in the local repository. If it is not, clicking the <strong>Save As Project</strong> command opens the <strong>Save As New Version</strong> dialog. Click <strong>Save</strong> in the dialog to store the new version in the local repository and enable the project export to a file.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="f8bbca43-247c-42ca-ae27-ff7a494e45fa">The <strong>Save </strong>dialog opens. Select the file location in the file system and click <strong>Save</strong>.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad6b027e-ab11-48ce-9a60-ca315d69e2d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Managing local project categories 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Managing local project categories</h3><p>Project categories in the local repository help you organize and structure your local projects, providing a more efficient project management experience. You can create, delete, rename, and move projects between them. Category management commands are available in the toolbar of the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog and the shortcut menus of categories and projects.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c8749e9-8f64-45bc-92eb-6e54550ce513"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The<span> </span><em>Standard Libraries</em><span> </span>category is pre-populated with the SysML v2 standard libraries required for modeling. It is created automatically when the SysML v2 Plugin is first launched and cannot be edited or deleted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Managing project categories in the local repository_2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The project categories are highlighted in the image.  </h6><h4>Creating new project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To create new project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1291e258-607d-4603-91b9-654e76d362a9"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> and do one of the following:<ol><li>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Create Category</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create category button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The <strong>Create </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Create Category</strong> command. The <strong>Create </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="d7058893-fded-492f-86a3-b04747edf78c">In the dialog, specify the parent category and the name for the new category, then click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Deleting project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To delete project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="254a4560-1f6f-498e-9e36-0d902a456b1f"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> and do one of the following:<ol><li>Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Delete Category <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete category button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> button. The <strong>Delete Category </strong>question dialog opens. </li><li>Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Delete Category </strong>command. The <strong>Delete Category </strong>question dialog opens. </li></ol></li><li data-uuid="7333dee0-3ce0-4649-9d71-ccfc578f0579">In the <strong>Delete Category </strong>dialog, click <strong>Yes </strong>to confirm the deletion.</li></ol><h4>Renaming project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To rename project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="be6ddb9f-b0f8-4628-98d2-2c1ea73d6e7b"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> and do one of the following: <ol><li>Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Rename Category</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rename category button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The <strong>Rename </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Rename Category</strong> command. The <strong>Rename </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="50cf8b7b-d547-4a38-b9f0-5fab7cac68ef">In the <strong>Rename </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, specify the new name and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="267a6347-c489-496b-85bd-4ee5bd2933cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Moving projects to other project categories in the local repository 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Moving projects to other project categories in the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c12ae60f-92b9-4df3-9e2f-980234f14238"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Moving the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. Learn more in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Managing project imports in the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing project imports in the local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> subsection.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To move projects to other project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5b66f264-f2fa-438f-a7a1-52dc9dfe153a"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> and do one of the following:<ol><li>Select the needed project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Move To Category</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move to category button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The <strong>Move To Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Right-click the project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Move To Category</strong> command. The <strong>Move To Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="86d699ae-8714-4c36-80e1-1330e62ea48a">In the <strong>Move To Category </strong>dialog, select the needed category and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Managing empty project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To manage empty project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="027b0385-2a74-4ca3-a7e6-a94b90980009"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="f96855a8-91fa-4bd8-91c1-3b0512fb8058">In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Show empty categories</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show empty categories button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button to enable or disable the display of empty categories. </li></ol><h3>Local repository information</h3><p>Locally saved projects are stored in the local repository within the modeling tool’s configuration files. You can access this repository in your file system; however, the projects are saved in a format readable only by the modeling tool and should be opened only as described in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Opening projects from the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Opening projects from the local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link><strong> </strong>procedure.</p><p>To access the local depository within the modeling tool’s configuration files in your file system</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="3fe04632-367f-4606-8fd3-d3879c78c90a">In the modeling tool's main menu, go to <strong>Help</strong> &gt; <strong>About &lt;modeling tool name&gt;</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="6886d7b3-aee9-4556-a416-34602b5eef73">In the <strong>Environment</strong> tab, click the file path next to <strong>Configuration Files</strong>. The opened repository contains the <strong>localRepository </strong>with the local projects (e.g., <em>...\AppData\Local\.&lt;modeling tool name&gt;\2026x\localRepository</em>).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1056659b-4c6e-4c80-8e93-633b138a2685"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Some buttons in the Manage Projects dialog are either disabled or not recommended for usage (e.g., <strong>Clone</strong>, <strong>Version Properties</strong>, <strong>Project History</strong>, <strong>Edit Branches</strong>, <strong>Set Password</strong>, and <strong>Update from Local Project</strong>) as they will be removed in future releases.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="175ece1f-43af-418f-92b0-425f7ae343e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2026x HotFix1 version</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This documentation page is for the 2026x HotFix1 version of the SysML v2 Plugin. If you are working with the 2026x version, expand the section above. However, we highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix1 version.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: left;">Local projects are managed via the local repository instead of working with individual files. The projects are stored in the local repository within the modeling tool’s configuration files. The local repository serves as a central storage for all your projects, their dependencies, and standard libraries. This makes it easier to keep track of your models, review your work, and smoothly transition between local and server projects. It also provides a more robust format for exchanging models in air-gapped environments between servers.</p><p>When you open a project from the file system, the tool first imports it into the local repository. After the import completes, the tool opens the project copy from the local repository, not the file in your file system. The tool handles sample projects in the same way. As a result, every project you create, open, or import is stored in the local repository, allowing you to view and modify it.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The projects can be saved in an </span><strong style="text-align: left;">.mdszip format</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">, where a single file contains both the project and its used projects. This file is intended only for importing projects into your local repository or server. Once imported, all ongoing work is saved directly in the repository. You only need to export to a file again if you want to share your project or create a backup.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Local_repository_2026xHF1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p><br /></p><h3>The Manage Projects dialog</h3><p style="text-align: left;">The Manage Project dialog allows you to manage projects in the local repository. It displays all the projects in the local repository and allows you to <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating projects in the local repository"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Opening projects from the local repository"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[open]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Importing projects to the local repository"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing projects from the local repository"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Renaming projects in the local repository"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rename]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>, and<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Managing project categories in the local repository"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[categorize]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> your projects.</p><ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="e2283cdd-eeae-41d7-9d66-8cb69fb30022">The <strong>Name </strong>column in the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog displays projects and project categories. Additionally, projects are displayed with the information about their <ac:link ac:anchor="Managing project imports in the local repository"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[imports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="df6ff85b-f58f-4d17-bd01-44bf7e990c66">The <strong>Last modified</strong> column displays the last project modification date and time.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Manage_projects_dialog_2026xHF1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4>Opening the Manage Projects dialog</h4><p>To open the Manage Projects dialog</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="85d97ec5-3e79-4b54-a743-8e62a1bb9d29">In the main menu, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Local Repository</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog opens.</li></ul><h3>Managing projects in the local repository</h3><h4>Creating projects in the local repository</h4><p>Create projects in the local repository to create your models.</p><p>To create projects in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ee4dc1bd-426f-478b-a2ba-880dc831475d"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="49fdd9ba-f50c-4182-95ad-04282f6175c1">In the dialog, click the <strong>New</strong> button. The <strong>New Project </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="f1bab07a-4c2f-4bb7-861d-bc0dbe7a0881">In the dialog, specify the project name and category, then click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Opening projects from the local repository</h4><p>Open projects from the local repository to view and modify them in the tool.</p><p>To open projects from the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="371a2a42-1e22-4355-b439-c3e671ce13b3"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="760f0de5-324f-4d64-bcdf-6be8e191cfe2">In the dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>Select the needed project and click the <strong>Open</strong> button.</li><li>Double-click the needed projects.</li></ol></li></ol><h4>Importing projects to the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f3f80e0-1412-46b6-a3c6-8951f34232ad"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li style="text-align: left;">The project from the file system is first imported into the local repository, and then opened from there. Therefore, the project file you open and work on in the tool is the project copy in the local repository, not the file in your file system. </li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="77541ec8-49af-4236-935e-6e052dc0f73a">Imported projects are displayed in the local repository using the following structure: <em>[&lt;file_name&gt;.mdszip imported @&lt;date and time of the import&gt;]</em>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To import projects to the local repository</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="19e3a14e-bd62-4d21-a323-bc5e363548f1"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>. In the dialog, click the <strong>Import</strong> button. The <strong>Open </strong>dialog opens. Select the needed file from the file system and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="fa68502d-35cd-4425-b6eb-58af1edb37de">In the main menu, click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Open Project</strong>. In the <strong>Open</strong> dialog, select the project and click<strong> Open</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="c815147d-bdf5-4ffb-8442-65f72ea16198">In the main toolbar, click the <strong>Open Project</strong> button.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="2c085453-ce82-47b6-8a1c-e605bb68abe5">On the keyboard, press <strong>Ctrl+O</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="43ef0d7e-c5ca-4213-bb82-09c2fcea84d3">Drag the project from the open window of your file system to the modeling tool. The project opens.</li></ul><h4>Removing projects from the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f17e9a71-ebd1-4500-baa1-73339606668e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Removing projects from the local repository"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Removing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project. Removing any subsequent import of the same project does not affect the original project. Learn more in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Managing project imports in the local repository"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> subsection.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To remove projects from the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="00b29092-b408-4bee-854d-c9344143b8b4"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="57d7b69c-0081-4134-aefb-3ee85f1ea08b">In the dialog, select the needed project and click the <strong>Remove</strong> button. The question dialog opens. Click the <strong>Delete</strong> button to confirm the removal of the project.</li></ol><h4>Renaming projects in the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="559532c8-f093-43f0-8ce2-44ee6f67431d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Renaming projects in the local repository"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Renaming]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. Learn more in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Managing project imports in the local repository"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> subsection.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1da4df71-dafa-4d67-816c-e5751622bae1">rename</ac:inline-comment-marker> projects in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="91711a88-b145-4af2-a80d-06c7e431dc22"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="396c4e95-1bde-405f-9c9b-dc2cffd6e82e">Select the needed project and click the <strong>Rename</strong> button. The <strong>Rename </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="d58279fe-fb67-4fab-a373-00566291e6d9">In the dialog, update the project name and click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h4>Saving projects in the local repository</h4><p>To save a local project in the local repository</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Save Project</strong>.</li><li>On the keyboard, press Ctrl+S. </li><li>In the main toolbar, click the <strong>Save Project </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9524795-92c1-43de-ab42-76fbdd6e33bd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use the <strong>Change Sets</strong> tab to track project modifications. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The latest changes are compared against the latest project version saved in the local repository.</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="d6ed4058-2971-482f-b19d-3a8933ba1194">The panel legend explains the modification markings.</li><li data-uuid="0b07e60e-1b1e-466b-974d-d2ac78e97243">Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> icon to update the display.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Change Sets.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Exporting projects from the local repository as .<em>mdszip</em> files</h4><p>Export projects from the local repository as <em>.mdszip</em> files, which can be stored anywhere on your file system as a backup or shared with colleagues.</p><p>To export projects from the local repository as .<em>mdszip</em> files</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5cf7e9a1-da44-47f9-9912-66fd996ddc36">In the main menu, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Save Project As</strong>. <ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b4c0a8a4-2b8c-44c5-83d6-c201d3a97518"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To export a project to a file, its changes must first be saved in the local repository. If it is not, clicking the <strong>Save As Project</strong> command opens a question dialog. Click <strong>Save</strong> in the dialog to save changes in the local repository and enable the project export to a file.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="7b98258a-32b0-4d80-8c0e-568e2ec647e6">The <strong>Save </strong>dialog opens. Select the file location in the file system and click <strong>Save</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Managing project imports in the local repository</h3><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Each originally created or imported project in the local repository may have multiple subsequent imports of the same project. For example, you might <ac:link ac:anchor="Exporting projects from the local repository as .mdszip files"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[export]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> a project from the local repository, share it with a colleague who makes changes while you continue working on the original, and then later <span><ac:link ac:anchor="Importing projects to the local repository"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link></span> your colleague’s modified copy back into your local repository. In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the original and the subsequent import of the same project. <span>You can modify both projects independently, and although they are displayed as separate projects, they are treated as distinct but still interdependent instances of the same project. </span></span></p><ul><li>The <strong>original project<strong> </strong></strong>is the first instance of the project that exists in the local repository, which can be created in or imported into the local repository. <ul><li data-uuid="6d2344a0-8bbf-4b3f-ad6c-da695ec0f405">If the original project was created in the local repository, it is displayed only by indicating the project name. </li><li data-uuid="a0cfe82f-74bf-4858-b3cb-8aeca3cef2d3">If the original project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed using the following structure: <em>[&lt;file_name&gt;.mdszip imported @&lt;date and time of the import&gt;]</em>.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="7e3f09e6-0e40-466b-bf11-f21b66c60206"><strong>Subsequent imports of the same project</strong> are any later instances of the project that are imported into the local repository.<ul><li data-uuid="e8b0764b-3f97-4d0a-9eb4-4fd3f9ac1221">Subsequent imports of the same project originate only by importing a project into the local repository. They are displayed using the following structure: <em>[&lt;file_name&gt;.mdszip imported @&lt;date and time of the import&gt;]</em>.</li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9d3f9c9-ec45-4eab-b985-ef854f116ad3"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="508ee9cc-674c-43e2-bb5d-dc300f89e591"><ac:link ac:anchor="Renaming projects in the local repository"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Renaming]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.</li><li data-uuid="f2e7ae5e-faba-49e9-af65-4ed168ea7f01"><ac:link ac:anchor="Removing projects from the local repository"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Removing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project. Removing any subsequent import of the same project does not affect the original project.</li><li data-uuid="e04f2e61-e594-48d4-8c70-b394c1d5061d"><ac:link ac:anchor="Moving projects to other project categories in the local repository"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Moving]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can view the project details in the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_instances_2026xHF1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Managing project categories in the local repository</h3><p>Project categories in the local repository help you organize and structure your local projects, providing a more efficient project management experience. You can create, delete, rename, and move projects between them. Category management commands are available in the toolbar of the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog and the shortcut menus of categories and projects.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae7eef1c-6c85-4c82-b820-b9265d06c858"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The<span> </span><em>Standard Libraries</em><span> </span>category is pre-populated with the SysML v2 standard libraries required for modeling. It is created automatically when the SysML v2 Plugin is first launched and cannot be edited or deleted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Managing project categories in the local repository_2026xHF1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The project categories are highlighted in the image. </h6><h4>Creating new project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To create new project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="61d8e72b-9d0d-4b21-aed1-e870466adef5"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> and do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="60838c58-13cb-4525-9761-d64e0cec68f9">In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Create Category</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create category button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The <strong>Create </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="6dc30037-0c50-4839-9317-799d2e78f2e5">Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Create Category</strong> command. The <strong>Create </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="7e35f7fd-be72-4cc7-b89f-c00608959faa">In the dialog, specify the parent category and the name for the new category, then click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Deleting project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To delete project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5a0a383a-0e1e-41cd-b9a2-7c406566b263"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> and do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="d2ec254e-df7e-49b9-b52b-5b4adf47ce72">Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Delete Category <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete category button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> button. The <strong>Delete Category </strong>question dialog opens. </li><li data-uuid="08d8f53f-f461-4cd5-ac1e-5db06f089abf">Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Delete Category </strong>command. The <strong>Delete Category </strong>question dialog opens. </li></ol></li><li data-uuid="946d2d08-2b5a-4b2d-b750-73b6c12b81e3">In the <strong>Delete Category </strong>dialog, click <strong>Yes </strong>to confirm the deletion.</li></ol><h4>Renaming project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To rename project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="fe96a45c-728b-4af1-9e92-9a37c7b3ca8c"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> and do one of the following: <ol><li data-uuid="89d0df79-e1d5-4e87-8b4d-e534217baac3">Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Rename Category</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rename category button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The <strong>Rename </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="36e28752-59cd-46ac-83c5-0d7c452b10bb">Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Rename Category</strong> command. The <strong>Rename </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="cea5f60c-bad1-4d94-a458-1cc60261d57e">In the <strong>Rename </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, specify the new name and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Moving projects to other project categories in the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ddbc4c8-3b0c-4931-804e-bbbae91126b4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Moving the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. Learn more in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Managing project imports in the local repository"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> subsection.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To move projects to other project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7a065692-e865-404c-a1df-d32a7629d177"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> and do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="eca0be86-c920-4901-993c-f460d9853825">Select the needed project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Move To Category</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move to category button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The <strong>Move To Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="bfedbfd9-e537-4483-9164-eeb13ff9b922">Right-click the project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Move To Category</strong> command. The <strong>Move To Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="bae0f61d-5f77-43cd-acfe-860c675844b2">In the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Move To Category </strong>dialog, select the needed category and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Managing empty project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To manage empty project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ed4fc3f2-d89a-4655-93c4-a9ec83852219"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="dd856de9-a5e3-4c55-a5ef-8976a0bb1230">In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Show empty categories</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show empty categories button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button to enable or disable the display of empty categories. </li></ol><h3>Local repository information</h3><p>Locally saved projects are stored in the local repository within the modeling tool’s configuration files. You can access this repository in your file system; however, the projects are saved in a format readable only by the modeling tool and should be opened only as described in the <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening projects from the local repository"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /></ac:link> </strong>procedure.</p><p>To access the local depository within the modeling tool’s configuration files in your file system</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="6bd43ea9-e048-4916-875d-21eeb520119a">In the modeling tool's main menu, go to <strong>Help</strong> &gt; <strong>About &lt;modeling tool name&gt;</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="ce7b1d93-27fd-4e8e-9cae-e307d2e84d5e">In the <strong>Environment</strong> tab, click the file path next to <strong>Configuration Files</strong>. The opened repository contains the <strong>localRepository </strong>with the local projects (e.g., <em>...<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2063d629-03c0-4671-87f3-44ed7bcb5f1d">\</ac:inline-comment-marker>AppData\Local\.&lt;modeling tool name&gt;\&lt;modeling tool version&gt;\localRepository</em>).</li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298092927 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00094: (2026x) Managing local used projects

- page_id: `298092927`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298092927/2026x+Managing+local+used+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-19T08:23:54.046+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXPAND: Click here to expand the 'Managing local used projects' documentation for the 2026x version...

Click here to expand the 'Managing local used projects' documentation for the 2026x version...

It is advisable to split large projects with loosely dependent parts into used projects. Project partitioning provides additional benefits, such as reusing model parts (e.g., libraries) across multiple projects and improving performance when working with very large models. The used projects are always added from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P']; thus, the projects must first be added there to be used in other projects. Once added to the main project, you can view the used projects under the Containment tree's Used Projects node.

You can manage used projects via the Containment tree, the **Project Usages**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'], and the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'].

There can be two kinds of used projects:

- *Directly used projects*: projects used directly by the main project.
- *Indirectly used projects*: projects used by other used projects. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] subsection.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### The Project Usages dialog 2026xThe Project Usages dialog

The Project Usages dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] a used project, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P']. Selecting a used project in the project usages tree displays the project's details (e.g., version, location) on the right side of the dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Project Usages dialog 2026xOpening the Project Usages dialog

To open the Project Usages dialog

1. Do one of the following:
  1. In the main menu, select Options > Project Usages .
  2. In the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
  3. In the Containment tree, expand the Used Projects node and right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click Options . The Project Usages dialog opens.

##### The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026xThe Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] a used project, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] of a used project. You can also review all the used projects' version information.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Some buttons in the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog are either disabled or not recommended for usage (e.g., **Project Usage Information**, **Notify on New Version**column functionality) as their functionality will be changed and/or removed in future releases.

###### Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026xOpening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To open the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. Open the **Project Usages**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the Project Usages dialog's toolbar, click the Usages Configuration [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog opens.

##### Using projects from the local repository

Used projects employ the sticky versions approach. Once a specific version of a project is used, the main project continues to use that exact version until you update it manually. This means that even if you modify a used project and save it as a new version, the main project will still use the previously added version. This prevents unexpected changes or damage to the main project.

A project must be stored in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] before it can be used locally.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The main project uses only the specified versions of the used projects, despite the newer versions they may have.

###### Using a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog 2026xUsing a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog

To use a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog

1. Open the **Project Usages**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the Project Usages dialog's toolbar, click the Use Project [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The Use Project dialog opens.
3. In the Use Project dialog, select the project you want to use and click the Next button.
4. Select the project version you want to use and click the Finish button. [ATTACHMENT filename='Using a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog 2026x.png']
5. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. Used projects are displayed under the Used Projects node in the Containment tree of your model. [ATTACHMENT filename='Used Projects node in the Containment tree 2026x.png']

###### Using a project from the local repository via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026xUsing a project from the local repository via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To use a project from the local repository via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. Open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog's toolbar, click the **Add** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button , and in its dropdown menu, click Use Project . The Use Project dialog opens.
3. In the Use Project dialog, select the project you want to use and click the Next button.
4. Select the project version you want to use and click the Finish button. The cell in the Action column for the added used project states ' Add '. [ATTACHMENT filename='Using a project from the local repository via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog 2026x.png']
5. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the Reset Changes for Selection command.
6. Do one of the following:
  1. To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the Apply button.
  2. To save changes and close the dialog, click the OK button.
  3. To discard changes and close the dialog, click the Cancel button.
7. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. Used projects are displayed under the Used Projects node in the Containment tree of your model.

##### Changing used project versions 2026xChanging used project versions

While a project may be used in the main project, it may be modified and saved to a new version, rendering its version used in the main project outdated. Therefore, in the main project, the used project is indicated with its version number, e.g., #1. You can choose to update the used project to a later version or continue using the older one. You can see the details about the used version in the Containment tree, the **Project Usages**dialog, and the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog. The **Edit Branches**dialog displays all existing versions of the used project.

- Refreshing version information display . To ensure the used project version information displayed in the Containment tree and the rest of the tool is up to date, press F5 or click View > Refresh in the main menu.
- Updating directly used projects . Updating directly used projects does not update indirectly used projects if they are outdated in the projects that use them. In this case, indirectly used projects must be updated individually. However, if the indirectly used project is already updated in the directly used one, then the indirectly used project's version is updated in the main project once you update the directly used project's version.

###### **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Updating all used projects to their latest versions

To update all used projects to their latest versions

1. Open the **Project Usages**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the Project Usages dialog's toolbar, click the Update Used Projects [ATTACHMENT filename='update_used_projects_button.png'] button. The Update Used Projects dialog opens.
3. In the Update Used Projects dialog, select the check box in the Update column for the used projects you want to update and click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='Updating all used projects to their latest versions 2026x.png']
4. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. The used projects are updated to their latest versions.

###### Changing a used project version via the Containment tree 2026xChanging a used project version via the Containment tree

To change a used project version via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, expand the Used Projects node, then right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click Change Version . The Edit Branches dialog opens.
2. In the Edit Branches dialog, select the version you want to use, and click OK . The used project version is changed. [ATTACHMENT filename='Changing a used project version via the Containment tree 2026x.png']

###### Changing a used project version via the Project Usages dialog 2026xChanging a used project version via the Project Usages dialog

To change a used project version via the Project Usages dialog

1. Open the **Project Usages**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] and d o one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the **Change Version** [ATTACHMENT filename='change_version_button.png'] button .
  2. In the dialog's project usages tree, right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Change Version**command. 
The Edit Branches dialog opens.
2. In the Edit Branches dialog, select the version you want to use, and click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='Changing a used project version via the Project Usages dialog 2026x.png']
3. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. The used project version is changed.

###### Changing a used project version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026xChanging a used project version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To change a used project version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. Open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog, do one of the following:
  1. Select a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Change Version [ATTACHMENT filename='change_version_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click a used project, then in its shortcut menu, select the Change Version command. The Edit Branches dialog opens.
3. In the Edit Branches dialog, select the version you want to use, and click OK. The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states 'Switch to <version number> in <project name>'. [ATTACHMENT filename='Changing a used project version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x.png']
4. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the Reset Changes for Selection command.
5. Do one of the following:
  1. To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the Apply button.
  2. To save changes and close the dialog, click the OK button.
  3. To discard changes and close the dialog, click the Cancel button.
6. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. The used project version is updated.

##### Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports 2026xSwitching between the original used project and its subsequent imports

In addition to versions, used projects may also have multiple imports of the same project. For example, you might [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] a project that is used in the main project from the local repository to your file system, share it with a colleague who makes changes while you continue working on the main project, and then later [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] your colleague’s modified copy back into your local repository. In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the originally used project and the subsequent import of the same project. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] page.

Although the main project continues to use the specific project (and its version) that was originally used, you can switch the used project to its different import if needed. Both the original and the import can be modified and saved as a new version, allowing you to update the used project to its latest version (as described in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] subsection).

You can view details about the used project (original or its imports) in the **Project Usages**and the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialogs:

- If the used project was created in the local repository, it is displayed as trunk .
- If the used project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed as <imported_file_name> .
  - If there already is an import with the same name, a number is added: <imported_file_name<number>> .

To ensure the used project import and/or version information displayed in the Containment tree and the rest of the tool is up to date, press **F5**or click **View**> **Refresh** in the main menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports

To switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports

See the following procedures:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P']

##### Opening used projects

Projects can only use other projects in read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as a stand-alone. You can do this from within the main project in which the project is used by following the procedures listed below.

###### Opening a used project via the Containment tree 2026xOpening a used project via the Containment tree

To open a used project via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, expand the Used Projects node.
2. Right-click a used project and select one of the following:
  1. Open This Version .
  2. Open Latest Version . The used project is opened.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog 2026xOpening a used project via the Project Usages dialog

To open a used project via the Project Usages dialog

1. Open the **Project Usages**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the Project Usages dialog, select a used project, then on the right side section of the dialog, click the Open button.
3. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. The used project is opened. [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog 2026x.png']

##### Removing used projects 2026xRemoving used projects

Used projects are removed from the main project with all their references, including their used projects.

###### Removing a used project via the Containment tree

To remove a used project via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree of the main project, expand the Used Projects node.
2. Right-click a used project and select Remove .
3. When the question dialog opens, click Yes to confirm that you want to remove the used project. [ATTACHMENT filename='Removing a used project via the Containment tree 2026x.png']

###### Removing a used project via the Project Usages dialog 2026xRemoving a used project via the Project Usages dialog

To remove a used project via the Project Usages dialog

1. Open the **Project Usages**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] and d o one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the **Remove with References** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. In the dialog's project usages tree, right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Remove with References**command.
2. When the question dialog opens, click Yes to confirm that you want to remove the used project.
3. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

###### Removing a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026xRemoving a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To remove a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. Open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog, do one of the following:
  1. S elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the **Remove with References** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. Right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Remove with References** command . The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states ' Remove, delete references '. [ATTACHMENT filename='Removing a used project via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog 2026x.png']
3. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the Reset Changes for Selection command.
4. Do one of the following:
  1. To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the Apply button.
  2. To save changes and close the dialog, click the OK button.
  3. To discard changes and close the dialog, click the Cancel button.
5. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

##### Indirectly used projects 2026xIndirectly used projects

The projects used in the main project may also use other projects, called indirectly used projects. When you use a project directly that may have other used projects, these indirectly used projects are loaded in the main project. Once loaded, the indirectly used projects use the same project version that is used in the directly used project. However, if the indirectly used project is later modified and you want this change to be reflected in the main project, you must update the project version in the directly used project, then update the version of the directly used project in the main project.

In the main project, you can see the indirectly used projects:

- Under the Containment tree's Used Projects node, displayed at the same node level as the project that uses it directly.
- The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog's Indirectly used projects category.
- The Project Usages dialog, nested under the project that uses it directly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using indirectly used projects directly 2026xUsing indirectly used projects directly

To use indirectly used projects directly

1. Open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog, do one of the following:
  1. S elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the **Use Project Directly** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. Right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Use Project Directly** command . The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states ' Use Project Directly '. [ATTACHMENT filename='Using indirectly used projects directly 2026x.png']
3. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the Reset Changes for Selection command.
4. Do one of the following:
  1. To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the Apply button.
  2. To save changes and close the dialog, click the OK button.
  3. To discard changes and close the dialog, click the Cancel button.
5. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

###### Opening indirectly used projects

To open indirectly used projects
