- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .

###### Removing indirectly used projects

Indirectly used projects can only be removed from the main project in the following cases:

- If you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
- If you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] first, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .

#### NOTE: 2026x HotFix1 version

2026x HotFix1 version

This documentation page is for the 2026x HotFix1 version of the SysML v2 Plugin. If you are working with the 2026x version, expand the section above. However, we highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix1 version.

It is advisable to split large projects with loosely dependent parts into used projects. Project partitioning provides additional benefits, such as reusing model parts (e.g., libraries) across multiple projects and improving performance when working with very large models. The used projects are always added from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing local projects' space='UAF2P']; thus, the projects must first be added there to be used in other projects. Once added to the main project, you can view the used projects under the Containment tree's Used Projects node.

You can manage used projects via the Containment tree, the **Project Usages**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'], and the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'].

There can be two kinds of used projects:

- *Directly used projects*: projects used directly by the main project.
- *Indirectly used projects*: projects used by other used projects. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] subsection.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### The Project Usages dialog

The Project Usages dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] a used project, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P']. Selecting a used project in the project usages tree displays the project's details (e.g., version, location) on the right side of the dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Project Usages dialog

To open the Project Usages dialog

1. Do one of the following:
  1. In the main menu, select Options > Project Usages .
  2. In the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
  3. In the Containment tree, expand the Used Projects node and right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click Options . The Project Usages dialog opens.

##### The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] a used project, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] of a used project. You can also review the information about the version and the imports of all used projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To open the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. Open the **Project Usages** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the Project Usages dialog's toolbar, click the Usages Configuration [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog opens.

##### Using projects from the local repository

Used projects employ the sticky versions approach. Once a specific version of a project is used, the main project continues to use that exact version until you update it manually. This means that even if you modify a used project and save it as a new version, the main project will still use the previously added version. This prevents unexpected changes or damage to the main project.

A project must be stored in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] before it can be used locally.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### The main project uses only the specified versions of the used projects, despite the newer versions they may have.

###### Using a project from the local repository via the Use Local Project command

To use a project from the local repository via the Use Local Project command

1. In the main menu, select File > Use Project > Use Local Project . The Use Project dialog opens. If there are no other projects in the local repository or all of them are already used in the open project, the **Use Local Project**command is disabled.
2. In the Use Project dialog, select the project you want to use and click the Next button.
3. Select the project version you want to use and click the Finish button. Used projects are displayed under the Used Projects node in the Containment tree of your model. [ATTACHMENT filename='Used Projects node in the Containment tree.png']

###### Using a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog

To use a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog

1. Open the **Project Usages** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the Project Usages dialog's toolbar, click the Use Project [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The Use Project dialog opens.
3. In the Use Project dialog, select the project you want to use and click the OK button. [ATTACHMENT filename='Using a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog.png']
4. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. Used projects are displayed under the Used Projects node in the Containment tree of your model.

###### Using a project from the local repository via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To use a project from the local repository via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. Open the**Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog's toolbar, click the **Add** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button , and in its dropdown menu, click Use Project . The Use Project dialog opens.
3. In the Use Project dialog, select the project you want to use and click the OK button. The cell in the Action column for the added used project states ' Add '. [ATTACHMENT filename='Using a project from the local repository via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog.png']
4. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the Reset Changes for Selection command.
5. Do one of the following:
  1. To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the Apply button.
  2. To save changes and close the dialog, click the OK button.
  3. To discard changes and close the dialog, click the Cancel button.
6. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

##### Updating used projects to their latest versions

While a project may be used in the main project, it may be modified and saved to a new version, rendering its version used in the main project outdated. Therefore, in the main project, the used project may refer to two of its available versions: **Older Version**or **Latest Version**. You can choose to update the used project to its latest version or continue using the older one. You can see more details about the used version in the Containment tree, the **Project Usages**dialog, and the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog.

- Refreshing version information display . To ensure the used project version information displayed in the Containment tree and the rest of the tool is up to date, press F5 or click View > Refresh in the main menu.
- Reverting used project version update . Once you update a used project to its latest version and save the project, reverting to an older version is not possible. However, if you do not save the main project after the update, you can close the main project without saving it to discard all changes, including the used project's version update. Thus, make sure the update is necessary to avoid potential model inconsistencies.
- Updating directly used projects . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] does not update indirectly used projects if they are outdated in the projects that use them. In this case, indirectly used projects must be updated individually. However, if the indirectly used project is already updated in the directly used one, then the indirectly used project's version is updated in the main project once you update the directly used project's version.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Updating all used projects to their latest versions

To update all used projects to their latest versions

1. Open the **Project Usages** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the Project Usages dialog's toolbar, click the Update Used Projects [ATTACHMENT filename='update_used_projects_button.png'] button. The Update Used Projects dialog opens.
3. In the Update Used Projects dialog, select the check box in the Update column for the used projects you want to update and click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='Updating all used projects to their latest versions.png']
4. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. The used projects are updated to their latest versions.

###### Updating a used project to its latest version via the Containment tree

To update a used project to its latest version via the Containment tree

- In the Containment tree, expand the Used Projects node, then right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click Switch to Latest Version . The used project is updated to its latest version. [ATTACHMENT filename='Updating a used project to its latest version via the Containment tree.png']

###### Updating a used project to its latest version via the Project Usages dialog

To update a used project to its latest version via the Project Usages dialog

1. Open the **Project Usages** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] and d o one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the **Change Version** [ATTACHMENT filename='change_version_button.png'] button , and in its dropdown menu, click Switch to Latest Version .
  2. In the dialog's project usages tree, right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Change Version > Switch to Latest Version******command.
2. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. The used project is updated to its latest version. [ATTACHMENT filename='Updating a used project to its latest version via the Project Usages dialog.png']

###### Updating a used project to its latest version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To update a used project to its latest version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. Open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog, do one of the following:
  1. Select a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Change Version [ATTACHMENT filename='change_version_button.png'] button, then in its dropdown menu, click Switch to Latest Version .
  2. Right-click a used project, then in its shortcut menu, select the Change Version > Switch to Latest Version command. The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states ' Switch to <version number> '. [ATTACHMENT filename='Updating a used project to its latest version via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog.png']
3. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the Reset Changes for Selection command.
4. Do one of the following:
  1. To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the Apply button.
  2. To save changes and close the dialog, click the OK button.
  3. To discard changes and close the dialog, click the Cancel button.
5. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. The used project version is updated.

##### Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports

In addition to versions, used projects may also have multiple imports of the same project. For example, you might [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] a project that is used in the main project from the local repository to your file system, share it with a colleague who makes changes while you continue working on the main project, and then later [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] your colleague’s modified copy back into your local repository. In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the originally used project and the subsequent import of the same project. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing local projects' space='UAF2P'] page.

Although the main project continues to use the specific project (and its version) that was originally used, you can switch the used project to its different import if needed. Both the original and the import can be modified and saved as a new version, allowing you to update the used project to its latest version (as described in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] subsection).

You can view details about the used project (original or its imports) in the **Project Usages**and the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialogs:

- If the used project was created in the local repository, it is displayed only by indicating the project name and version ( Latest/Oldest Version ).
- If the used project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed using the following structure: <[<file_name>.mdszip imported @<date and time of the import>] <version (Latest/Oldest Version)>> .

To ensure the used project import and/or version information displayed in the Containment tree and the rest of the tool is up to date, press **F5**or click **View**> **Refresh** in the main menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Containment tree

The Containment tree only displays the project version information (Latest/Oldest). You can view more details about the import in the **Project Usages**and the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialogs.

To switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, expand the Used Projects node, then right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click Switch To.. . The Switch To menu opens.
2. In the Switch To menu, select the project you want to use. The used project is updated. [ATTACHMENT filename='Switching to a different instance of a used project via the Containment tree.png']

###### Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Project Usages dialog

To switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Project Usages dialog

1. Open the **Project Usages** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] and d o one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the **Change Version** [ATTACHMENT filename='change_version_button.png'] button , and in its dropdown menu, click **Switch To...** . The Switch To menu opens.
  2. In the dialog's project usages tree, right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Change Version >** **Switch To...******command. The Switch To menu opens.
2. In the Switch To menu, select the project you want to use.
3. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. The used project is updated.

###### Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. Open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog, do one of the following:
  1. S elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the **Change Version****[IMAGE alt='' src='']**button, then in its submenu, click **Switch To...** . The Switch To menu opens.
  2. Right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Change Version** command , then in its submenu, click **Switch To...** . The Switch To menu opens.
3. In the Switch To menu, select the project you want to use. The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states ' Switch to <project version> <project import> '. [ATTACHMENT filename='Switching to a different instance of a used project via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog.png']
4. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the Reset Changes for Selection command.
5. Do one of the following:
  1. To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the Apply button.
  2. To save changes and close the dialog, click the OK button.
  3. To discard changes and close the dialog, click the Cancel button.
6. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

##### Opening used projects

Projects can only use other projects in read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as a stand-alone. You can do this from within the main project in which the project is used by following the procedures listed below.

###### Opening a used project via the Containment tree

To open a used project via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, expand the Used Projects node.
2. Right-click a used project and click Open Latest Version . The used project is opened. [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening a used project via the Containment tree.png']

3. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. The used project is opened. [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog.png']

##### Removing used projects

Used projects are removed from the main project with all their references, including their used projects (indirectly used projects).

###### Removing a used project via the Containment tree

To remove a used project via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree of the main project, expand the Used Projects node.
2. Right-click a used project and select Remove .
3. When the question dialog opens, click Yes to confirm that you want to remove the used project. [ATTACHMENT filename='Removing a used project via the Containment tree.png']

###### Removing a used project via the Project Usages dialog

1. Open the **Project Usages** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] and d o one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the **Remove with References** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. In the dialog's project usages tree, right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Remove with References**command.
2. When the question dialog opens, click Yes to confirm that you want to remove the used project.
3. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

###### Removing a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To remove a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. Open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog, do one of the following:
  1. S elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the **Remove with References** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. Right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Remove with References** command . The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states ' Remove, delete references '. [ATTACHMENT filename='Removing a used project via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog.png']
3. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.

4. Do one of the following:

5. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

##### Indirectly used projects

The projects used in the main project may also use other projects, called indirectly used projects. When you use a project directly that may have other used projects, these indirectly used projects are loaded in the main project. Once loaded, the indirectly used projects use the same project version that is used in the directly used project. However, if the indirectly used project is later modified and you want this change to be reflected in the main project, you must update the project version in the directly used project, then update the version of the directly used project in the main project.

In the main project, you can see the indirectly used projects:

- Under the Containment tree's Used Projects node, displayed at the same node level as the project that uses it directly.
- The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog's Indirectly used projects category.
- The Project Usages dialog, nested under the project that uses it directly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using indirectly used projects directly

To use indirectly used projects directly

1. Open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
2. In the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog, do one of the following:
  1. S elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the **Use Project Directly** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. Right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Use Project Directly** command . The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states ' Use Project Directly '. [ATTACHMENT filename='Using indirectly used projects directly .png']
3. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the Reset Changes for Selection command.
4. Do one of the following:
  1. To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the Apply button.
  2. To save changes and close the dialog, click the OK button.
  3. To discard changes and close the dialog, click the Cancel button.
5. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

###### Opening indirectly used projects

To open indirectly used projects

See the procedures:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .

###### Removing indirectly used projects

Indirectly used projects can only be removed from the main project in the following cases:

- If you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .
- If you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] first, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='UAF2P'] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75724cc6-5683-49c3-bf35-d537469688f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Click here to expand the 'Managing local used projects' documentation for the 2026x version...</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is advisable to split large projects with loosely dependent parts into used projects. Project partitioning provides additional benefits, such as reusing model parts (e.g., libraries) across multiple projects and improving performance when working with very large models. The used projects are always added from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>; thus, the projects must first be added there to be used in other projects. Once added to the main project, you can view the used projects under the Containment tree's Used Projects node. </p><p>You can manage used projects via the Containment tree, <ac:link ac:anchor="The Project Usages dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>, and <ac:link ac:anchor="The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</p><p>There can be two kinds of used projects:</p><ul><li data-uuid="98e6c574-87e1-4ffe-981d-9a2e40d2a3c9"><span><em>Directly used projects</em>: projects used directly by the main project.<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="a5b0f501-dc6a-4847-86ca-685ff7fc3e1d"><span><em>Indirectly used projects</em>: projects used by other used projects. For more information, see the </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Indirectly used projects 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Indirectly used projects]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link><span> subsection.</span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Used Projects node in the Containment tree 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70675dfb-bd5d-49fc-a57e-ea602397d806"><ac:parameter ac:name="">The Project Usages dialog 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>The Project Usages dialog</h3><p>The Project Usages dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can <ac:link ac:anchor="Using a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Changing a used project version via the Project Usages dialog 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[update]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[open]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing a used project via the Project Usages dialog 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link> a used project, and <ac:link ac:anchor="Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>. Selecting a used project in the project usages tree displays the project's details (e.g., version, location) on the right side of the dialog.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="The Project Usages dialog 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a9e2cb2-b192-4f48-b246-4c799a85d61f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Opening the Project Usages dialog 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Opening the Project Usages dialog</h4><p>To open the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="8916dfc7-3e6c-47f0-8548-759b1fa5761a">Do one of the following:<ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>In the Containment tree, expand the <strong>Used Projects </strong>node and right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Options</strong>.<br />The <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78511087-c35a-4ff9-9c9e-0265172c99b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h3><p>The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can <ac:link ac:anchor="Using a project from the local repository via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use (and undo this command)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove (and undo this command)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link> a used project, and <ac:link ac:anchor="Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[switch between the original used project and its imports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link> of a used project. You can also review all the used projects' version information.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9c8e29d-9d4b-4d97-91cd-8bdbf90d935c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Some buttons in the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialog are either disabled or not recommended for usage (e.g., <strong>Project Usage Information</strong>, <strong>Notify on New Version </strong>column functionality</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">) as their functionality will be changed and/or removed in future releases.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edb5aa93-7feb-4261-9a1c-0c6054d86f3e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h4><p>To open the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="b23aaf09-c2c5-4faf-9dbd-3bac15ccbb05"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>. </li><li data-uuid="48e1cea3-4353-445b-bb3e-97772da72148">In the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Usages Configuration <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="usages_configurations_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>button. <br />The <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol><h3>Using projects from the local repository</h3><p>Used projects employ the sticky versions approach. Once a specific version of a project is used, the main project continues to use that exact version until you update it manually. This means that even if you modify a used project and save it as a new version, the main project will still use the previously added version. This prevents unexpected changes or damage to the main project. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6d6403b-461c-48bf-a780-7b77a1ed129e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A project must be stored in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before it can be used locally. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sticky_versions.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The main project uses only the specified versions of the used projects, despite the newer versions they may have. </h6><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abd49907-276f-4db9-8a54-a469e5715cf9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Using a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Using a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog</h4><p>To use a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="9f844472-54b5-4828-b00e-a9e29d58b6f2"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>. </li><li data-uuid="711bb0a1-657e-4c97-b32b-f87e2fceba8c">In the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Use Project <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="use_project_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>button. The <strong>Use Project</strong> dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="0e9eb0c0-3a4a-40c2-be77-99cdb04e9b96">In the <strong>Use Project</strong> dialog, select the project you want to use and click the <strong>Next </strong>button.</li><li data-uuid="a6a44139-f956-4408-8c09-8e14ff0e4da1">Select the project version you want to use and click the <strong>Finish</strong> button.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Using a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="38ab48e9-cb97-4d02-8853-418ccfe49fb0">Click the <strong>OK</strong> button to close the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog.<br />Used projects are displayed under the <strong>Used Projects</strong> node in the Containment tree of your model.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Used Projects node in the Containment tree 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e9a10ee-3dd3-4bf7-ba66-9691a3c7912b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Using a project from the local repository via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Using a project from the local repository via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h4><p>To use a project from the local repository via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ab6d32e7-7ecb-4348-881f-7c30272ba033"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>. </li><li data-uuid="8a322b3f-71e1-48a1-b829-e61b6f931fae">In the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog's toolbar, <span>click the <strong>Add</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button</span>, and in its dropdown menu, click <strong>Use Project</strong><span>. </span>The <strong>Use Project</strong> dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="a8cb0416-ceda-4ec0-b167-a0432c0773b8">In the <strong>Use Project</strong> dialog, select the project you want to use and click the <strong>Next </strong>button.</li><li data-uuid="875dad34-5a9a-441b-bd02-17917c5723c2">Select the project version you want to use and click the <strong>Finish</strong> button. The cell in the <strong>Action</strong> column for the added used project states '<strong>Add</strong>'.  <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Using a project from the local repository via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="2df9ea5f-b5f9-44cb-8eb9-63240aa14b28">(Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:<ol><li>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reset_changes_for_selection_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</li><li>Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection </strong>command.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="c1d3b0f4-e735-48b4-bbe2-7c928646d172">Do one of the following:<ol><li>To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the <strong>Apply </strong>button.</li><li>To save changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>OK </strong>button.</li><li>To discard changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>Cancel</strong> button.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="8f85444e-eb6d-497b-bf0a-82f1934f49fa">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.<br />Used projects are displayed under the <strong>Used Projects</strong> node in the Containment tree of your model.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cdd60b1c-4994-40b1-9345-88ed6b730325"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Changing used project versions 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Changing used project versions </h3><p>While a project may be used in the main project, it may be modified and saved to a new version, rendering its version used in the main project outdated. Therefore, in the main project, the used project is indicated with its version number, e.g., #1. You can choose to update the used project to a later version or continue using the older one. You can see the details about the used version in the Containment tree, the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog, and the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialog. The <strong>Edit Branches </strong>dialog displays all existing versions of the used project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06bc0bc9-b3b6-43fe-bdfb-e13cba65d352"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="66be102b-c280-4523-876e-8de29b28a22b"><strong>Refreshing version information display</strong>. To ensure the used project version information displayed in the Containment tree and the rest of the tool is up to date, press <strong>F5 </strong>or click <strong>View </strong>&gt; <strong>Refresh</strong> in the main menu.</li><li data-uuid="986629ad-314b-4025-9b4d-0c55286d5a1a"><strong>Updating directly used projects</strong>. Updating directly used projects does not update indirectly used projects if they are outdated in the projects that use them. In this case, indirectly used projects must be updated individually. However, if the indirectly used project is already updated in the directly used one, then the indirectly used project's version is updated in the main project once you update the directly used project's version.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Updating used projects to their latest versions 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></strong></h4><h4>Updating all used projects to their latest versions</h4><p>To update all used projects to their latest versions</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c0783e8a-4bdd-4022-9a6c-55c689624625"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>. </li><li data-uuid="b232faf8-6ace-4e2d-97dc-abab23c0733e">In the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Update Used Projects</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="update_used_projects_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The <strong>Update Used Projects </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="2dd74f33-a852-4475-877e-325c546e815f">In the <strong>Update Used Projects </strong>dialog, select the check box in the <strong>Update </strong>column for the used projects you want to update and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Updating all used projects to their latest versions 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="4aab2816-8bbe-4c6a-9992-69275fecad3e">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.<br />The used projects are updated to their latest versions. </li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="639d75e3-8e7b-422e-a29a-4ed01a8f8809"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Changing a used project version via the Containment tree 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Changing a used project version via the Containment tree</h4><p>To change a used project version via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="f2061742-6ad6-4501-8e02-d8a88cc1d4d9">In the Containment tree, expand the<span> </span><strong>Used Projects</strong><span> </span>node, then right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Change Version</strong>. The <strong>Edit Branches </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="cac7b8b1-d14a-4b90-8336-517e34f9cd2a">In the <strong>Edit Branches </strong>dialog, select the version you want to use, and click <strong>OK</strong>. <br />The used project version is changed. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Changing a used project version via the Containment tree 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5db3c197-4652-4b77-a82f-2259dfe91c74"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Changing a used project version via the Project Usages dialog 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Changing a used project version via the Project Usages dialog</h4><p>To change a used project version via the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="f0957746-da4d-4589-b3b1-2d34801ce078"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link> and d<span>o one of the following:</span><ol><li><span>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Change Version </strong></span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_version_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span> button</span>.</li><li><span>In the dialog's project usages tree, right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Change Version </strong>command.<br />The </span><strong>Edit Branches </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="cc2d4df3-8e5c-418f-8779-2635bcdf1efe">In the <strong>Edit Branches </strong>dialog, select the version you want to use, and click <strong>OK</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Changing a used project version via the Project Usages dialog 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="dabb2eb7-cf93-4d2a-8f18-b63fb8804f4c">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.<br />The used project version is changed. </li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c15a389e-78b5-42a5-a71d-3ad2dab66e64"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Changing a used project version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Changing a used project version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h4><p>To change a used project version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="0b1afe81-a9ad-45cb-8b11-889b9f614e5f"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="7d4cc5d2-b6a4-4ee7-bc41-797059dc5cce"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialog</span>, do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Select a used project <span>and in the dialog's toolbar</span>, click the</span> <strong>Change Version</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_version_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</li><li>Right-click a used project, then in its shortcut menu, select the <strong>Change Version </strong>command.<br /><span>The </span><strong>Edit Branches </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="a63e22cf-b418-49d2-8a01-1c13913b2ee8">In the <strong>Edit Branches </strong>dialog, select the version you want to use, and click OK. <br />The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states 'Switch to &lt;version number&gt; in &lt;project name&gt;'.  <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Changing a used project version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="cf6dfeec-ee3b-4d5d-9363-63e5888a9d9f">(Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:<ol><li>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reset_changes_for_selection_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</li><li>Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection </strong>command.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="abe3bade-05c5-4854-bd0e-b3f7ac71b68a">Do one of the following:<ol><li>To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the <strong>Apply </strong>button.</li><li>To save changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>OK </strong>button.</li><li>To discard changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>Cancel</strong> button.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="b88f3ae1-1d87-4e26-a4e2-713d6eac0dce">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.<br />The used project version is updated. </li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c761ff5-3b3f-4ac5-b00b-928442748751"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports </h3><p>In addition to versions, used projects may also have multiple imports of the same project. <span>For example, you might <ac:link ac:anchor="Exporting projects from the local repository as .mdszip files"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[export]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> a project that is used in the main project from the local repository to your file system, share it with a colleague who makes changes while you continue working on the main project, and then later <ac:link ac:anchor="Importing projects to the local repository"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> your colleague’s modified copy back into your local repository. In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the originally used project and the subsequent import of the same project. For more information, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Managing project imports in the local repository"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing local projects]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></p><p>Although the main project continues to use the specific project (and its version) that was originally used, you can switch the used project to its different import if needed. Both the original and the import can be modified and saved as a new version, allowing you to update the used project to its latest version (as described in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Changing used project versions 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Changing used project versions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link> subsection).</p><p>You can view details about the used project (original or its imports) in the <strong>Project Usages </strong>and the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialogs:</p><ul><li data-uuid="222f9bd8-8c6d-493a-9b50-cc6ffa2ee214">If the used project was created in the local repository, it is displayed as <em>trunk</em>.</li><li data-uuid="0881d071-e6c2-4ebd-8653-5f7589fcfe34">If the used project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed as <em>&lt;imported_file_name&gt;</em>.<ul><li>If there already is an import with the same name, a number is added:<span> </span><em>&lt;imported_file_name&lt;number&gt;&gt;</em>.</li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90219e0c-2b07-4bda-93e1-f8846b407d44"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To ensure the used project import and/or version information displayed in the Containment tree and the rest of the tool is up to date, press <strong>F5 </strong>or click <strong>View </strong>&gt; <strong>Refresh</strong> in the main menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4>Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports</h4><p>To switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports</p><hr /><p>See the following procedures:</p><ul><li data-uuid="b7cdc79f-fbfd-4b0b-a1d5-d02a9b241384"><ac:link ac:anchor="Changing a used project version via the Containment tree 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Changing a used project version via the Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="115bc33c-e2a8-4f3b-aa1c-f03befd05d14"><ac:link ac:anchor="Changing a used project version via the Project Usages dialog 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Changing a used project version via the Project Usages dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="3c7f7542-8cb0-4eb5-aeed-953a249cd4b1"><ac:link ac:anchor="Changing a used project version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Changing a used project version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link></li></ul><h3>Opening used projects </h3><p>Projects can only use other projects in read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as a stand-alone. You can do this from within the main project in which the project is used by following the procedures listed below.</p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b3a5efe8-3574-41bc-ac1c-bd7bb267dea3"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Opening a used project via the Containment tree 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Opening a used project via the Containment tree </h4><p>To open a used project via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="68a5b133-04d8-49ce-b0ff-32baee7dda05">In the Containment tree, expand the<span> </span><strong>Used Projects</strong><span> </span>node.</li><li data-uuid="69441cfb-67dd-4ed9-ab00-81081942a89e">Right-click a used project and select one of the following:<ol><li><strong>Open This Version</strong>.</li><li><strong>Open Latest Version</strong>.<br />The used project is opened.</li></ol></li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening a used project via the Containment tree 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1a97f56-cda1-4377-8034-fb3a21db8129"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog</h4><p>To open a used project via the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="3b83430a-5c7f-4d45-ac22-e2a59f4c16af"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="d1d7bf5c-07d7-4571-ba8e-46b1fc4cacc9">In the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog, select a used project, then on the right side section of the dialog, click the <strong>Open </strong>button.</li><li data-uuid="533b7455-e005-4fdb-b053-c03b073c78f4">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.<br />The used project is opened.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea82a32c-44d9-47f2-abb5-671c45e73c1e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Removing used projects 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Removing used projects</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1ca6091-06e4-4965-97c2-e47e8c36b0f5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Used projects are removed from the main project with all their references, including their used projects.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Removing a used project via the Containment tree</h4><p>To remove a used project via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="32c9d927-6f1c-430a-8b4e-66cce9e176d0">In the Containment tree of the main project, expand the<span> </span><strong>Used Projects</strong><span> </span>node.</li><li data-uuid="ace25df1-a46e-4f3d-91d4-cc9133ecdf4d">Right-click a used project and select <strong>Remove</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="0c727af4-3c39-45e4-9105-c54ee3b58dd6">When the question dialog opens, click <strong>Yes</strong> to confirm that you want to remove the used project.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Removing a used project via the Containment tree 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4150f111-1512-4b24-aaf3-68f18cadbe13"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Removing a used project via the Project Usages dialog 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Removing a used project via the Project Usages dialog </h4><p>To remove a used project via the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="984db426-a3fa-463a-a83b-dc32a3712082"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link> and d<span>o one of the following:</span><ol><li><span>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Remove with References</strong> <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_with_references_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> button</span><span>.</span></li><li><span>In the dialog's project usages tree, right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Remove with References </strong>command.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="f9655972-9c10-479f-b628-b6c62b0a7f19">When the question dialog opens, click<span> </span><strong>Yes</strong><span> </span>to confirm that you want to remove the used project.</li><li data-uuid="623775ce-5a9c-4c5c-9f0f-c30233df2a8c">Click the<span> </span><strong>OK<span> </span></strong>button to close the<span> </span><strong>Project Usages<span> </span></strong>dialog.</li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="708665c5-7fae-424c-bf0f-c747b4d8f401"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Removing a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Removing a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h4><p>To remove a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d342e84d-4b86-4541-bee3-df34693178f6"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="1d367b86-ba12-48fe-9b87-c4e92ffaff6d">In the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>S<span>elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Remove with References</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_with_references_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button</span><span>. </span></li><li>Right-click <span>a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Remove with References</strong> command</span><span>. <br /></span>The cell in the <strong>Action</strong> column for the removed used project states '<strong>Remove, delete references</strong>'.  <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Removing a used project via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="8904eaa5-1b80-4fb1-8452-9be56feef453">(Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:<ol><li>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reset_changes_for_selection_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</li><li>Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection </strong>command.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="c0d3773f-8a74-493a-8773-535c1cfddb85">Do one of the following:<ol><li>To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the <strong>Apply </strong>button.</li><li>To save changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>OK </strong>button.</li><li>To discard changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>Cancel</strong> button.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="fcb24fd0-3069-4e09-9192-63dea0924144">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f165223-92c9-40b9-a274-90ae6548a716"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Indirectly used projects 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Indirectly used projects</h3><p>The projects used in the main project may also use other projects, called indirectly used projects. When you use a project directly that may have other used projects, these indirectly used projects are loaded in the main project. Once loaded, the indirectly used projects use the same project version that is used in the directly used project. However, if the indirectly used project is later modified and you want this change to be reflected in the main project, you must update the project version in the directly used project, then update the version of the directly used project in the main project. </p><p>In the main project, you can see the indirectly used projects:</p><ul><li data-uuid="d58a7069-39b6-4440-99c1-0215cf62eef8">Under the Containment tree's <em>Used Projects</em> node, displayed at the same node level as the project that uses it directly.</li><li data-uuid="799ee506-c4b5-4e3c-add1-09c35971de32">The <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialog's <em>Indirectly used projects</em> category.</li><li data-uuid="59c50988-dc5a-40fa-9194-2aa97d8992da">The <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog, nested under the project that uses it directly.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Indirectly used projects 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7bc6280b-de84-4823-ac70-8417964229a5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Using indirectly used projects directly 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Using indirectly used projects directly </h4><p>To use indirectly used projects directly</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="4ac000d8-ab13-4d12-a71b-1203588a42f9"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="06ca2470-3782-49b8-b33e-fd6ae3188de7">In the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>S<span>elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Use Project Directly</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Use_Project_Directly_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button</span><span>. </span></li><li>Right-click <span>a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Use Project Directly</strong> command</span><span>. <br /></span>The cell in the <strong>Action</strong> column for the removed used project states '<strong>Use Project Directly</strong>'.  <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Using indirectly used projects directly 2026x.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="28bb48f8-75da-46be-9208-333111ac12f0">(Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:<ol><li>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reset_changes_for_selection_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</li><li>Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection </strong>command.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="5f8dade9-cd97-4cbb-bb71-753036a90e69">Do one of the following:<ol><li>To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the <strong>Apply </strong>button.</li><li>To save changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>OK </strong>button.</li><li>To discard changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>Cancel</strong> button.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="24d03548-48aa-4078-8faa-2c10aa754469">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.</li></ol><h4>Opening indirectly used projects</h4><p>To open indirectly used projects</p><hr /><p>See the procedures:</p><ul><li data-uuid="6b7e1aa7-c2d2-4613-8bb0-5d2245fb72c1"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening a used project via the Containment tree 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Opening a used project via the Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="59ac5cc5-7c23-4a52-8f4a-93e725b7de9d"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</li></ul><h4>Removing indirectly used projects</h4><p>Indirectly used projects can only be removed from the main project in the following cases:</p><ul><li data-uuid="52fc65e7-8275-42ec-9147-a8b2bdc77b75">If you <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing used projects 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove the directly used project that uses the project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>. </li><li data-uuid="9a413bdc-7d86-469c-974b-12cf3c1df4ba">If you <ac:link ac:anchor="Using indirectly used projects directly 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use the indirectly used project directly]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link> first, then <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing used projects 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove it as a directly used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0862e273-07e4-4631-b2c0-755e9d812bc0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2026x HotFix1 version</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This documentation page is for the 2026x HotFix1 version of the SysML v2 Plugin. If you are working with the 2026x version, expand the section above. However, we highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix1 version.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>It is advisable to split large projects with loosely dependent parts into used projects. Project partitioning provides additional benefits, such as reusing model parts (e.g., libraries) across multiple projects and improving performance when working with very large models. The used projects are always added from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Managing local projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>; thus, the projects must first be added there to be used in other projects. Once added to the main project, you can view the used projects under the Containment tree's Used Projects node. </p><p>You can manage used projects via the Containment tree, <ac:link ac:anchor="The Project Usages dialog"><ac:link-body>the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>, and <ac:link ac:anchor="The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ac:link-body>the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</p><p>There can be two kinds of used projects:</p><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><em>Directly used projects</em>: projects used directly by the main project.<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="8731e327-e94a-4ce8-a9bf-11d0b15b2241"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><em>Indirectly used projects</em>: projects used by other used projects. For more information, see the </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Indirectly used projects"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> subsection.</span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Used Projects node in the Containment tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>The Project Usages dialog</h3><p>The Project Usages dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can <ac:link ac:anchor="Using a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Updating a used project to its latest version via the Project Usages dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[update]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[open]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing a used project via the Project Usages dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link> a used project, and <ac:link ac:anchor="Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Project Usages dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>. Selecting a used project in the project usages tree displays the project's details (e.g., version, location) on the right side of the dialog.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="The Project Usages dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4>Opening the Project Usages dialog</h4><p>To open the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7e600da9-6479-4977-85a9-aa4216b9f102">Do one of the following:<ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>In the Containment tree, expand the <strong>Used Projects </strong>node and right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Options</strong>.<br />The <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol></li></ol><h3>The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h3><p>The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can <ac:link ac:anchor="Using a project from the local repository via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use (and undo this command)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Updating a used project to its latest version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[update]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove (and undo this command)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link> a used project, and <ac:link ac:anchor="Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[switch between the original used project and its imports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link> of a used project. You can also review the information about the version and the imports of all used projects.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="The Advanced Usages Configuration dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4>Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h4><p>To open the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="653adb75-bc64-445c-87c4-0acfe4338636"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>. </li><li data-uuid="ad169f26-2f7e-4642-a7e5-6da6e9653ca1">In the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Usages Configuration <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="usages_configurations_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>button. <br />The <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol><h3>Using projects from the local repository</h3><p>Used projects employ the sticky versions approach. Once a specific version of a project is used, the main project continues to use that exact version until you update it manually. This means that even if you modify a used project and save it as a new version, the main project will still use the previously added version. This prevents unexpected changes or damage to the main project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a0a9844e-3492-4f25-8c3e-2d0b775d2587"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A project must be stored in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Managing local projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before it can be used locally.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-subtle,#f87168);"><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sticky_versions.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The main project uses only the specified versions of the used projects, despite the newer versions they may have. </h6><h4>Using a project from the local repository via the Use Local Project command</h4><p>To use a project from the local repository via the Use Local Project command</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ee2e66fd-36ba-4099-9bf9-834aa11e3f0c">In the main menu, select <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Use Project </strong>&gt; <strong>Use Local Project</strong>. The <strong>Use Project</strong> dialog opens.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="77e67fd0-936a-4e83-8893-f0e346ec6731"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If there are no other projects in the local repository or all of them are already used in the open project, the <strong>Use Local Project </strong>command is disabled.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="f2b73152-4cbd-4b36-a7f6-4e3172caf712">In the <strong>Use Project</strong> dialog, select the project you want to use and click the <strong>Next </strong>button.</li><li data-uuid="00487bb7-abb7-45b1-98cd-ce9e23ac9df8">Select the project version you want to use and click the <strong>Finish</strong> button.<br />Used projects are displayed under the <strong>Used Projects</strong> node in the Containment tree of your model.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Used Projects node in the Containment tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h4>Using a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog</h4><p>To use a project from
````
