the local repository via the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="08db1b6b-6d51-4e4b-a86e-721d22ed06e7"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>. </li><li data-uuid="2c4c8e38-a5f3-4419-8f18-a2245b821d62">In the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Use Project <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="use_project_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>button. The <strong>Use Project</strong> dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="ab526153-2a5f-4f93-9184-529442c4d7a0">In the <strong>Use Project</strong> dialog, select the project you want to use and click the <strong>OK </strong>button.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Using a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="241af7f5-678b-459d-9b73-a7ca04496061">Click the <strong>OK</strong> button to close the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog.<br />Used projects are displayed under the <strong>Used Projects</strong> node in the Containment tree of your model.</li></ol><h4>Using a project from the local repository via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h4><p>To use a project from the local repository via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="9fe684c8-c14b-49ae-954c-a526685dd3ec"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the<strong> Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="76be2c26-3126-463d-906a-bc323d19f20b">In the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog's toolbar, <span>click the <strong>Add</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button</span>, and in its dropdown menu, click <strong>Use Project</strong><span>. </span>The <strong>Use Project</strong> dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="eaecb8f8-e0b1-4c30-9cdf-7753caa94c9c">In the <strong>Use Project</strong> dialog, select the project you want to use and click the <strong>OK </strong>button. The cell in the <strong>Action</strong> column for the added used project states '<strong>Add</strong>'.  <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Using a project from the local repository via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="592b5117-694d-40ad-a0e2-04afbf6b2655">(Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:<ol><li>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reset_changes_for_selection_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</li><li>Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection </strong>command.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="300e64ff-d3c5-4476-85f7-e7275111c0a3">Do one of the following:<ol><li>To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the <strong>Apply </strong>button.</li><li>To save changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>OK </strong>button.</li><li>To discard changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>Cancel</strong> button.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="8a57881b-ad94-4abe-a865-2cf8c501fa7e">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.</li></ol><h3>Updating used projects to their latest versions</h3><p>While a project may be used in the main project, it may be modified and saved to a new version, rendering its version used in the main project outdated. Therefore, in the main project, the used project may refer to two of its available versions: <strong>Older Version </strong>or <strong>Latest Version</strong>. You can choose to update the used project to its latest version or continue using the older one. You can see more details about the used version in the Containment tree, the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog, and the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialog.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1652fa9a-7164-42c1-a163-11275548d0b7"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="ea6dae6e-1241-437e-9755-81121c2aa916"><strong>Refreshing version information display</strong>. To ensure the used project version information displayed in the Containment tree and the rest of the tool is up to date, press <strong>F5 </strong>or click <strong>View </strong>&gt; <strong>Refresh</strong> in the main menu.</li><li data-uuid="4d03d5a4-028e-4cbf-89f5-86e01759ce95"><strong>Reverting used project version update</strong>. Once you update a used project to its latest version and save the project, reverting to an older version is <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c21f1c83-458c-4809-a027-88554ac59965">not possible.</ac:inline-comment-marker> However, if you do not save the main project after the update, you can close the main project without saving it to discard all changes, including the used project's version update. Thus, make sure the update is necessary to avoid potential model inconsistencies.</li><li data-uuid="67f2ed78-c68e-48cb-9096-7b95161395d9"><strong>Updating directly used projects</strong>. <ac:link ac:anchor="Indirectly used projects"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Updating directly used projects]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link> does not update indirectly used projects if they are outdated in the projects that use them. In this case, indirectly used projects must be updated individually. However, if the indirectly used project is already updated in the directly used one, then the indirectly used project's version is updated in the main project once you update the directly used project's version.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-icon-accent-lime,#6a9a23);"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Updating used projects to their latest versions.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></strong></span></p><h4>Updating all used projects to their latest versions</h4><p>To update all used projects to their latest versions</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="722ee114-ddee-452b-958a-86b9f7ebcec0"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>. </li><li data-uuid="954c7c92-6da9-4ffe-a96b-50ae6cf4eeff">In the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Update Used Projects</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="update_used_projects_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The <strong>Update Used Projects </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="6aaf82c3-ad73-48b2-8612-631c28bd5066">In the <strong>Update Used Projects </strong>dialog, select the check box in the <strong>Update </strong>column for the used projects you want to update and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Updating all used projects to their latest versions.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="d4fe78d1-a150-4605-8ff0-227dc74e5b00">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.<br />The used projects are updated to their latest versions. </li></ol><h4>Updating a used project to its latest version via the Containment tree</h4><p>To update a used project to its latest version via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="12e9917c-799d-4d4b-bd54-9162f73a7aae">In the Containment tree, expand the<span> </span><strong>Used Projects</strong><span> </span>node, then right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Switch to Latest Version</strong>.<br />The used project is updated to its latest version. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Updating a used project to its latest version via the Containment tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><h4>Updating a used project to its latest version via the Project Usages dialog</h4><p>To update a used project to its latest version via the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link> and d<span>o one of the following:</span><ol><li><span>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Change Version </strong></span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_version_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span> button</span>, and in its dropdown menu, click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Switch to Latest Version</strong>.</li><li><span>In the dialog's project usages tree, right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Change Version &gt; Switch to Latest Version</strong><strong> </strong>command.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="28398e39-b9e4-479f-8d6e-6624d22666c2">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.<br />The used project is updated to its latest version. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Updating a used project to its latest version via the Project Usages dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h4>Updating a used project to its latest version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h4><p>To update a used project to its latest version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="72c5afea-069e-4bb7-aa15-bcc8fcdc6a41"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="2f766927-1970-4b64-876b-2a737d57871a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialog</span>, do one of the following:</span><ol><li data-uuid="f6f3f2ec-e633-4825-af34-da3a2c4072be"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Select a used project <span>and in the dialog's toolbar</span>, click the</span> <strong>Change Version</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_version_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button, then in its dropdown menu, click <strong>Switch to Latest Version</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="86ccd267-da4d-4d63-8a50-e1e97a448123">Right-click a used project, then in its shortcut menu, select the <strong>Change Version &gt; Switch to Latest Version </strong>command.<br />The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states '<strong>Switch to &lt;version number&gt;</strong>'.  <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Updating a used project to its latest version via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="7026b736-3066-488a-be65-7af1079a9529">(Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:<ol><li>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reset_changes_for_selection_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</li><li>Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection </strong>command.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="c8caea2f-a47e-4c4e-8761-563226cde88c">Do one of the following:<ol><li>To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the <strong>Apply </strong>button.</li><li>To save changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>OK </strong>button.</li><li>To discard changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>Cancel</strong> button.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="5a7c1339-7724-4150-927d-d16794b7b706">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.<br />The used project version is updated. </li></ol><h3>Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports </h3><p>In addition to versions, used projects may also have multiple imports of the same project. <span>For example, you might <ac:link ac:anchor="Exporting projects from the local repository as .mdszip files"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Managing local projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[export]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> a project that is used in the main project from the local repository to your file system, share it with a colleague who makes changes while you continue working on the main project, and then later <ac:link ac:anchor="Importing projects to the local repository"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Managing local projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> your colleague’s modified copy back into your local repository. In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the originally used project and the subsequent import of the same project. For more information, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Managing project imports in the local repository"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Managing local projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing local projects]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></p><p>Although the main project continues to use the specific project (and its version) that was originally used, you can switch the used project to its different import if needed. Both the original and the import can be modified and saved as a new version, allowing you to update the used project to its latest version (as described in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Updating used projects to their latest versions"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link> subsection).</p><p>You can view details about the used project (original or its imports) in the <strong>Project Usages </strong>and the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialogs:</p><ul><li data-uuid="caa73de5-cecb-4e46-a0d1-66b9d2c4ad32">If the used project was created in the local repository, it is displayed only by indicating the project name and version (<em>Latest/Oldest Version</em>). </li><li data-uuid="d21cca26-d712-4a15-97bf-7522917126ab">If the used project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed using the following structure: <em>&lt;[&lt;file_name&gt;.mdszip imported @&lt;date and time of the import&gt;] &lt;version (Latest/Oldest Version)&gt;&gt;</em>.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dbb3f07b-06c6-4c63-a5bd-125968cf30b3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To ensure the used project import and/or version information displayed in the Containment tree and the rest of the tool is up to date, press <strong>F5 </strong>or click <strong>View </strong>&gt; <strong>Refresh</strong> in the main menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Switching to a different revision of a used project.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4>Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Containment tree </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a60eb6c-e308-4df6-b7c3-a12ef1425039"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Containment tree only displays the project version information (Latest/Oldest). You can view more details about the import in the <strong>Project Usages </strong>and the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialogs.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="b76f6ccf-a757-4668-9535-ba36a94b598d">In the Containment tree, expand the<span> </span><strong>Used Projects</strong><span> </span>node, then right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Switch To..</strong>. The <strong>Switch To </strong>menu opens.</li><li data-uuid="fafab55b-96d1-4435-92ca-062a7dd90d51">In the <strong>Switch To </strong>menu, select the project you want to use.<br />The used project is updated.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Switching to a different instance of a used project via the Containment tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h4>Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Project Usages dialog</h4><p>To switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="734fa276-6ddc-4c47-9b62-6fc7320b493f"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link> and d<span>o one of the following:</span><ol><li><span>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Change Version </strong></span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_version_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span> button</span>, and in its dropdown menu, click <span><strong>Switch To...</strong></span><span>. The </span><strong>Switch To </strong>menu opens.</li><li><span>In the dialog's project usages tree, right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Change Version &gt;</strong> <strong>Switch To...</strong><strong> </strong>command. </span><span>The </span><strong>Switch To </strong>menu opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="4c5b9af8-018f-438f-8672-2c9f61bc6019">In the <strong>Switch To </strong>menu, select the project you want to use.</li><li data-uuid="de1778a0-3463-44b8-a635-d01a97a9159f">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.<br />The used project is updated.</li></ol><h4>Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h4><p>To switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a8d8e603-935b-4320-a5b1-b514b5d93225"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="60409c82-500f-4cca-8fd7-f7bfcdf1d924">In the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>S<span>elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Change Version</strong><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_version_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>button, then in its submenu, click <strong>Switch To...</strong></span><span>. The </span><strong>Switch To </strong>menu opens.</li><li>Right-click <span>a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Change Version</strong> command</span><span>, then in its submenu, click <strong>Switch To...</strong></span><span>. </span><span>The </span><strong>Switch To </strong>menu opens.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong>Switch To </strong>menu, select the project you want to use.<span><br /></span>The cell in the <strong>Action</strong> column for the removed used project states '<strong>Switch to &lt;project version&gt; &lt;project import&gt;</strong>'.  <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Switching to a different instance of a used project via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="4da4a7b6-1046-4bd6-bf11-5841b58dbe92">(Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:<ol><li>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reset_changes_for_selection_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</li><li>Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection </strong>command.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="e2d79801-9308-4d57-9ebd-4258e43dc6ac">Do one of the following:<ol><li>To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the <strong>Apply </strong>button.</li><li>To save changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>OK </strong>button.</li><li>To discard changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>Cancel</strong> button.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="00efa913-4221-4fc8-bae0-425900dddf33">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.</li></ol><h3>Opening used projects </h3><p>Projects can only use other projects in read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as a stand-alone. You can do this from within the main project in which the project is used by following the procedures listed below.</p><h4>Opening a used project via the Containment tree</h4><p>To open a used project via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="156ce0df-3f70-495d-a881-460fe041c520">In the Containment tree, expand the<span> </span><strong>Used Projects</strong><span> </span>node.</li><li data-uuid="7d7570fb-70f2-4fce-9b6c-ca3ef2bcd1b0">Right-click a used project and click <strong>Open Latest Version</strong>.<br />The used project is opened.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening a used project via the Containment tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h4>Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog</h4><p>To open a used project via the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="2fe95945-9d51-417b-b8e3-5a09e832e257"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="a4567379-e0c9-413b-9dd1-b9c067a932a0">In the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog, select a used project, then on the right side section of the dialog, click the <strong>Open Latest Version</strong> button.</li><li data-uuid="77d75c32-a4ef-419d-9d11-c6a3717e6692">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.<br />The used project is opened.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h3>Removing used projects </h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a61c8aed-3286-427f-8e75-32cfe7a50dc4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Used projects are removed from the main project with all their references, including their used projects (indirectly used projects).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Removing a used project via the Containment tree</h4><p>To remove a used project via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="867e76e6-7294-4b24-b809-7b89c4001264">In the Containment tree of the main project, expand the<span> </span><strong>Used Projects</strong><span> </span>node.</li><li data-uuid="1447d339-5ded-418d-9b19-8191b2e17737">Right-click a used project and select <strong>Remove</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="41b80938-f503-489c-88a5-de8949e1d288">When the question dialog opens, click <strong>Yes</strong> to confirm that you want to remove the used project.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Removing a used project via the Containment tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h4>Removing a used project via the Project Usages dialog</h4><p>To remove a used project via the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d209f8a2-3e0b-48da-bc23-1dd50f554cb2"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link> and d<span>o one of the following:</span><ol><li data-uuid="106e78fb-ece0-4ff9-912f-c207d1d37f5a"><span>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Remove with References</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_with_references_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button</span><span>.</span></li><li data-uuid="1779d52c-3227-40e5-8f06-cef0ce82587f"><span>In the dialog's project usages tree, right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Remove with References </strong>command.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="62c9fece-8505-4ab2-a581-150c85aa1ecb">When the question dialog opens, click <strong>Yes</strong> to confirm that you want to remove the used project.</li><li data-uuid="f8addf89-bda8-481f-9082-125270cdb33b">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.</li></ol><h4>Removing a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h4><p>To remove a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="6bcbb9a1-38a2-408b-aade-2d5997e4436b"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="ca3bf304-e824-4dcc-98b9-82319532184e">In the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>S<span>elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Remove with References</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_with_references_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button</span><span>. </span></li><li>Right-click <span>a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Remove with References</strong> command</span><span>. <br /></span>The cell in the <strong>Action</strong> column for the removed used project states '<strong>Remove, delete references</strong>'.  <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Removing a used project via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="add3b93c-e1bd-49db-8160-65425bd94ea9">(Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:<ol><li>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reset_changes_for_selection_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</li><li>Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection </strong>command.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="892e4856-6487-4bf3-823c-49fc2f9909e6">Do one of the following:<ol><li>To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the <strong>Apply </strong>button.</li><li>To save changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>OK </strong>button.</li><li>To discard changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>Cancel</strong> button.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="d181e4fa-213a-4cce-b511-5bf523104c4e">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.</li></ol><h3>Indirectly used projects</h3><p>The projects used in the main project may also use other projects, called indirectly used projects. When you use a project directly that may have other used projects, these indirectly used projects are loaded in the main project. Once loaded, the indirectly used projects use the same project version that is used in the directly used project. However, if the indirectly used project is later modified and you want this change to be reflected in the main project, you must update the project version in the directly used project, then update the version of the directly used project in the main project. </p><p>In the main project, you can see the indirectly used projects:</p><ul><li data-uuid="b3ad5356-186f-48c7-be21-eb0dbee5f415">Under the Containment tree's <em>Used Projects</em> node, displayed at the same node level as the project that uses it directly.</li><li data-uuid="06f217d0-7685-4ef3-af94-5f85a4f2c1fa">The <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialog's <em>Indirectly used projects</em> category.</li><li data-uuid="048379a2-36f4-41a6-81b0-5ee15a5e490a">The <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog, nested under the project that uses it directly.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Indirectly used projects.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4>Using indirectly used projects directly </h4><p>To use indirectly used projects directly</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="91d70d1c-0e14-4149-81e3-05929085ce2a"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="8766157e-f180-427a-a681-709af897e2f9">In the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>S<span>elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Use Project Directly</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Use_Project_Directly_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button</span><span>. </span></li><li>Right-click <span>a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Use Project Directly</strong> command</span><span>. <br /></span>The cell in the <strong>Action</strong> column for the removed used project states '<strong>Use Project Directly</strong>'.  <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Using indirectly used projects directly .png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="01f66e90-a866-4f65-8957-bfc60e6c4b9f">(Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:<ol><li>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reset_changes_for_selection_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</li><li>Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection </strong>command.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="12b8c0ab-b953-4da7-a788-012488eb2b4c">Do one of the following:<ol><li>To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the <strong>Apply </strong>button.</li><li>To save changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>OK </strong>button.</li><li>To discard changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>Cancel</strong> button.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="92d39831-28bf-4f66-a367-959279e194fa">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.</li></ol><h4>Opening indirectly used projects</h4><p>To open indirectly used projects</p><hr /><p>See the procedures:</p><ul><li data-uuid="4f88ecf3-57ab-420d-bc35-065658b75c4b"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening a used project via the Containment tree"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="6f951f66-2d40-4927-97c2-992c5ce62996"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</li></ul><h4>Removing indirectly used projects</h4><p>Indirectly used projects can only be removed from the main project in the following cases:</p><ul><li data-uuid="1e507fba-8f0b-43e7-ad0f-8c802a912389">If you <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing used projects "><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove the directly used project that uses the project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>. </li><li data-uuid="36822e57-44db-45a6-ac59-dfc323358138">If you <ac:link ac:anchor="Using indirectly used projects directly "><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use the indirectly used project directly]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link> first, then <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing used projects "><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove it as a directly used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /></ac:link>.</li></ul><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093129 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00095: (2026x) Managing Textual Editor

- page_id: `298093129`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093129/2026x+Managing+Textual+Editor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-11T09:13:45.523+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model management > (2026x) Working with Textual Editor
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page outlines the basic procedures for working with the Textual Editor and the textual notation.

For SysML v2 Textual Editor-related environment options, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Environment options' space='UAF2P'] page.

##### Managing the Textual Editor

###### Opening the Textual Editor

To open the Textual Editor

- In the Containment tree, right-click an element. In the shortcut menu, under Textual Editor , click Open .
- On a view , right-click an element symbol. In the shortcut menu, under Textual Editor , click Open .
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular views' space='UAF2P'] , click the **Open in Textual Editor** button.

###### Saving changes in the Textual Editor

To save changes in the Textual Editor

Do one of the following:

- In the Textual Editor's toolbar, click the Synchronize button [ATTACHMENT filename='sync_button.png'] .
- Use the Alt+S shortcut keys. You must synchronize the textual notation before saving your project. Otherwise, the project will be saved without the changes made via the Textual Editor. If the **Synchronize model with errors** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Environment options' space='UAF2P'] is set to:*true*(default), an informational message is displayed informing you the notation contains errors. The notation cannot be synchronized. Fix the errors and synchronize the changes again.*false*, the notation is synchronized as it is. This may lead to an incorrect model.

###### Updating changes in the Textual EditorUpdating changes in the Textual Editor

Making changes both in the textual notation and elsewhere in the model with the editor tab still open results in concurrent editing, which may lead to data loss. If you do make changes in the model while the Textual Editor is open, an informational message is displayed requesting you to refresh the textual notation displayed in the Textual Editor.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this case, there are two options:

- Click the **Refresh** button in the message. This updates the textual notation with the changes made elsewhere in the model, removing any changes made in the Textual Editor that were not previously saved.
- Click the **Synchronize** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button in the editor toolbar. This updates the model with changes made in the textual notation, removing any changes made elsewhere in the model after making modifications in the Textual Editor. You can detect previously unsaved changes made in the Textual Editor that will be lost after clicking the **Refresh**button by looking for elements that do not have display IDs in the textual notation.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Commenting in the Textual Editor

- There can be two types of comments:
  - A line note.
    - This type of comment is for the Textual Editor only; it does not create a comment element in your model.
    - Two ways to write it:
      - Write it using double forward slashes followed by the text you want to leave in the textual notation.
      - Place the cursor on the line you want to comment out and press Ctrl+/ on the keyboard.
  - A comment element.
    - This type of comment creates a comment element in your model.
    - For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Annotations' space='UAF2P'] page.
- By default, comments are indicated in #248000 color, differentiating them from other notational text. // May require revision - a comment that exists only in the Textual Editor 
// the following two options are equivalent; both create a *comment*element with the 'May require revision' text: 
/* May require revision */ 
comment/* May require revision */

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">This page outlines the basic procedures for working with the Textual Editor and the textual notation.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d46203b2-e0ed-492e-a688-bf954ea2c4e0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For SysML v2 Textual Editor-related environment options, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Environment options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Environment options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Managing the Textual Editor</h3><h4>Opening the Textual Editor</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To open the Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ul><li>In the Containment tree, right-click an element. In the shortcut menu, under <strong>Textual Editor</strong>, click <strong>Open</strong>.</li><li>On a <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">view</span>, right-click an element symbol. In the shortcut menu, under <strong>Textual Editor</strong>, click <strong>Open</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="f2391800-e740-40fc-a34c-fee8a12f2503">In the <ac:link ac:anchor="Tabular view toolbar buttons"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[tabular view's toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Open in Textual Editor</strong> button.</span> </li></ul><h4>Saving changes in the Textual Editor</h4><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To save changes in the Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>In the Textual Editor's toolbar, click the <strong>Synchronize</strong> button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sync_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Textual Editor" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Use the Alt+S shortcut keys.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b42913d8-f543-4f10-9714-2b153936282c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You must synchronize the textual notation before saving your project. Otherwise, the project will be saved without the changes made via the Textual Editor.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="31055868-951f-455e-8f4b-5708ab064c2d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Synchronize model with errors</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Environment options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[environment option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is set to:</p><ul><li><em>true </em>(default), an informational message is displayed informing you the notation contains errors. The notation cannot be synchronized. Fix the errors and synchronize the changes again.</li><li><em>false</em>, the notation is synchronized as it is. This may lead to an incorrect model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f536a4a9-4efd-4978-9d8b-60f50c40df2d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Updating changes in the Textual Editor</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Updating changes in the Textual Editor</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Making changes both in the textual notation and elsewhere in the model with the editor tab still open results in concurrent editing, which may lead to data loss. If you do make changes in the model while the Textual Editor is <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cb2491d2-77c3-4698-9763-79f22cae5b7c">open</ac:inline-comment-marker>, an informational message is displayed requesting you to refresh the textual notation displayed in the Textual Editor.<br /></span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="outdated_textual_notation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Textual Editor" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In this case, there are two options:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button in the message. This updates the textual notation with the changes made elsewhere in the model, removing any changes made in the Textual Editor that were not previously saved.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong></span> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sync_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Textual Editor" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">button in the editor toolbar. This updates the model with changes made in the textual notation, removing any changes made elsewhere in the model after making modifications in the Textual Editor.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="642d8e21-4e29-40ca-82ea-4c2e305715c3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can detect previously unsaved changes made in the Textual Editor that will be lost after clicking the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Refresh</strong> </span>button by looking for elements that do not have display IDs in the textual notation.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_IDs_in_textual_notation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Textual Editor" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h4>Commenting in the Textual Editor</h4><ul><li>There can be two types of comments:<ul><li>A line note.<ul><li>This type of comment is for the Textual Editor only; it does not create a <em>comment </em>element in your model.</li><li>Two ways to write it:<ul><li>Write it using double forward slashes followed by the text you want to leave in the textual notation.</li><li>Place the cursor on the line you want to comment out and press Ctrl+/ on the keyboard.</li></ul></li></ul></li><li>A comment element. <ul><li>This type of comment creates a <em>comment </em>element in your model.</li><li>For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Annotations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Annotations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">By default, comments are indicated in <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-lime,#4c6b1f);">#248000</span> color, differentiating them from other notational text.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="137005f8-340b-44e5-b4fe-4e630c56f533"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// May require revision - a comment that exists only in the Textual Editor <br /></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the following two options are equivalent; both create a <em>comment </em>element with the 'May require revision' text:<br /></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* May require revision */</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">comment</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* May require revision */</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093147 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00096: (2026x) Managing textual notation

- page_id: `298093147`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093147/2026x+Managing+textual+notation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:35:02.388+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model management > (2026x) Working with Textual Editor
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The sections on this page provide the basic information for writing the textual notation using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Textual Editor' space='UAF2P'].

##### Element declaration

- An element is declared with (1) a keyword indicating the element kind, followed by (2) the element name, and then either (3) curly braces for specifying the element's body or a semicolon to indicate the end of the element's specification. part flashlight;// keyword 'part', followed by the element name, and a semicolon, indicating the end of the element specification. 
part 'LED Flashlight' {// opening curly braces for specifying the declared element's body 
 part bulb;// element's body 
}// closing curly braces indicating the end of the element specification. 
part; // declared element without a name

##### Keywords

- Keywords indicate element kind ( part def, part, flow, item , etc.), relationships ( subsets, specializes, redefines ), or additional properties, such as directions ( in, out, to, from , etc.).
- Keywords are always typed in lowercase.
- Special textual symbols may replace specialization keywords. See the subpages under the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] page.
- By default, keywords are indicated in #1D0FFF color, differentiating them from other notational text.
- Multiple keywords may be specified in a defined order: (1) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifiers' space='UAF2P'] (direction, derived , abstract , constant , ref ), (2) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Occurrences' space='UAF2P'] , (3) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Occurrences' space='UAF2P'] (snapshot, timeslice), (4) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Metadata' space='UAF2P'] , (5) element metaclass, (6) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Element names' space='UAF2P'] , (7) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Multiplicity' space='UAF2P'] , (8) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] . individualtimeslicepartmyCardefinedbyCar;// individual, portion kind (timeslice), element metaclass (part), element name (myCar), specialization (defined by Car).
- For all element keyword examples, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model composition' space='UAF2P'] section.

##### Naming

- Unrestricted element names that include whitespace, special characters, or that correspond to keywords, must be encapsulated with single quotation marks.
  - Press the quotation mark (or Shift+quotation mark) key to encapsulate a text selection with single or double quotation marks.
- Basic element names without special characters or whitespace do not have to be encapsulated. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Element names' space='UAF2P'] page. part 'LED Flashlight' { // unrestricted element name that includes whitespace 
 part bulb; //basic element name without whitespace 
}

##### Name resolution

Name resolution allows you to target an element by its name, which can be done in the following ways:

- Feature chaining
  - A feature chain allows you to target a specific usage element located within the scope of another usage element, as long as the targeting and the target elements are within the scope of the same parent element.
  - A feature chain can only chain usage elements.
  - A feature chain is specified as a sequence of two or more usage element names separated by dot symbols. [ATTACHMENT filename='feature_chaining.png']
- Qualified name
  - A qualified name identifies an element qualified by the namespace relative to which the element name is to be resolved.
  - A qualified name can be used to target any element (definitions, usages, packages).
  - A qualified name is specified as a sequence of segments that indicate namespaces, which are separated by the :: symbol. [ATTACHMENT filename='qualified_name.png']

##### Textual notation errors

If a portion of the textual notation is incompatible or cannot be rendered, error messages may appear in place of the invalid content. Although rare, this may occur when importing a non-compliant model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093481 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00097: (2026x) Metadata

- page_id: `298093481`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093481/2026x+Metadata
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:49:12.107+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Common elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Metadata allows extending model elements by adding various attributes, semantics, and notation. It can be used to customize various models for specific domains or purposes.Metadata can be applied to any element. Metadata usage annotates its containing element; otherwise, an annotation relationship can be specified using the *about*syntax.

#### INFO: Semantic vs Non-Semantic Metadata

Semantic vs Non-Semantic Metadata

Semantic metadata refers to a metadata definition element that specializes the *SemanticMetadata* metaclass (e.g., *<moe> MeasureOfEffectiveness*, *<derive> DerivedRequirementMetadata*, etc.). There are several key differences in how elements are displayed in the Containment tree, Specification panel, symbols on views, and any other UI instances, depending on the kind of metadata applied to them:

If an element has at least one semantic metadata applied to it:

- The element's metaclass keyword (e.g., part ) is not displayed.
- The applied semantic metadata name is displayed with the # sign within guillemets « ». The def keyword is appended for definition elements. [ATTACHMENT filename='semantic_metadata.png']

If an element has only non-semantic metadata applied to it:

- The applied semantic metadata name is not displayed; thus, the element representation remains essentially unchanged. The element only displays metadata-related information in the metadata compartment and a metadata annotation. [ATTACHMENT filename='non-semantic_metadata.png']

##### Creating metadata definition elements

###### Creating metadata definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create metadata definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the metadata def keyword .
2. Name the element.
3. Specify the metadata attributes in the element body.
4. Click the **Synchronize**button.

metadata defApproval{attributeapproved:Boolean; 
attributeapprover:String; 
}

###### Creating metadata definition elements via the Create Element command

To create metadata definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select the metadata def element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the metadata attributes.

###### Creating metadata definition elements via the view palette

To create metadata definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Items/Parts/Attributes group, click the **metadata** def button to create a metadata definition element.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the element.
3. Specify the metadata attributes.

##### Applying metadata to an element

###### Applying metadata with attributes to an element via the Textual Editor

To apply metadata with attributes to an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element for which you want to apply metadata, do one of the following:
  1. For unnamed metadata usage, do one of the following:
    1. Declare the @ symbol followed by the metadata definition element name.
    2. Declare the keyword metadata followed by the metadata definition name. The notational difference between using the textual symbol @ or the keyword *metadata* depends on the **Display Metadata as Keywords** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Environment options' space='UAF2P']. By default, it is set to *false*, thus the notational representation of metadata in the textual notation is set to the textual symbol @. Even if you use a different notation, once the Textual Editor is reopened, the notational representation is changed to the one corresponding to the environment option. Set the environment option to *true* to use the keyword *metadata*notational representation instead.
  2. For a named metadata usage element:
    1. Declare the @ symbol followed by the element name, and then define it by the metadata definition.
    2. Declare the keyword metadata, specify the element name, and then define it by the metadata definition.
2. Specify the metadata attribute values in the element body.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. // unnamed metadata usage defined by metadata definition:partsystem{@Approval{ // the at sign @ symbol followed by the metadata definition element name (step 1.a.i)approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
}/* or */ 
metadataApproval{ // the keyword *metadata* followed by the metadata definition name (step 1.a.ii) 
approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
 } 
}// named metadata usage defined by metadata definition:partsystem{@approvalSystem:Approval{ ****// the at sign @ symbol followed by the metadata usage name, defined by the metadata definition (step 1.a.i) 
approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
}metadataapprovalSystem:Approval{// the keyword *metadata* followed by the metadata usage name, defined by metadata definition (step 1.b.ii) 
approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
} 
}

###### Applying metadata without attributes to an element via the Textual Editor

Using the following notation applies the metadata to an element without the possibility of specifying the metadata attributes.

To apply metadata without attributes to an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, prior to the element keyword of the element you want to apply the metadata to, type the hash sign # followed by the metadata definition element name.
2. Click the **Synchronize**button. #Approvalpartsystem;

###### Applying metadata to an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To apply metadata to an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view using

- Drag and drop a metadata element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P'] on an element symbol executing the Apply Metadata command.

###### Applying annotation metadata to an element via the Textual Editor

For other element annotation options, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Annotations' space='UAF2P'] page.

To apply annotation metadata to an element via the Textual Editor

Whether you want to apply annotation metadata with or without attributes, the overall procedure is generally the same as for:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Metadata' space='UAF2P']

However, in this case:

- The element to which the annotation metadata is applied must be declared separately.
- The applied metadata keyword is followed by the keyword about and the name of the element to which the annotation metadata is to be applied.

partsystem;// element declaration

@Approvalaboutsystem{// annotation metadata applied to the element 'system' using the 'about' syntax 
approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
}

/* or */

metadataApprovalaboutsystem{// annotation metadata applied to the element 'system' using the 'about' syntax 
approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Metadata allows extending model elements by adding various attributes, semantics, and notation. It can be used to customize various models for specific domains or purposes. </span>Metadata can be applied to any element. Metadata usage annotates its containing element; otherwise, an annotation relationship can be specified using the <em>about </em>syntax.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de96df3d-418f-45d3-829e-6d3410c47ed8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Semantic vs Non-Semantic Metadata</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Semantic metadata refers to a metadata definition element that specializes the <em>SemanticMetadata</em> metaclass (e.g., <em>&lt;moe&gt; MeasureOfEffectiveness</em>, <em>&lt;derive&gt; DerivedRequirementMetadata</em>, etc.). There are several key differences in how elements are displayed in the Containment tree, Specification panel, symbols on views, and any other UI instances, depending on the kind of metadata applied to them:</p><p>If an element has at least one semantic metadata applied to it:</p><ul><li data-uuid="2e1486f6-00db-42ed-b5f0-23796941864b">The element's metaclass keyword (e.g., <em>part</em>) is not displayed.</li><li data-uuid="f728d90b-2ca2-4c7d-a87e-414d6176930c">The applied semantic metadata name is displayed with the # sign within guillemets « ». The <em>def</em> keyword is appended for definition elements. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="semantic_metadata.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Metadata" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p>If an element has only non-semantic metadata applied to it:</p><ul><li data-uuid="302b4ae8-124e-422b-a39b-ba5606388108">The applied semantic metadata name is not displayed; thus, the element representation remains essentially unchanged. The element only displays metadata-related information in the metadata compartment and a metadata annotation. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="non-semantic_metadata.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Metadata" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating metadata definition elements</h3><h4>Creating metadata definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create metadata definition elements via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the <em>metadata def </em>keyword<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Specify the metadata attributes in the element body.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="802b05e2-76b4-4c33-bb2a-fa228b066a7c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>metad<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="86d4f0de-0443-4c67-ba61-6a54bf328e39">ata def</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Approval</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">{<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Boolean</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">approver</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">String</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating metadata definition elements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create metadata definition elements via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* or */</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Approval</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">about</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">system</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// annotation metadata applied to the element 'system' using the 'about' syntax</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">approved</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">approver</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;John Smith&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094198 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00098: (2026x) Migration from SysML v1 to SysML v2

- page_id: `298094198`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094198/2026x+Migration+from+SysML+v1+to+SysML+v2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T14:34:06.923+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration plugin to enable model transformation features, allowing you to transform SysML v1 models to SysML v2.

#### NOTE: Prerequisite

Prerequisite

The SysML v1 to v2 Migration plugin, which is available in the Resource Manager via the *<tool_installation_directory>\data\resourcemanager\distribution\core.rdzip* file, must be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Installation and system requirements' space='UAF2P'] in the modeling tool.

##### Migrating SysML v1 to v2

The migration of SysML v1 models to SysML v2 is done by exporting a SysML v1 project to a local SysML v2 project.

To export a SysML v1 project to a local SysML v2 project file

- In the main menu, click File > Export To > SysML v2 Model . Specify the project name and location in the Save window and click Save . The exported project is opened in the modeling tool. Migration statusIf failures occur during the migration, a migration status file *MigrationReportOf<projectName>*.*xlsx* is generated. The link to the file is displayed in the Notification window. The file contains two sheets: 'Not Migrated Elements' and 'Not Migrated Element Properties', listing failed element and property migrations, respectively.

##### SysML v1 to v2 element migration map

#### WARNING: SysML v1 to v2 element migration - a work in progress

SysML v1 to v2 element migration - a work in progress

Currently, only partial SysML v1 element migration is supported by the modeling tool, as SysML v1 to v2 element migration is a work in progress, covering about 20% of the metamodel. Diagram migration is currently not covered.

See the table below for elements covered by the migration.

| SysML v1 input | Result in the SysML v2 model |
| --- | --- |
| Block | Part Definition |
| Package | Package |
| Comment | Comment |
| Generalization | Subclassification |
| Interface Block | Interface Definition |
| Proxy Port | Port Usage |
| Port | Port Usage |
| ~Interface Block | Conjugated Port Definition |
| Signal | Item Definition |
| Flow Property | Item Usage |
| Part Property | Part Usage |
| Value Property | Attribute Usage |
| Constraint Property | Assert Constraint Usage |
| Reference Property | Attribute Usage |
| Value Type | Attribute Definition |
| Connector | Connection Usage / Interface Usage |
| Connector End | Reference Usage (IsEnd=true) |
| Requirement | Requirement Usage |
| Enumeration | Enumeration Definition |
| Enumeration Literal | Enumeration Usage |
| Dependency | Dependency |
| Use case | Use Case Definition |
| Include | Include Use Case Usage |
| Extend | No alternative match |
| ExtensionPoint | No alternative match |
| Actor | Part Usage owned via Actor Membership |
| Activity | Action Definition |
| Call Behavior Action | Action Usage |
| Structured Activity Node | Action Usage |
| Activity Node | abstract |
| Input Pin | Reference Usage (Direction=in) |
| Output Pin | Reference Usage (Direction=out) |
| Action Input Pin | Reference Usage (Direction=in) |
| Pin | abstract |
| Value Pin | Reference Usage (Direction=in) |
| Fork Node | Fork Node |
| Join Node | Join Node |
| Merge Node | Merge Node |
| Decision Node | Decision Node |
| Parameter | Reference Usage |
| Behavior | abstract |
| Object Flow | Succession Flow Usage / Flow Usage |
| Object Node | abstract |
| Control Flow | Succession Usage / Transition Usage |
| Activity Edge | abstract |
| Transition | Transition Usage |
| State | State Usage |
| State Machine | State Definition |
| Region | State Usage |
| Trigger | Accept Action Usage |
| Central Buffer Node | Action Usage |
| Activity Parameter Node | No alternative match |
| Opaque Expression | Calculation Usage |
| Send Signal Action | Send Action Usage |
| Accept Event Action | Accept Action Usage |
| Item/Information Flow | Flow Usage / Flow Definition |
| Binding Connector | Binding Connector As Usage |
| Association | Connection Definition / Interface Definition |
| Class | Occurrence Definition |
| Satisfy | Satisfy Requirement Usage |
| Derive | Dependency + Metadata |
| Verify | Dependency + Metadata |
| Property | Reference Usage**Details...If the property:Has no type: Reference usageTyped by data type: Attribute usageTyped by signal: Item usage Typed by class: Occurrence usageTyped by activity: Action usage Typed by a state machine: State usage Typed by use case: Use case usage Typed by interface: Port usageIf the property is typed by an element that is not in the list, the property is migrated to a reference usage. |
| Refine | Dependency + Metadata |
| Initial Node | Start Node |
| Activity Final Node | Terminate Action Usage |
| Flow Final Node | No alternative match |
| Pseudo State (kind=initial) | Start Node |
| Activity Partition | Perform Action |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section contains information regarding the modeling capabilities available for specific elements in the SysML v2 Plugin.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This section contains information regarding the modeling capabilities available for specific elements in the SysML v2 Plugin.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094178 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00100: (2026x) Model customization

- page_id: `298094178`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094178/2026x+Model+customization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-30T17:41:31.661+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Model customization allows you to customize certain predefined model components based on your needs and preferences. You can create model-based custom views with customized table columns, view palettes, symbolic view symbol styles, and view creation dialogs. Instead of, or in addition to, using the predefined components, you can model them yourself based on your needs for specific projects.

The following model components are available for model-based customization.

###### Views customizations

Model-based views customization is described under the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model composition' space='UAF2P'] section's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) View' space='UAF2P'] subsection:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom tabular view definitions' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom tabular view definitions' space='UAF2P']

###### Symbol styles customizations

Model-based symbol styles customization is described under the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model composition' space='UAF2P'] section's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) View' space='UAF2P'] subsection:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom style sheets' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheets' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing style sheet components' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P']

###### Creation dialogs customizations

Model-based creation dialogs customization is described under the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model customization' space='UAF2P'] section's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom creation dialogs' space='UAF2P'] subsection:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom creation dialogs' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P']

Instead of, or in addition to, using the predefined components, you can model them yourself based on your needs for specific projects.</p><p>The following model components are available for <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093064 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00101: (2026x) Model management

- page_id: `298093064`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093064/2026x+Model+management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:30:48.469+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Model management options

###### Containment tree

The Containment tree displays the hierarchy of your model elements, allowing you to review, organize, and modify the model.By default, the Containment tree has the Essential Elements Only mode enabled, displaying the model's essential elements most relevant to your modeling process. Disabling this option displays all model elements, including those less relevant to your modeling process. 
Learn more on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Containment tree' space='UAF2P'] page.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Textual Editor

Textual Editor is akin to a code editor allowing you to view and create your model using textual notation in a code-like format. The Textual Editor displays the model of a specific root namespace. 
Learn more on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Textual Editor' space='UAF2P'] page. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Views

Views allow you to create a specific view of the model and create and modify model elements and their symbols. They do not display the model in its entirety with all of the elements the model contains. A view represents a particular set of model elements, encompassing a particular view of the model. 
Learn more on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with views' space='UAF2P'] page. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Disabling this option displays all model elements, including those less relevant to your modeling process. <br />Learn more on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Working with Containment tree" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Working with Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Model management" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><h4>Textual Editor</h4><p>Textual Editor is akin to a code editor allowing you to view and create your model using textual notation in a code-like format. The Textual Editor displays the model of a specific root namespace.<br />Learn more on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Working with Textual Editor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Working with Textual Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.<br /><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="textual_notation_view.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Model management" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><h4>Views</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Views allow you to create a specific view of the model and create and modify model elements and their symbols.
