ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6e37a9b-b22f-4dc4-b1ba-251f9dd62e20"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the part that owns or inherits the perform action owns or inherits parts that are not yet displayed as swimlanes, clicking the + button displays a dropdown menu with the following categories:</p><ul><li><strong>Display </strong>that has the following options:<ul><li><em>part &lt;the name of the part that does not yet have the corresponding swimlane displayed in the view&gt;</em>. Click to create the corresponding swimlane.</li><li><em>All</em>. Click to create the corresponding swimlanes for all parts that are not represented in the swimlane symbol.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Create </strong>that has the following option:<ul><li><em>part</em>. Click to create a part, represented by a new swimlane. </li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcfa6669-6efb-460b-b753-00bd54493835"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce4a83b6-a594-469d-8568-977305b8c880"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_or_display_swimlanes.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f14c0b5-cd33-44a6-ab35-9d0a81945b20"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6aaaa2c7-48cf-4ab7-b67c-3fd8388fbe40"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'weather station'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// subpart whose representative swimlane is not displayed yet, thus it is available in the Display dropdown menu</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Displaying actions in swimlanes</h4><p>To display actions in swimlanes</p><hr /><ul><li>Select one swimlane by right-clicking its header or all swimlanes by right-clicking the swimlane pool. In the shortcut menu, <span>click </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:link-body><strong>Display</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link><span>, then</span><span> </span><strong>Display Actions </strong>in the dropdown menu<span>. <br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3dc1898d-3456-4fd1-8697-4223a6d0f6c7"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The specific swimlane's shortcut menu contains the same commands as the</span> part element symbol it represents<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h4><span>Displaying parameters, connectors, or other features for element symbols in swimlanes</span></h4><p><span>To display parameters, connectors, or other features for element symbols in swimlanes</span></p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click element symbol(s) in the swimlane(s). In the shortcut menu, click <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:link-body><strong>Display</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>, then click the appropriate command in its dropdown menu. </li></ul><h3>Modeling actions with swimlanes</h3><h4>Creating actions in swimlanes</h4><p>You may create actions and the appropriate subparts' perform actions via the Textual Editor and then display the actions in swimlanes via the <ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying actions in swimlanes"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes" /></ac:link> procedure. However, you can create actions in swimlanes using the view palette. This way, the appropriate perform actions are automatically created for the subparts in whose representative swimlanes the actions are created.</p><p>To create actions in swimlanes </p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying swimlane grid and creating additional swimlanes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display or create swimlanes for the perform action in a view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes" /></ac:link>.</li><li>In the view palette, under the Actions group, click the button for the needed action.</li><li>Click on the swimlane representing the part that you want to perform the action.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8538ff03-a6b8-4378-9cb3-b2761aa73c73"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Indicate the start and end of an action sequence </span>using start and done actions<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Start and done actions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Start and done actions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></span></li><li><span>Connect and sequence actions using successions, flows, and succession flows. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Action succession and flow" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Action succession and flow]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></li><li><span>Control the sequencing of actions using control nodes. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Control nodes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Control nodes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></li></ul><p><span>Start and done actions, as well as control nodes, are not performed, thus parts in whose representative swimlanes they are displayed, do not have perform actions for performing them. These actions and nodes are owned by the perform action that owns the swimlane grid symbol, while their symbols are owned by the swimlane grid symbol. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c32bdda-ab7d-4ac1-9a35-c3df069876ad"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8ec2a9a-e63e-495a-b4e3-214a2e9a0e1b"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_action_in_swimlane_before.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be524e1a-8eaf-4c26-9488-2b7393181112"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e1d31c9-1f45-4af7-88ae-ca0f11dc2264"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_action_in_swimlane_after.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9cbb8236-6503-4583-a1a7-c5b35723b86e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'check battery'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// perform action with applicable feature chain automatically created for the part once the</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> action 'check battery'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">was created in the swimlane that represents the part 'drone'</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;      <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// perform action with applicable feature chain automatically created for the part once the<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> action 'start mission' was created in the </span></span>swimlane that represents the part 'mission control center'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                               <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// action created via view</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'check battery'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                              <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// action created via view</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'check battery'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// succession created via view</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Deleting actions in swimlanes</h4><p>To delete actions in swimlanes</p><hr /><ul><li>To delete an action element or symbol in swimlane, see the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Deleting an element or its symbol via a view"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Deleting an element or its symbol via a view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23004a7d-2f45-46df-a439-5977de4cbf46"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Deleting an action element via swimlane deletes the appropriate perform action from the part responsible for performing the deleted action. Any associated connectors are deleted as well.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h4>Changing which parts are responsible for performing actions via swimlanes</h4><p>You can move actions between swimlanes to change which parts are responsible for performing these actions. The responsible parts are indicated in the header of the swimlane with the keyword <span>«performer»</span>.</p><p>To change which parts are responsible for performing actions via swimlanes </p><hr /><ul><li>Click and drag the action symbol itself from its source swimlane to the target swimlane of the needed perform action.</li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Details regarding the change:</strong></p><ul><li>If the source and target <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">swimlanes are in the same swimlane grid symbol owned by the common perform action, m</span>oving an action from one swimlane to another updates which part is the owner of the perform action responsible for performing the moved action. </li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="748624ae-7832-4d30-a7d2-2960c078083c"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae60f251-9b35-45d3-bfaa-417b2f6dbadb"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_action_to_another_swimlane_before.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="101feb5c-37b0-496a-89d6-7ec1e7e3701a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// perform action is owned by the part 'drone'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85516a69-43af-40b7-af5d-042339d2452c"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_action_to_another_swimlane_after.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9e0de7a-64e3-4018-88af-bed7e66bda57"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// perform action's owner is updated to the part 'mission control center'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ul><li>If the source and target <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">swimlanes are in different swimlane grid symbols owned by different perform actions (see example below), m</span>oving an action updates:<ul><li>the moved action's owner to the new perform action;</li><li>which part is the owner of the perform action responsible for performing the moved action;<ul><li>the feature chain <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">for the referenced feature of the appropriate perform action</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span></li></ul></li></ul></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The same applies if<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> the moved action is not inherited or owned by the perform action that owns the target swimlane. </span><br /></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9316d807-eecd-432b-9498-6223719c52cb"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d8209dd7-5f3d-4552-bb0a-13e851859333"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_action_to_different_swimlane_symbol_before.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1eae025a-02e8-424d-aefe-dfcb557d00fb"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">the part 'mission control center' is the owner of the perform action responsible for performing the action 'start mission'; </span></span>the feature chain for the referenced action is 'run mission'.'start mission'</span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the owner of the action 'start mission' is the perform action 'run mission'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in delivery mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'distribution center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef2a0bb8-e369-4a9b-adf9-9ededc3ee428"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_action_to_different_swimlane_symbol_after.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8fefd25d-d556-4a7d-80f6-154339de961b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the previously owned perform action is moved out</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the previously owned <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">action 'start mission' is moved out</span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in delivery mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'distribution center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run delivery mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">the part 'distribution center' is updated as the owner of the perform action responsible for performing the action 'start mission'; </span></span>the feature chain for the referenced action is updated to 'run delivery mission'.'start mission'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run delivery mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the owner of the action 'start mission' is updated to the perform action 'run delivery mission'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Deleting parts responsible for performing actions via swimlanes </h4><p>To delete parts responsible for performing actions via swimlanes </p><hr /><ol><li>Select the header of the swimlane representing the part you want to delete. </li><li>Follow the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Deleting an element or its symbol via a view"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Deleting an element or its symbol via a view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> on the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> page.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5fb24ce6-d020-4b1f-b5c6-0516b68bd973"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Deleting parts responsible for performing actions via swimlanes also deletes perform actions contained by the deleted part. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Adapting actions to specific context using swimlanes</h4><p align="justify"><span>You can adapt the existing actions to specific contexts without affecting the already modeled elements. You can do this by:</span></p><ol><li><span>First, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the part that owns the perform action, which in turn owns the actions performed by the subparts. </span><span>The subsetting part inherits the features of the subsetted part, including the subparts and the perform action.</span></li><li><span>You can then <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[redefine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the inherited perform action to adapt the subsetting part and its inherited features to the new context. </span></li></ol><p align="justify"><span>Then you can:</span></p><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying swimlane grid and creating additional swimlanes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display the swimlane grid and create new swimlanes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes" /></ac:link> relevant to this context, which creates subparts for the subsetting part. You can then <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating actions in swimlanes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create actions in these swimlanes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes" /></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefine the inherited subparts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. You can then manage actions in these swimlanes:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fe048c4-f991-4dc6-8e05-ed47ddfcef16"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To easily redefine the inherited subparts via the swimlane headers, <span>right-click the header of the swimlane that represents the subpart you want to redefine. In the shortcut menu, click </span><strong>Refactor</strong><span>, then </span><strong>Redefine</strong><span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eb949be-e3ae-48b3-936d-0fc3c7518543"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If an action is not owned but simply referenced by the perform action that is an inherited feature of the part represented by the swimlane, the action is displayed with the ^ prefix. See example below.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining a usage via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the actions in these swimlanes via the Textual Editor. </li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Deleting an element or its symbol via a view"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Remove from the view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> action symbols that are unnecessary in this context.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating actions in swimlanes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create actions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes" /></ac:link> that would then be owned by the redefining perform action of the subsetting part.</li></ol></li></ol><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actions_adapted_to_specific_context_using_swimlanes.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">The part </span><em style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">drone in delivery mission</em><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);"> is created by subsetting the part <em>drone in any mission </em>(the example of which is displayed at the top of this page). It inherits the perform action </span><em style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">run mission</em><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">. However, since additional procedures should be involved, the inherited perform action </span><em style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">run mission</em><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);"> needs modifications within the context of the part </span><em style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">drone in delivery mission</em><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">. This is achieved by redefining the perform action </span><em style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">run mission</em><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);"> specifically for delivery missions. This allows introducing new actions relevant to delivery missions without impacting the <em>drone in mission </em>part.</span></h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094032 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00105: (2026x) Modeling tabular view columns

- page_id: `298094032`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094032/2026x+Modeling+tabular+view+columns
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-01-08T11:39:49.646+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Creating views > (2026x) Creating tabular views > (2026x) Tabular view columns
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the procedures on this page for modeling tabular view columns.

#### Prerequisites

The procedures for modeling tabular view columns described on this page can be used for modeling columns for[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular views' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom tabular view definitions' space='UAF2P'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space='UAF2P']. However, the following procedure, Redefining the inherited renderer,must be done in all cases.

##### Redefining the inherited renderer

To redefine the inherited renderer

1. Ensure the package containing the view usage/definition for which you want to model columns imports KerML , SysML , and DS_Views packages. packageCustomTabularViews{ 
privateimportKerML::*; 
privateimportSysML::*; 
privateimportDS_Views::*; 
}
2. Ensure the following steps are done based on whether you want to model table columns for a view usage or a custom view definition:
  1. For a view usage:
    1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space='UAF2P'] .
    2. The view has an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exposing elements for views' space='UAF2P'] .
    3. (Optional) The view has [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Filtering elements for views' space='UAF2P'] .
  2. For a custom view definition:
    1. Ensure you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom tabular view definitions' space='UAF2P'] .
3. Within the body of the view usage/definition, declare the keywords*render rendering*, (optionally) specify the name for the element,**and do one of the following:
  1. Redefine the inherited *asTable*renderer. This applies to:
    1. View usages: views defined by predefined tabular view definitions.
    2. View definitions: new custom tabular view definitions created from scratch or those reusing predefined tabular view definitions. All standard predefined tabular view definitions use the *DS_Views::CoreViews::asTable***renderer.
  2. R edefine the inherited renderer. This applies to:
    1. View usages: views defined by custom tabular view definitions.
    2. View definitions: new custom tabular view definitions that reuse custom-created tabular view definitions .
4. Click the Synchronize button. Proceed to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modeling tabular view columns' space='UAF2P'].

50%

// ---------- view usages

// a view usage defined by a predefined tabular view definition:

view'Drone Structure Table': TabularViews::gt{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>>asTable;// *Generic Table*has the rendering *asTable* 
}

// a view usage defined by a custom tabular view definition:

view'VCCU System Behavior Table':'System Behavior Table'{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>> asNewTable1;// *'System Behavior Table'*has the rendering *asNewTable1*, which redefines the *asTable*rendering 
}

50%

// ---------- view definitions

// a new view definition created from scratch:

viewdef'System Behavior Table':>CoreViews::bt{renderrenderingasNewTable1:>>asTable;}

// a new view definition created by reusing a predefined view definition *Generic Table*:

viewdef'System Components Taxonomy Table':> TabularViews::gt{ 
renderrenderingasNewTable2:>>asTable;

}

// a new view definition created by reusing a custom-created view definition 'System Behavior Table':

viewdef'Variant Table':> 'System Behavior Table'{ 
renderrenderingasNewTable3:>> asNewTable1;// *asNewTable3*redefines the *'System Behavior Table'* view definition's *asNewTable1* rendering, which itself redefines the *Base Table*view definition's *asTable* rendering

}

#### Modeling tabular view columns

##### Removing inherited table columns

To remove inherited table columns

1. Ensure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modeling tabular view columns' space='UAF2P'].
2. Within the body of the renderer, declare a view, redefine the column you want to remove, and set its multiplicity to zero. The table columns that are inherited from the standard predefined tabular view definitions are indicated in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular views' space='UAF2P'] table.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

// ------ a view defined by a predefined tabular view:

view'System Components Taxonomy Table': TabularViews::gt{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>>asTable{ 
view:>>'Declared Short Name'[0]; // view redefines *Declared Short Name***inherited via *Generic Table,*specifying the redefining column's multiplicity to zero to remove it 
} 
}

// ------ a view defined by a custom tabular view:

view'VCCU System Behavior Table':'System Behavior Table'{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>> asNewTable1{ 
view:>>'Declared Short Name'[0];// view redefines *Declared Short Name*inherited via *'System Behavior Table',*specifying the redefining column's multiplicity to zero to remove it 
} 
}

##### Adding table columns

###### Adding table columns by feature

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular view columns' space='UAF2P'] table on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular view columns' space='UAF2P'] page.

To add table columns by feature

1. Within the body of the renderer, declare a view, specify the column name, define it by *CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView*, and subset *column*.
2. Within the column view's body, declare *ref item*, redefine *columnFeature*, and specify the expression for the targeted metadata feature.
3. Click the Synchronize button. view'VCCU System Behavior Table':'System Behavior Table'{exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>> asNewTable1{view'Declared Name':CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new columnrefitem:>> columnFeature = declaredNamemetaKerML::Core::Feature; // targets *Declared Name*}view'Declared Short Name': CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new column 
refitem:>> columnFeature = declaredShortNamemetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets *Declared Short Name* 
} 
viewDocumentation: CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new column 
refitem:>> columnFeature = documentationmetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets *Documentation* 
} 
view'Qualified Name': CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new column 
refitem:>> columnFeature = qualifiedNamemetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets *Qualified Name* 
} 
viewOwner: CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new column 
refitem:>> columnFeature = ownermetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets *Owner* 
} 
view'Owned Element': CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new column 
refitem:>> columnFeature = ownedElementmetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets for *Owned Element* 
}} 
}Alternative notation// the following expressions are equivalent: 
refitem:>>columnFeature=declaredShortNamemetaKerML::Core::Feature; 
refitem:>>columnFeature=declaredShortName.metadata as KerML::Core::Feature;

###### Adding table columns by expression

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular view columns' space='UAF2P'] table on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular view columns' space='UAF2P'] page.

To add table columns by expression

1. Within the body of the renderer, declare a view, specify the element name, define it by *CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView*, and subset *column*.
2. Within the column view's body, d eclarethe keywords*render rendering*, specify the name for the element, define it by the needed cell rendering definition, and subset *CoreViews::asTableCell*. The available table cell rendering can be found in the *CoreViews* package.Click here to view themrenderingdefBooleanCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefElementCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefEnumCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefIntegerCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefMetaobjectCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefRealCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefStringCellRendering:> TableCellRendering;rendering def TableCellRendering { 
 calc getValue { in rowElement [0..1] : Metaobjects::Metaobject; } 
 attribute isMultiValued [0..1] : ScalarValues::Boolean; 
 }
3. Within the body of the rendering, declare a calculation and redefine *getValue*. Specify the *in* direction, redefine the *rowElement*. Specify the needed expression. See the example below.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Drone Structure Table':>TabularViews::gt,SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter{// custom view definition reusing *Generic Table* 
filterhastypeSysML::Systems::PartDefinition|hastypeSysML::Systems::PartUsage; 
renderrenderingnewTable:>>asTable{// redefining the inherited table rendering 
viewWeight:CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView:>column{// new custom column *Weight* 
renderrenderingasTableCell:CoreViews::RealCellRendering:>CoreViews::asTableCell{// specifying the table column's cell rendering as *Real* 
calc:>>getValue{ 
in:>>rowElement:KerML::Root::Element; 
ifgetWeight(rowElement) @KerML::Kernel::LiteralInteger? (getWeight(rowElement)asKerML::Kernel::LiteralInteger).valueelse 
(getWeight(rowElement)asKerML::Kernel::LiteralRational).value 
} 
calcgetWeight{ 
ine:KerML::Root::Element; 
(((e.ownedElementasSysML::Systems::AttributeUsage)->ControlFunctions::select{ 
inee; 
(eeasKerML::Root::Element).name=="weight" 
}asSysML::Systems::AttributeUsage).ownedRelationshipasKerML::Kernel::FeatureValue).value 
} 
} 
} 
} 
}

##### Modifying table columns

###### Modifying table column attributes

To modify table column attributes

1. (Conditional) For inherited columns, redefine the column whose attributes you want to modify.
2. Within the column view's body, redefine the needed attribute and set its value. Predefined column attributes are:*isMultiline*. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular view columns' space='UAF2P']>]]>
3. Click the Synchronize button.

view'VCCU System Behavior Table':TabularViews::gt{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>>asTable{ 
 // new column 
view'Qualified Name':CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:>column{ // new column 
attribute:>>isMultilinedefaulttrue;// setting multiline to *true*to wrap long qualified name 
refitem:>>columnFeature=qualifiedNamemetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets Qualified Name 
} 
// redefining an inherited column 
 view :>> 'Qualified Name' { // redefining an inherited column 
 attribute :>> isMultiline default true;// setting multiline to *true*to wrap a long qualified name 
 } 
} 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">See the procedures on this page for modeling tabular view columns. </span></p><h2><span>Prerequisites</span></h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c931d1f2-e291-47de-9b8b-6684503e5178"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The procedures for modeling tabular view columns described on this page can be used for modeling columns for </span><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[predefined tabular view definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating custom tabular view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom tabular view definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[individual tabular view usages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. However, the following procedure</span><span>, <a>Redefining the inherited renderer</a>, </span><span>must be done in all cases.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Redefining the inherited renderer</h3><p>To redefine the inherited renderer</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="74e261ca-4f3c-4c8d-99cd-04ee7aaf6350">Ensure the package containing the view usage/definition for which you want to model columns imports <em>KerML</em>, <em>SysML</em>, and <em>DS_Views </em>packages.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="afd463e3-d9e2-4dc0-8b25-6a5cb3652e41"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomTabularViews</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerML</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::*;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::*;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::*;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="c2a1a51a-9646-47e6-bc7c-5e8175be36a5">Ensure the following steps are done based on whether you want to model table columns for a view usage or a custom view definition:<ol><li>For a view usage:<ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[The view is defined by the needed tabular view definition]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>The view has an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Exposing elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[expose relationship]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </li><li>(Optional) The view has <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Filtering elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol></li><li>For a custom view definition:<ol><li>Ensure you have <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating a custom tabular view definition"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating custom tabular view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[created the new view definition instance]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="079eeaa9-ccca-4761-b3e7-128de7ff0165">Within the body of the view usage/definition, declare <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the keywords<span> </span><em>render rendering</em>, (optionally) specify the name for the element,<em><span> </span></em>and do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Redefine the inherited <em>asTable </em>renderer. This applies to:</span><ol><li>View usages: views defined by predefined tabular view definitions.</li><li>View definitions: new custom tabular view definitions created <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">from scratch or those </span>reusing predefined tabular view definitions.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b00fbd3a-de63-4494-bfef-0c39cd24dee3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>All standard predefined tabular view definitions use the <em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">DS_Views::CoreViews::asTable</span></em><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">renderer.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li>R<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">edefine the inherited renderer. This applies to:</span><ol><li>View usages: views defined by custom tabular view definitions.</li><li>View definitions: new custom tabular view definitions that reuse custom-created tabular view definitions<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></li></ol></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="f330cbc2-5cc7-46b4-b8ba-4d84a1cf0be8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button. Proceed to <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[model table columns]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling tabular view columns" /></ac:link>.</span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40f2c93a-6f18-4de1-b355-5ca59e6cd394"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span tabular view definition:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a view usage defined by a custom tabular view definition:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'VCCU System Behavior Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'System Behavior Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>'System Behavior Table' </em>has the rendering <em>asNewTable1</em>, which redefines the
````
