/></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3df55f68-d1ad-42e3-9909-27233f5b2aea"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07e6f2c7-3e19-46e3-a9ac-070c78967525"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// ---------- view definitions</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a new view definition created from scratch:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'System Behavior Table'</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CoreViews::bt</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><u style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> asNewTable1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable;<br /></span></u><u style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">}</span></u></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a new view definition created by reusing a predefined view definition <em>Generic Table</em>:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'System Components Taxonomy Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TabularViews</span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> asN</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ewTable2</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable;</span></u></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a new view definition created by reusing a custom-created view definition 'System Behavior Table':</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Variant Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'System Behavior Table'</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> asN</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ewTable3</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; <u>asN<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ewTable1</span></u></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">;</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>asNewTable3 </em>redefines the <em>'System Behavior Table'</em> view definition's <em>asNewTable1</em> rendering, which itself redefines the <em>Base Table </em>view definition's <em>asTable</em> rendering  </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>Modeling tabular view columns</h2><h3><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Removing inherited table columns</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To remove inherited table columns</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d6763656-d7db-477b-91b7-ad39080aa30a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Ensure <ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining the inherited renderer"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[the inherited renderer is redefined]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling tabular view columns" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="cfac2fde-c91e-40ca-b319-4ece21fbab6f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the renderer, declare a view, redefine the column you want to remove, and set its multiplicity to zero.</span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69a869db-c56f-47bc-9fb5-dd9e448a09bf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The table columns that are inherited from the standard predefined tabular view definitions are indicated in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Tabular views"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Tabular views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="a12a0793-1b44-42c4-b029-43e324cc64bd"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0da79d01-6c4d-4e59-91f4-1ca0e1f8119b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// ------ a view defined by a predefined tabular view:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'System Components Taxonomy Table' </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TabularViews</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::</span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Declared Short Name'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><u>];</u> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view redefines <em>Declared Short Name</em><em> </em>inherited via <em>Generic Table, </em>specifying the redefining column's multiplicity to zero to remove it</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// ------ a view defined by a custom tabular view:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'VCCU System Behavior Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'System Behavior Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; asNewTable1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Declared Short Name'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">];</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view redefines <em>Declared Short Name </em>inherited via <em>'System Behavior Table', </em>specifying the redefining column's multiplicity to zero to remove it</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Adding table columns</h3><h4>Adding table columns by feature</h4><p>For more information, see the <span><ac:link ac:anchor="Column kinds"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Column kinds]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> table on the <span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Tabular view columns]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page</span>.</span></p><p>To add table columns by feature</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c6b4e088-ffa6-4f3e-a8f9-cd10d9d5ae40"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the renderer, declare a view, specify the column name, define it by <em>CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView</em>, and subset <em>column</em>. </span></li><li data-uuid="fa7906bf-8bee-4a52-88b8-577ad0f5e562"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the column view's body, declare <em>ref item</em>, redefine <em>columnFeature</em>, and specify the expression for the targeted metadata feature. </span></li><li data-uuid="ad4fc296-c895-4c9c-804b-4dd461ef0fe5"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7aafe6e3-8594-45d9-a734-6baf31f3e660"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'VCCU System Behavior Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'System Behavior Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; asNewTable1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">      view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Declared Name'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt; column {</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new column <br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; columnFeature = declaredName </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">meta</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> KerML::Core::Feature;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets <em>Declared Name<br /></em></span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Declared Short Name'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView </span>:&gt; column {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new column </span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; columnFeature = declaredShortName </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">meta</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> KerML::Core::Feature;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets <em>Declared Short Name</em></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">Documentation</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView </span>:&gt; column {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new column </span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; columnFeature = documentation </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">meta</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> KerML::Core::Feature;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets <em>Documentation</em></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Qualified Name'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView </span>:&gt; column {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new column </span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; columnFeature = qualifiedName </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">meta</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> KerML::Core::Feature;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets <em>Qualified Name</em></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">Owner</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView </span>:&gt; column {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new column </span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; columnFeature = owner </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">meta</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> KerML::Core::Feature;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets <em>Owner</em></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Owned Element'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView </span>:&gt; column {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new column </span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; columnFeature = ownedElement </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">meta</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> KerML::Core::Feature;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets for <em>Owned Element</em></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b9fc132d-e9d2-4121-bdd4-98f0433d70cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Alternative notation</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55e92dd5-8bf9-425d-8227-76ad3200dfd0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the following expressions are equivalent:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">columnFeature</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">declaredShortName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">meta</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerML::Core::Feature</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">columnFeature</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">declaredShortName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata as <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerML::Core::Feature</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Adding table columns by expression</h4><p>For more information, see the <span><ac:link ac:anchor="Column kinds"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Column kinds]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> table on the <span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Tabular view columns]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page</span>.</span></p><p>To add table columns by expression</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="8186e9bc-18a8-437b-a313-b854a6eb44e6"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the renderer, declare a view, specify the element name, define it by <em>CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView</em>, and subset <em>column</em>. </span></li><li data-uuid="40d1ce1b-ac2c-4f56-a253-9da21c61d67d"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the column view's body, d</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">eclare<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the keywords<span> </span><em>render rendering</em>, specify the name for the element, define it by the needed cell rendering definition, and subset <em>CoreViews::asTableCell</em>.</span></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7d2b960-d8ba-4118-90c0-ea9614b0f73f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The available table cell rendering can be found in the <em>CoreViews</em> package. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de900749-4699-4b89-88bc-cd1f61a6e481"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Click here to view them</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0281730f-df5d-4c82-8975-144ed9c2b265"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">BooleanCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ElementCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">EnumCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">IntegerCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MetaobjectCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">RealCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StringCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; TableCellRendering;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span> <span }</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="5571ec5c-812a-4b59-bbca-63a61e40ff2d"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the rendering, declare a calculation and redefine <em>getValue</em>. See the example below.</span></li><li data-uuid="4bbdd351-c0eb-4fe4-8e33-bd84a0b8f680"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: qualified name </span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093422 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00107: (2026x) Modifiers

- page_id: `298093422`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093422/2026x+Modifiers
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:39:32.479+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Usages can have additional properties indicated by the keywords added in the usage declaration. The keywords should be specified in the following order:

1. *in, out, inout* – indicates a direction for a directed usage.
  1. *in* – specifies what is input to an instance.
  2. *out* – specifies what is output by an instance.
  3. *inout* – specifies what is both input to and output by an instance.
2. *derived* – specifies a derived usage that is typically expected to have a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Binding' space='UAF2P'] expression that completely determines its value at all times.
3. *abstract* – specifies an abstract usage whose value must also be a value of a concrete usage that specializes the abstract usage. If a usage is not abstract, it is a concrete usage.
4. *constant –* specifies that the usage is constant. Its values are the same during the entire existence of the featuring instance.
5. *ref* – specifies a referential usage. Directed usages are referential by default, whether the*ref*keyword is specified or not. If a usage is not referential, it is a composite usage, which is deleted once its featuring instances are deleted.

- For more information on using multiple keywords and their order in element declaration, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space='UAF2P'] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space='UAF2P'] page.
- The modifier keyword's display on the element symbol can be controlled via the Show Modifiers symbol style. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space='UAF2P'] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space='UAF2P'] page.

##### Specifying the direction modifier for features

###### Specifying the direction modifier for features via the Textual Editor

To specify the direction modifier for features via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the needed direction keyword: *in, out*, or *inout.*
2. Click the Synchronize button. partdefTank:>Container{ 
initemfuelFlow:Fuel; 
}

###### Specifying the direction modifier for features via the element shortcut menu

To specify the direction modifier for features via the element shortcut menu

- Right-click a usage element in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in its shortcut menu, in the Properties group, click**Direction**, and in its submenu, clickeither *in*, *out*, or *inout* .

##### Specifying the derived modifier for features

###### Specifying the derived modifier for features via the Textual Editor

To specify the derived modifier for features via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the derived keyword.
2. Click the Synchronize button. itemdefTestRecord{ 
//... 
derivedattributeaverageScore[1] :Rational; 
}

##### Specifying the abstract modifier for features

###### Specifying the abstract modifier for features via the Textual Editor

To specify the abstract modifier for features via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the abstract keyword.
2. Click the Synchronize button. abstractpartdefVehicle;

##### Specifying the constant modifier for features

###### Specifying the constant modifier for features via the Textual Editor

To specify the constant modifier for features via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the constant keyword.
2. Click the Synchronize button. occurrencedefApprovedFlight:>Flight{ 
constant ref partapprovedAircraftredefinesaircraft; 
}

##### Specifying the ref modifier for features

#### NOTE: Referential, composite and reference usages

Referential, composite and reference usages

Referential usage references a usage that is an instance of another composition. If a usage is not referential, it is composite, which is deleted once its featuring instances are deleted.

partdefVehicle{ 
parteng:Engine;// composite 
refpartdriver:Person;// referential 
}

Reference usages are declared without the element kind keyword and are not restricted to being defined by a definition of any specific kind. Its declaration may optionally include the *ref*keyword, however, reference usages are implicitly referential.

part def Container { 
 item content; 
} 
part def Tank :> Container { 
 fuel : Fuel :>> content; // reference usage 
}

###### Specifying usages as composite or referential via the Textual Editor

To specify usages as composite or referential via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify one of the following:
  1. For a referential usage, specify the keyword ref .
  2. For a composite usage, remove the keyword ref .
2. Click the Synchronize button. partdefVehicle{ 
parteng:Engine;// composite 
refpartdriver:Person;// referential 
}

###### Specifying usages as composite or referential via the element shortcut menu

To specify usages as composite or referential via the element shortcut menu

1. Right-click a usage in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in the shortcut menu, and do one of the following:
  1. For a referential usage, deselect the **Is Composite** property.
  2. For a composite usage, select the **Is Composite** property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Usages can have additional properties indicated by the keywords added in the usage declaration. The keywords should be specified in the following order:</span></p><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>in, out, inout</em> – indicates a direction for a directed usage.</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>in</em> – specifies what is input to an instance.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>out</em> – specifies what is output by an instance.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>inout</em> – specifies what is both input to and output by an instance.<br /></span></li></ol></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>derived</em> – </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">specifies a derived usage that is typically expected to have a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Binding" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[bound value]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> expression that completely determines its value at all times.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>abstract</em> – specifies an abstract usage whose value must also be a value of a concrete usage that specializes the abstract usage. If a usage is not abstract, it is a concrete usage.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>constant – </em></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">specifies that the usage is constant. Its values are the same during the entire existence of the featuring instance.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>ref</em> – specifies a referential usage. Directed usages are referential by default, whether the<span> </span><em>ref</em><span> </span>keyword is specified or not. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094137 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00108: (2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols

- page_id: `298094137`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094137/2026x+Modifying+styles+of+individual+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:38:20.400+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize the styles of individual symbols. Using the Symbol Style dialog, you can specify style properties for specific symbols, determining their appearance.

Changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based symbol styles; the modifications persist even after updating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space='UAF2P']. To reset, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space='UAF2P'] procedure.

##### Opening the Symbol Style dialog

To open the Symbol Style dialog

Do one of the following:

- Right-click the selected element symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, click **Symbol Style**. To invoke the Symbol Style dialog for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Docked symbols' space='UAF2P'], you must right-click the symbol's header.
- Select the element symbol(s) and press Alt+Enter .

##### Modifying individual symbol styles

To modify individual symbol styles

1. Select the symbol(s) whose styles you want to modify and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space='UAF2P'] .
2. Locate the property you want to modify and change its value to the needed one in the Value column.
3. Click Close .

##### Resetting changes made in the Symbol Style dialog

While changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based symbol styles, you can easily undo them following the procedure below.

To reset changes made in the Symbol Style dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space='UAF2P'] .
2. Locate the property you want to reset to the style of the currently activated style sheet. Its check box in the Default column is deselected. [ATTACHMENT filename='Resetting_changes.png']
3. Select the check box in the Default column for the needed property.
4. Click Close .

##### The Symbol Style dialog

The table below lists the style properties available for modification and their descriptions.

- Modifying a symbol style changes its Default value to false .
- You can hide unwanted symbol styles from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] page.

In the **Symbol Style** dialog, symbol styles are ordered into the following groups:

- **Main**: this property group displays symbol styles available to the majority of element kinds.
- **Compartment-based properties**: the availability of specific symbol style groups depends on the element kind, as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P']. Some of the symbol styles available to individual compartment-based symbol style groups may be the same as in the Main group, while others are compartment-specific.
  - Compartment-based style groups can be: Documentation, Objective, Subject, Actors, Stakeholders, Include Use Cases, Parts, Ports, Parameters, Attributes, Ends, Satisfy Requirements , Items, Constraints, Require Constraints, Assume Constraints, Assert Constraints, Actions, Perform Actions, Exhibit States, Frames, Enumerations, and Metadata.

| Name | Value options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Main |  |  |
| Line Width | Positive number | Specify the width of the symbol's outline. |
| Fill Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's header.To enable a gradient for this property, enable the view style property. |
| Use Fill Color | true/false | Specify whether to use a specific color for the symbol's header. |
| Pen Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's outline. |
| Text Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's text. |
| Font | Multiple font optionsMultiple style options (italic, bold, etc.)Multiple size options | Specify the font style of the symbol's text. |
| Wrap | true/false | Specify whether the text should wrap to fit the width of the element symbol. |
| Compartment Fill Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's compartments. |
| Compartment Text Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color for the text in the symbol's compartments. |
| Show Short Name | true/false | Specify whether the element's should be displayed on the element symbol. |
| Show Name | true/false | Specify whether the element's should be displayed on the element symbol. |
| Show Modifiers | true/false | Specify whether the element's (e.g., in, ref, abstract, etc.) should be displayed on the element symbol. The property does not control the individual and portion kind (timeslice, snapshot) modifiers. |
| Show Full Text | true/false | Specify whether element values should be displayed in full or abbreviated. Modifying this property affects the values displayed in both the selected symbol's name and its compartments.Modifying this property does not affect element values displayed in the metadata compartment. Values in this compartment are always displayed in full.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Content Fill Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's body.To enable a gradient for this property, enable the view style property.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Text Vertical Position | TopCenterBottom | Specify the element name position for the symbol.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind.The property is only effective for element symbols displayed without their compartments. |
| Show Specialization | true/false | Specify whether the textual symbols should be displayed in the element name (e.g., the textual symbol :> or the keyword specializes, in the case of subclassification). The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Qualified Name for Specialization | true/false | Specify whether the specialization's full should be displayed in the element name.Enabling the property also requires the 'Show Specialization' property to be enabled.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Orientation | HorizontalVertical | Specify the orientation for the symbol.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Path Style | RectilinearObliqueBezier | Specify the style of the path.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Value | true/false | Specify whether the element's defined value should be displayed.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Inherited Sign | true/false | Specify whether the inherited sign should be displayed on the symbol.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Payload | true/false | Specify whether the payload name and sign should be displayed on the connector path.The property is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Text Horizontal Position | LeftCenterRight | Specify the text position for the vertical and horizontal separator symbols. |
| Line Style | SolidDashedDottedetc. | Specify the line style for the vertical and horizontal separator symbols. |
| Is Rounded | true/false | Specify whether the rectangular shape symbol's corners should be rounded. |
| Compartment-based symbol styles (in compartment-based symbol style groups, e.g., Documentation, Objective, Subject, Actors, etc.) |  |  |
| Show Compartment | true/false | Specify whether the compartment should be displayed or suppressed. |
| Show Inherited | true/false | Specify whether the inherited elements should be displayed on the symbol. |
| Show Only Owned | true/false | Specify whether only the owned elements or imported ones, too, should be displayed in the symbol compartment.The property is only available for the package symbol and applies to its Members compartment. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can customize the styles of individual symbols. Using the Symbol Style dialog, you can specify style properties for specific symbols, determining their appearance.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e80d6bd3-c74a-45dd-9c7a-65a10a9ee3ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based symbol styles; the modifications persist even after updating <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Activating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[active style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. To reset, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Resetting changes made in the Symbol Style dialog"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /></ac:link> procedure.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Opening the Symbol Style dialog</h3><p>To open the Symbol Style dialog</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click the selected element symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Symbol Style</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01b07579-7af8-4cc3-aacf-48bc421211e3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To invoke the Symbol Style dialog for a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Docked symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[docked symbol]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, you must right-click the symbol's header.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Select the element symbol(s) and press <strong>Alt+Enter</strong>.</li></ul><h3>Modifying individual symbol styles</h3><p>To modify individual symbol styles</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the symbol(s) whose styles you want to modify and <ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Symbol Style dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[open the Symbol Style dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Locate the property you want to modify and change its value to the needed one in the Value column.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Resetting changes made in the Symbol Style dialog</h3><p>While changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based symbol styles, you can easily undo them following the procedure below.</p><p>To reset changes made in the Symbol Style dialog</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Symbol Style dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Symbol Style dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Locate the property you want to reset to the style of the currently activated style sheet. Its check box in the <strong>Default </strong>column is deselected.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Resetting_changes.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Select the check box in the <strong>Default </strong>column for the needed property.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>The Symbol Style dialog</h3><p>The table below lists the style properties available for modification and their descriptions. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f005f26-68b4-4ee7-9f09-947dee503a50"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="4878db54-cf23-45f9-8497-eb4527ddc56d">Modifying a symbol style changes its <strong>Default </strong>value to <em>false</em>.</li><li data-uuid="c01759d3-49a4-4dda-a32d-c5ed3585566e">You can hide unwanted symbol styles from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds. See the <ac:link ac:anchor="Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedure on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating style sheet components]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Symbol Style</strong> dialog, symbol styles are ordered into the following groups:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Main</strong>: this property group displays symbol styles available to the majority of element kinds.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Compartment-based properties</strong>: the availability of specific symbol style groups depends on the element kind, as <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[each compartment is only available to specific element kinds]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Some of the symbol styles available to individual compartment-based symbol style groups may be the same as in the Main group, while others are compartment-specific.</span><ul><li data-uuid="22f52dfa-1cc3-4088-bcec-06710565e3a3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Compartment-based style groups can be: Documentation, Objective, Subject, Actors, Stakeholders, Include Use Cases, Parts, Ports, Parameters, Attributes, Ends, </span>Satisfy Requirements<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, Items, Constraints, Require Constraints, Assume Constraints, Assert Constraints, </span>Actions, Perform Actions, Exhibit States, Frames, Enumerations, and Metadata.</li></ul></li></ul><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 74.0672%;"><colgroup><col separator symbols.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Is Rounded<br /></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the rectangular shape symbol's corners should be rounded.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="grey"><div class="content-wrapper" title=""><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Compartment-based symbol styles (in compartment-based symbol style groups, e.g., Documentation, Objective, Subject, Actors, etc.)</strong></span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show </span>Compartment</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the compartment should be displayed or suppressed.</span></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Inherited</span></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the inherited elements should be displayed on the symbol.</span></span></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Only Owned</span></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether only the owned elements or imported ones, too, should be displayed in the symbol compartment.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe7a65db-dbe1-4e47-bffb-d5f14279bad0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The property is only available for the package symbol and applies to its Members compartment.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094141 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00109: (2026x) Modifying styles of individual views

- page_id: `298094141`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094141/2026x+Modifying+styles+of+individual+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:38:30.111+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The primary ways to customize the styles of individual views:

- Managing style sheets for the specific view.
- Managing style properties for the specific view via the View Style dialog.

Changing the styles for an individual view via the View Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based style sheets; the modifications persist even after updating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space='UAF2P']. To reset, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual views' space='UAF2P'] procedure.

##### Managing style sheets for the specific view

- For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space='UAF2P'] procedures on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space='UAF2P'] page.

##### Managing style properties for the specific view via the View Style dialog

###### Opening the View Style dialog

To open the View Style dialog

- Right-click the selected view. In the shortcut menu, click **View Style**.

###### Modifying an individual view's style

To modify an individual view's style

1. Select the view(s) whose styles you want to modify and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual views' space='UAF2P'] .
2. Locate the property you want to modify and change its value to the needed one in the Value column.
3. Click Close .

###### Resetting changes made in the View Style dialog

While changing the styles for an individual view via the View Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based style sheets, you can easily undo them following the procedure below.

To reset changes made in the View Style dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual views' space='UAF2P'] .
2. Locate the property you want to reset to the style of the currently activated style sheet. Its check box in the Default column is deselected.
3. Select the check box in the Default column for the needed property.
4. Click Close .

###### The View Style dialog

The table below lists the style properties available for modification and their descriptions.

Modifying a view style property changes its **Default**value to *false*.

Currently, other view style properties displayed in the dialog besides the **Use Gradient Fill**are not functional.

| Name | Value options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Main |  |  |
| Use Gradient Fill | true/false | Specify whether a gradient should be used for the Fill Color and Content Fill Color . |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094034 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00110: (2026x) Modifying tabular view cells

- page_id: `298094034`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094034/2026x+Modifying+tabular+view+cells
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-01-15T18:37:59.593+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Creating views > (2026x) Creating tabular views > (2026x) Tabular view columns
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular view columns' space='UAF2P'] cells are the intersections of every row and column, which represent element data values. The represented data values depend on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exposing elements for views' space='UAF2P'] elements for the tabular view.

Tabular view column cells are modeled via the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modeling tabular view columns' space='UAF2P'].

##### Modifying tabular view column cells

###### Modifying cell data via the tabular view

Only the String, Element, Enum, Integer, Boolean, and Real cell values can be edited in the table view's diagrammatic representation. This can only be done for *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular view columns' space='UAF2P']* column cells.

To modify cell data via the tabular view

1. In a tabular view, click the cell whose data you want to modify.
2. Specify the new value.
3. Click elsewhere on the table for the changes to be saved.

###### Tabular view cell errors

You may encounter the following icons and buttons in tabular view column cells:

| Icon / Button | Message | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Click to load the cell. | Cell loading was canceled after exceeding 1000 ms. Click the button to restart loading; the modeling tool will wait until the cell has finished loading.If the table is only partially loaded and its data is modified (e.g., via the Containment tree, Textual Editor, etc.) without refreshing the table afterward, the icon is displayed in the cells that were not loaded. In this case, the cell cannot be reloaded; click the Refresh button in the table toolbar to refresh the entire table. |
|  | Failed to evaluate expression. | Resolve model errors related to the column expression. The error may also appear if the expression specified for the tabular view column and/or cell does not apply to the specific element. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

This can only be done for <span><em><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ColumnByFeatureView]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em> column cells</span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To modify cell data via the tabular view</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a7993a2e-c033-4f5b-8398-8cacbfe9738f">In a tabular view, click the cell whose data you want to modify.</li><li data-uuid="6b131bcc-1dc1-4258-a1f9-01faf2ca9a2f">Specify the new value.</li><li data-uuid="c7cc8032-6344-4847-9c23-03f590e4e89a">Click elsewhere on the table for the changes to be saved.</li></ol><h4>Tabular view cell errors</h4><p>You may encounter the following icons and buttons in tabular view column cells:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Icon / Button</th><th scope="col">Message</th><th scope="col">Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Click to load the cell icon.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying tabular view cells" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Click to load the cell.</td><td><p>Cell loading was canceled after exceeding 1000 ms. Click the button to restart loading; the modeling tool will wait until the cell has finished loading.</p><p>If the table is only partially loaded and its data is modified (e.g., via the Containment tree, Textual Editor, etc.) without refreshing the table afterward, the icon is displayed in the cells that were not loaded. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093430 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00111: (2026x) Multiplicity

- page_id: `298093430`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093430/2026x+Multiplicity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:13:26.527+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Usages can have a multiplicity specifying how many values they can have for the instances of theirfeaturing definition or usage.The multiplicity is indicated as a range of natural numbers in square brackets, e.g., [lowerBound..upperBound].

- The lower bound must be finite. If the lower bound is unspecified, then it is taken to be equal to the upper bound, except when the upper bound is *, in which case it is treated as 0.
- The upper bound must be equal to or greater than the lower bound number. It can be unbounded, indicated by the * sign.

If a multiplicity is not declared for a usage, then the usage inherits the multiplicity constraints of any other usages it subsets or redefines. If no tighter constraint is inherited, the effective default is the most general multiplicity [0..*]. However, a tighter default of [1..1] is implicitly declared for the usage if all of the following conditions hold:

- The usage is an attribute usage, an item usage (including a part usage, except if it is a connection usage), or a port usage.
- The usage is owned by a definition or another usage (not a package).
- The usage does not have any explicit owned subsettings or owned redefinitions.

The following keywords can also be used after the multiplicity or in place of it:

- *nonunique*– a non-unique usage indicates that the same usage value can appear more than once. Usages are unique by default, i.e., two usage values can be the same.
- *ordered*– an ordered usage indicates that the usage values can be placed in an order from 1 to the number of values. The features are unordered by default.

For information on cross multiplicity, which is used only within the context of cross features and is usually specified for the*end*elements of a connector, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Cross features' space='UAF2P']page.

##### Specifying usage multiplicity

###### Specifying usage multiplicity via the Textual Editor

To specify usage multiplicity via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, follow the part name with brackets.
2. Specify a range of natural numbers in square brackets, e.g., [lowerBound..upperBound] .
3. Click the Synchronize button.

partbodyAssy:BodyAssy{ 
partdoors[2..4] :Door; 
partbody; 
partsunroof[0..1] :Sunroof; 
}

###### Specifying usage multiplicity via the Specification panel

To specify usage multiplicity via the Specification panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">] : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Sunroof</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Specifying usage multiplicity via the Specification panel</h4><p>To specify usage multiplicity via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>See the procedure<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the multiplicity of the element via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modifying the multiplicity of the element via the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>on the<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the multiplicity of the element via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>page. </li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093702 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00112: (2026x) N-ary connection

- page_id: `298093702`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093702/2026x+N-ary+connection
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:54:40.208+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Structure > (2026x) Connection
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An n-ary connection is a complex connector between three or more elements. The n-ary connection is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the connection.

The kind keyword (e.g., *part*) is optional for connection ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Cross features' space='UAF2P'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating n-ary connections

###### Creating n-ary connections via the Textual Editor

To create n-ary connections via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the connection keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) N-ary connection' space='UAF2P'] (optional), then the connect keyword, then state the features explicitly (using the connect...to syntax ) or in a comma-separated list of related features. If the connection has no name and no specialization, the keyword *connection*can be omitted.You can also name connection ends, associating them to features via[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'].
3. Click the Synchronize button.

connectiondefCanBus{ 
endparttransmission:Transmission; 
endpartelectricalPowerController:ElectricalPowerController; 
endpartinternalCombustionEngine:InternalCombustionEngine; 
}

part'power subsystem'{ 
partelectricalPowerController; 
parttransmission; 
partinternalCombustionEngine;

// named specialized (feature typing) connection usage with named endsconnecting features in a comma-separated list: 
connection'can bus':CanBusconnect(t::>transmission,epc::>electricalPowerController,ice::>internalCombustionEngine);

// unnamed unspecialized connection usage connecting featuresin a comma-separated list: 
connect(transmission,electricalPowerController,internalCombustionEngine);}

###### Creating n-ary connections via the view palette

To create n-ary connections via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **n-ary connection** button. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the **connection** or the **connection****def**button and click the **n-ary connection** button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary connection symbol to be created.
3. Select the n-ary connection symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the end command.
4. Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the symbol of the element you want to connect. Repeat for all elements you want to connect.

##### Converting binary and n-ary connectors

###### Converting binary connectors to n-ary via the view

To convert binary connectors to n-ary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the binary connector you want to convert to an n-ary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to N-ary Dot . The binary connector is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.

###### Converting n-aryconnectorstobinaryvia the view

To convert n-ary connectors to binary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the connector you want to convert to a binary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to Path . The n-ary connector dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed. The **Convert to Path**command is only available if the connector has no more than two ends.

##### Creating an n-ary connection definition

###### Creating n-ary connection definitions via the Textual Editor

To create n-ary connection definitions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the connection def keyword.
2. Name the element, then specify the connection definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the connection definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] of the needed element.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. connectiondefCanBus{ 
endparttransmission:Transmission; 
endpartelectricalPowerController:ElectricalPowerController; 
endpartinternalCombustionEngine:InternalCombustionEngine; 
}

###### Auto-creating n-ary connection definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create n-ary connection definition elements via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='UAF2P']page.

###### Creating n-ary connection definitions via the view palette

To create n-ary connection definitions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the**connection def** button. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **connection**or the **n-ary connection******button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the connection def symbol. The connection def symbol is created containing two end element symbols.
  1. Create additional ends via the Create Element button's end command. See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] page.

connection definitions via the view palette</h4><p>To create n-ary connection definitions via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<strong> connection def</strong> button. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cff2a965-5045-40df-b752-951ecdbe9604"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the <span><strong>connection </strong></span>or the <strong>n-ary connection</strong><strong> </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the connection def symbol. The connection def symbol is created containing two <strong>end</strong> element symbols.<ol><li data-uuid="41ba0992-f70b-4518-9cf8-53c11537501b">Create additional ends via the <strong>Create Element </strong>button's <strong>end </strong>command. See the procedure<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating elements via compartments"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating elements via compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>on the<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>page. </li></ol></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093725 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00113: (2026x) N-ary interface

- page_id: `298093725`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093725/2026x+N-ary+interface
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:55:17.892+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Structure > (2026x) Interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An n-ary interface is a complex connector between three or more ports. The n-ary interface is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the interface.

- The ends of an interface definition or usage must be port usages.
- The port keyword is optional for interface ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Cross features' space='UAF2P'] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating n-ary interfaces

###### Creating n-ary interfaces via the Textual Editor

To create n-ary interfaces via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the interface keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional), then the connect keyword, then state the feature ports explicitly (using the connect...to syntax ) or in a comma-separated list of related features. If the interface has no name and no specialization, the keyword *connect*can be omitted.You can also name interface end ports, associating them to feature ports via[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space='UAF2P'].
3. Click the Synchronize button.

interfacedefCanBusInterface{endporttransmissionPort:CanBusPorts; 
endportelectricalPowerControllerPort:CanBusPorts; 
endportinternalCombustionEnginePort:CanBusPorts; 
}

part'power subsystem'{ 
partelectricalPowerController{portfp;} 
parttransmission{portfp;} 
partinternalCombustionEngine{portfp;}

//named specialized (feature typing) interface usage with named end portsconnecting feature portsin a comma-separated list:interface'can bus':CanBusInterfaceconnect(transmissionPort::>transmission.fp,electricalPowerControllerPort::>electricalPowerController.fp,internalCombustionEnginePort::>internalCombustionEngine.fp);

// unnamed unspecialized interface usage connecting feature portsin a comma-separated list:interface(transmission.fp,electricalPowerController.fp,internalCombustionEngine.fp);}

###### Creating n-ary interfaces via the view palette

To create n-ary interfaces via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the n-ary interface button. If it is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the interface button and click the n-ary interface button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary interface symbol to be created.
3. Select the n-ary interface symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the end command.
4. Draw the end from the n-ary interface symbol to the symbol of the port you want to connect. Repeat for all ports you want to connect.

##### Converting binary and n-ary interfaces

###### Converting binary interface to n-ary via the view

To convert binary interface to n-ary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the binary interface you want to convert to an n-ary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to N-ary Dot . The binary interface is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.

###### Converting n-aryinterfacetobinaryvia the view

To convert n-ary interface to binary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the interface you want to convert to a binary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to Path . The n-ary interface dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed. The **Convert to Path**command is only available if the dependency has no more than two ends.

##### Creating n-ary interface definition

###### Creating n-ary interfaces definition via the Textual Editor

To create n-ary interfaces definition via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the interface def keyword followed by its name.
2. Name the element, then specify the interface definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the interface definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] of the needed port.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. interfacedefCanBusInterface{endporttransmissionPort:CanBusPorts; 
endportelectricalPowerControllerPort:CanBusPorts; 
endportinternalCombustionEnginePort:CanBusPorts; 
}

###### Auto-creating n-ary interfacedefinition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create n-ary interface definition elements via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='UAF2P']page.

###### Creating n-ary interface definitions via the view palette

To create n-ary interface definitions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the**interface def** button. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **interface**or the **n-ary interface**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the interface def symbol. The interface def symbol is created containing two end element symbols.
  1. Create additional ends:
    1. Via the Create Element button's end command. See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] page.
    2. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click the port button. Click on the interface def symbol to create a port for it.
