<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094106 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00123: (2026x) Predefined style sheets

- page_id: `298094106`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094106/2026x+Predefined+style+sheets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:51:13.363+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Each SysML v2 project is automatically loaded with the *3DS**SysML Customization* library that contains predefined style sheets comprised of sets of rules defining symbol styles based on element metaclass.The predefined style sheets are located in the *StyleSheets* package in the *<DS_Styles> DassaultSystemesStyles*namespace of the *3DS**SysML Customization* library.

There are two predefined style sheets in the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets*package:

50%

- DefaultStyleSheet The DefaultStyleSheet style sheet contains rule-based styles that apply to all element symbols in SysML v2 projects by default based on element metaclass.
  - You can view the predefined style definitions used for symbol styles in the 'Predefined style definitions' column in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] page, and the DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions package. [ATTACHMENT filename='defaultstylesheetpartdefinition.png']

50%

- BlackAndWhiteStyleSheet The BlackAndWhiteStyleSheet style sheet contains rule-based styles for representing all element symbols in black and white style. [ATTACHMENT filename='blackandwhitestylesheetpartdef.png']

You can:

- Use the default style sheet (default functionality).
- Switch between style sheets (predefined and/or custom ones). See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space='UAF2P'] page.
- Create your own style sheets either by reusing and customizing an existing (predefined or custom) style sheet (e.g., DefaultStyleSheet ) or creating your own. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom style sheets' space='UAF2P'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheets' space='UAF2P'] pages.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Symbol styles represented by the *DefaultStyleSheet*(left)**and *BlackAndWhiteStyle*Sheet**(right) style sheets.

##### Accessing predefined style sheets

To access the predefined style sheets

1. In the Containment tree, expand the following nodes: Used Projects > 3DS SysML Customization > DassaultSystemesStyles>StyleSheets .
2. Right-click the StyleSheets package and in the shortcut menu, click Open under Textual Editor . The style sheets' textual notation is opened in the Textual Editor.

##### *DefaultStyleSheet*style sheet components

The *DefaultStyleSheet*style sheet contains multiple rules for symbol styles conditioned by element metaclass. The styles applied to the rules use predefined style definitions. You can view them once you access the style sheet.

###### *DefaultStyleSheet*rules

The rules are structured with comments based on the element metaclass and look like the following:

/* -------------PART/PORT---------*/ 
partpartPortRule :> rule { 
part:>> condition : ByElementMetaclassCondition { 
refitem:>> elementMetaclass = (SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata, SysML::Systems::PartDefinition.metadata, SysML::Systems::PortUsage.metadata, 
SysML::Systems::PortDefinition.metadata); 
} 
view:>> style : AbstractGreenStyle; 
}

###### *DefaultStyleSheet*predefined style definitions

You can view the full list of predefined style definitions in the 'Predefined style definitions' column on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] page.

The predefined style definitions are listed in the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions*packageand look like the following:

/* ------------------colors---------------------- */

viewdefAbstractGreenStyle:>ShapeStyle{ 
attribute:>>fillColordefault"#E3F2E3"; 
attribute:>>compartmentFillColordefault"#F6FBF6"; 
attribute:>>penColordefault"#123D15"; 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

</em>package.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="defaultstylesheetpartdefinition.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Predefined style sheets" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7a2f61b-0c6f-49b0-bec3-b333763901e9"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="4244c2dc-a134-4f42-b907-0f12298dcd3a"><em>BlackAndWhiteStyleSheet</em><em><br /></em>The <em>BlackAndWhiteStyleSheet </em>style sheet contains rule-based styles for representing all element symbols in black and white style.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="blackandwhitestylesheetpartdef.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Predefined style sheets" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You can:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="e13cb431-5054-47d8-8537-dec3e7db5adc">Use the default style sheet (default functionality).</li><li data-uuid="a56f9c58-99ad-4bf5-8899-e9a46c2cded5">Switch between style sheets (predefined and/or custom ones). See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Activating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activating style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li><li data-uuid="e6ff4d90-aba3-4cd8-9abe-b6c1872b34a2">Create your own style sheets either by reusing and customizing an existing (predefined or custom) style sheet (e.g., <em>DefaultStyleSheet</em>) or creating your own. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Custom style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> pages.</li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="predefined_style_sheets.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Predefined style sheets" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Symbol styles represented by the <em>DefaultStyleSheet </em>(left)<em> </em>and <em style="text-align: left;">BlackAndWhiteStyle<em>Sheet</em> </em>(right) style sheets.</h6><h3>Accessing predefined style sheets</h3><p>To access the predefined style sheets</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ddf094c9-b09f-46c8-838b-8688877b7476">In the Containment tree, expand the following nodes: <em>Used Projects</em>&gt;<em>3DS SysML Customization</em>&gt;<em>DassaultSystemesStyles&gt;StyleSheets</em>. </li><li data-uuid="f26e90e0-4bc2-4fb3-b09e-6411800c7ec9">Right-click the <em>StyleSheets </em>package and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Open </strong>under <strong>Textual Editor</strong>. The style sheets' textual notation is opened in the Textual Editor. </li></ol><h3><em>DefaultStyleSheet </em>style sheet components</h3><p>The <em>DefaultStyleSheet </em>style sheet contains multiple rules for symbol styles conditioned by element metaclass. The styles applied to the rules use predefined style definitions. You can view them once you access the style sheet.</p><h4><em>DefaultStyleSheet </em>rules</h4><p>The rules are structured with comments based on the element metaclass and look like the following:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca6b2093-0ef3-4862-ba4c-084454fa0061"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* -------------PART/PORT---------*/</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> partPortRule :&gt; rule {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; condition : ByElementMetaclassCondition {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; elementMetaclass = (SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, SysML::Systems::PartDefinition.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, SysML::Systems::PortUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">,</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                                                 SysML::Systems::PortDefinition.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">);</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; style : AbstractGreenStyle;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><em>DefaultStyleSheet </em>predefined style definitions</h4><p>You can view the full list of predefined style definitions in the 'Predefined style definitions' column on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Style sheet conditions and styles tables]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p><p>The predefined style definitions are listed in the <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><em>DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions </em>package </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">and look like the following:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd8930c6-eb12-4f40-bc00-fe0251db1dc8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* ------------------colors---------------------- */</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">AbstractGreenStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#E3F2E3&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">compartmentFillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#F6FBF6&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">penColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#123D15&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298092841 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00124: (2026x) Prerequisites

- page_id: `298092841`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298092841/2026x+Prerequisites
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-01-06T15:58:44.970+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To work with all of the SysML v2 features, the following software should be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Installation and system requirements' space='UAF2P']:

- One of the following CATIA Magic/No Magic modeling tools:
  - Cameo Systems Modeler (Enterprise Edition only)
  - Cameo Enterprise Architecture
  - Magic Cyber Systems Engineer
  - Magic Systems of Systems Architect
- SysML v1 Plugin and Cameo Requirements Modeler. The plugins must be installed in addition to the following plugins to use SysML v2 in the modeling tool.
- SysML v2 Plugin. It provides the modeling tool with the majority of the SysML v2 features. It also includes the SysML v2 Textual Editor, allowing you to work with the SysML v2 features using textual notation via a textual editor.
- (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin Documentation' space='SYSML2EP']. It allows you to conduct static SysML v2 model evaluations (mathematical calculations, calculation usage evaluation, constraints evaluation, and requirements evaluation).
- (Optional)[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Migration from SysML v1 to SysML v2' space='UAF2P']. It enables model transformation features.

#### NOTE: Font Compatibility on macOS

Font Compatibility on macOS

The default font used in the modeling tool for SysML v2 is Roboto. However, because Roboto is not pre-installed on macOS, certain non-Latin characters may not render correctly when using the tool on macOS. To avoid character rendering issues, please [download](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto) and [install](https://support.apple.com/guide/font-book/install-and-validate-fonts-fntbk1000/mac) the Roboto font on your machine.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To work with all of the SysML v2 features, the following software should be <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Installation and system requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[installed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:</p><ul><li data-uuid="b4e417ca-7c0b-42ce-b1ce-e75a9e2fccef"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">One of the following CATIA Magic/No Magic modeling tools:</span><ul><li>Cameo Systems Modeler (Enterprise Edition only)</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture</li><li>Magic Cyber Systems Engineer</li><li>Magic Systems of Systems Architect</li></ul></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2856abbc-c3f8-496c-8c09-435ed730c927">SysML v1 Plugin and Cameo Requirements Modeler. The plugins must be installed in addition to the following plugins to use SysML v2 in the modeling tool.</ac:inline-comment-marker> </li><li>SysML v2 Plugin. It provides the modeling tool with the majority of the SysML v2 features. It also includes the SysML v2 Textual Editor, allowing you to work with the SysML v2 features using textual notation via a textual editor.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">(Optional) <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2EP" ri:content-title="SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It allows you to conduct static SysML v2 model evaluations (mathematical calculations, calculation usage evaluation, constraints evaluation, and requirements evaluation).</span></p></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(Optional)<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Migration from SysML v1 to SysML v2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It enables model transformation features.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01f4567c-73fc-466b-9eb4-7d700ccaadd9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Font Compatibility on macOS</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default font used in the modeling tool for SysML v2 is Roboto. However, because Roboto is not pre-installed on macOS, certain non-Latin characters may not render correctly when using the tool on macOS. To avoid character rendering issues, please <span><a href="https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto">download</a> and <a href="https://support.apple.com/guide/font-book/install-and-validate-fonts-fntbk1000/mac">install</a></span> the Roboto font on your machine.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093353 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00125: (2026x) Printing

- page_id: `298093353`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093353/2026x+Printing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:00:27.218+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model management > (2026x) Working with views > (2026x) View image export
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can print:

- Active diagram
- Any diagram(s)
- All open diagrams
- Selected symbols of active diagram

You can specify the printing options or preview pages by using the following dialogs:

- Print Dialog
- Print Options Dialog
- Print Preview Window

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Print dialog

##### Printing

###### Printing an active diagram

To print an active diagram

1. Open a diagram you want to print.
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the main toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='print.png'] Print Active Diagram button.
  - In the main menu, click File > Print or press Ctrl+P on the keyboard . On the Print dialog, Print what area, select Active diagram , and click the Print button.

###### Printing any diagram(s)

To print any diagram(s)

1. In the main menu, click File > Print or press Ctrl+P on the keyboard . The Print dialog opens.
2. In the Print what area, choose Selected diagram(s) . The Select Diagram dialog opens.
3. Select the diagram(s) you want to print.
4. Click OK .
5. Click the Print button.

###### Printing all open diagrams

To print all open diagrams

1. In the main menu, click File > Print or press Ctrl+P on the keyboard . The Print dialog opens.
2. In the Print what area, select All opened diagram(s) .
3. Click the Print button.

###### Printing selected symbols of active diagram

To print selected symbols of active diagram

1. On the diagram, select the symbols you want to print.
2. In the main menu, click File > Print or press Ctrl+P on the keyboard. The Print dialog opens.
3. In the Print dialog, Print what area, choose Selected symbols of active diagram
4. Click the Print button.

##### Print preview

You can preview diagrams before printing them using the **Print Preview**window.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Print Preview window

###### Previewing diagrams before printing

To preview diagrams before printing

1. Open the Print Preview window in one of the following ways:
  1. In the main toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='print_preview_button.png'] Print Preview button.
  2. In the main menu, click File > Print Preview .
2. Select the diagram you want to preview from the list on the left side of the window. The diagram is previewed on the right side of the window.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can print:</p><ul><li>Active diagram</li><li>Any diagram(s)</li><li>All open diagrams</li><li>Selected symbols of active diagram</li></ul><p>You can specify the printing options or preview pages by using the following dialogs:</p><ul><li>Print Dialog</li><li>Print Options Dialog</li><li>Print Preview Window</li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="print_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Printing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Print dialog</h6><h3>Printing</h3><h4>Printing an active diagram</h4><p>To print an active diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a diagram you want to print.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the main toolbar, click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="print.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Printing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><strong>Print Active Diagram</strong> button.</li><li>In the main menu, click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Print</strong> or<strong> </strong>press <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Ctrl+P </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">on the keyboard</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. The <strong>Print</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Print what</strong> area, select <strong>All opened diagram(s)</strong>. </li><li>Click the <strong>Print</strong> button.</li></ol><h4>Printing selected symbols of active diagram</h4><p>To print selected symbols of active diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram, select the symbols you want to print.</li><li>In the main menu, click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Print </strong>or<strong> </strong><span>press </span><strong>Ctrl+P </strong><span>on the keyboard. </span>The <strong>Print</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Print</strong> dialog, <strong>Print what</strong> area, choose <strong>Selected symbols of active diagram</strong></li><li>Click the <strong>Print</strong> button.</li></ol><h3>Print preview</h3><p>You can preview diagrams before printing them using the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Print Preview </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">window</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.<br /></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="print_preview_window.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Printing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Print Preview window</span></h6><h4>Previewing diagrams before printing</h4><p>To preview diagrams before printing</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Print Preview </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">window in one of the following ways:</span><ol><li>In the main toolbar, click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="print_preview_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Printing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <strong>Print Preview</strong> button.</li><li>In the main menu, click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Print Preview</strong><span>. </span></li></ol></li><li>Select the diagram you want to preview from the list on the left side of the window. The diagram is previewed on the right side of the window. </li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093004 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00127: (2026x) Project options

- page_id: `298093004`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093004/2026x+Project+options
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T14:38:55.974+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the **Project Options** dialog to specify general project-specific options.The **Project Options** dialog includes option groups, each designated for specific features and containing lists of corresponding options. Groups are represented using the tree structure.

##### Opening the Project Options dialog

To open the Project Options dialog

- In the main menu, click Options > Project . [ATTACHMENT filename='project_options_dialog.png']

##### Modifying project options

To modify project options

1. Open the **Project Options** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Project options' space='UAF2P'] .
2. Locate the option you want to modify. Use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Quick filter' space='MT']uick filter to find the needed option in the list. Filtering is performed in all available options, not only the selected option group. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']Click the **Show Description** button to display the project option description section at the bottom of the dialog to read about the option functionality. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. An option value can be changed by typing a new value, setting a value to true / false , or selecting a value from the list. Click the **Reset to Defaults** button to restore default option values.
4. Click OK .

##### Project Options

The table below displays the default project options. Additional options may appear based on installed plugins.

| Option | Default value | Value options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| General |  |  |  |
| Diagrams |  |  |  |
| Image Export |  |  |  |
| Time Limit to Build Diagram | 60 | Number | Specify the maximum time (in seconds) allotted for diagram building while exporting a diagram. If the time limit is exceeded, the diagram is not exported. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the time limit. |
| Image Height Limit | 10000 | Number | Specify the maximum image height (in pixels) for diagram export. If the height limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image height limit. |
| Image Width Limit | 10000 | Number | Specify the maximum image width (in pixels) for diagram export. If the width limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image width limit. |
| Export Preference When Limits Exceeded | Export Cropped Diagram | Export Cropped DiagramDo Not Export | Specify the diagram export preference if the diagram image height and/or width limits are exceeded. |
| General |  |  |  |
| General |  |  |  |
| Path to Used Project | <project.dir><install.root>\profiles<install.root>\modelLibraries | Custom | Specify the paths to store used projects and extension elements. |
| Decimal Places | 4 | Number | Specify the maximum number of decimal places to be used when displaying the real number in tables and element Specification windows. |
| SysML v2 |  |  |  |
| SysML v2 |  |  |  |
| Active View Creation Dialog | SysMLViewCreationDialog | Custom | Specify the Active Create View Dialog configuration. |

<p><br /></p><p>Use the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog to s<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">pecify general project-specific options. </span>The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog includes option groups, each designated for specific features and containing lists of corresponding options. Groups are represented using the tree structure.</p><h3>Opening the Project Options dialog</h3><p>To open the Project Options dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_options_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Project options" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Modifying project options</h3><p>To modify project options</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Options dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Project options" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Locate the option you want to modify.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7672be49-876c-41ce-9a52-f49c111fe1d2"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Quick filter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[q]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>uick filter to find the needed option in the list. Filtering is performed in all available options, not only the selected option group.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_options_quick_filter.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Project options" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click the <strong>Show Description</strong> button to display the project option description section at the bottom of the dialog to read about the option functionality.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The Show Description button in the Project Options toolbar" ac:alt="The Show Description button in the Project Options toolbar"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_group_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Project options" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>An option value can be changed by typing a new value, setting a value to <em>true</em>/<em>false</em>, or selecting a value from the list.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37f945ab-1085-4e2c-9a06-d8bd3009f2a3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Click the <strong>Reset to Defaults</strong> button to restore default option values.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Project Options</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="894362a7-7ba8-4d42-9b4e-d7a987937a9d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The table below displays the default project options. Additional options may appear based on installed plugins.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 102.448%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 15.8723%;" /><col style="width: 17.7904%;" /><col style="width: 10.3098%;" /><col style="width: 56.0085%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Option</th><th>Default value</th><th>Value options</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>General</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" title="Background color : Light blue 35%" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Diagrams</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" title="Background color : Light grey 100%" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Image Export</td></tr><tr><td>Time Limit to Build Diagram </td><td>60</td><td>Number</td><td>Specify the maximum time (in seconds) allotted for diagram building while exporting a diagram. If the time limit is exceeded, the diagram is not exported. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the time limit. </td></tr><tr><td>Image Height Limit</td><td>10000</td><td>Number</td><td>Specify the maximum image height (in pixels) for diagram export. If the height limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image height limit. </td></tr><tr><td>Image Width Limit</td><td>10000</td><td>Number</td><td>Specify the maximum image width (in pixels) for diagram export. If the width limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image width limit. </td></tr><tr><td>Export Preference When Limits Exceeded</td><td>Export Cropped Diagram</td><td><p>Export Cropped Diagram</p><p>Do Not Export</p></td><td>Specify the diagram export preference if the diagram image height and/or width limits are exceeded.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" title="Background color : Light blue 35%" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>General</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">General</td></tr><tr><td>Path to Used Project</td><td><p>&lt;project.dir&gt;</p><p>&lt;install.root&gt;\profiles</p><p>&lt;install.root&gt;\modelLibraries</p></td><td>Custom</td><td>Specify the paths to store used projects and extension elements.</td></tr><tr><td>Decimal Places</td><td>4</td><td>Number</td><td>Specify the maximum number of decimal places to be used when displaying the real number in tables and element Specification windows.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>SysML v2</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="grey">SysML v2</td></tr><tr><td>Active View Creation Dialog</td><td><em>SysMLViewCreationDialog</em></td><td>Custom</td><td>Specify the Active Create View Dialog configuration.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093028 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00128: (2026x) Project Statistics

- page_id: `298093028`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093028/2026x+Project+Statistics
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:05:42.408+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The project statistics feature calculates detailed statistics for the entire project and exports the results to a separate file. The exported statistical report will help solve performance problems you may be experiencing. Project statistics are calculated in the main project and, if selected, in used projects. The generated detailed project statistics file includes quantitative data about diagrams, elements, Project/Environment options, hardware & system, Teamwork Cloud projects, and performance.

#### NOTE: Confidential Information

Confidential Information

The exported file contains the names of the main project, used projects (if selected), and used elements. If they are confidential, you should select **Confidential**to keep their names hidden.

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Exporting detailed statistics

To export detailed statistics

1. In the main menu, select Tools > Project Statistics .
2. In the Project Statistics dialog, click the Export Detailed Statistics button. The Export Detailed Statistics Options dialog opens.
3. Select data to export along with the OS and hardware, diagrams/elements by types, and project options statistics that are included by default.
4. Specify other export options:
  - Select Include Used Project Statistics to generate and export used project statistics as well.
  - Select Confidential to keep the project element names hidden in the exported statistics report.
5. Click Export . The Select Location dialog opens.
6. Select the location where the file should be exported and click Save .
7. When the statistics file is generated, click Close .

##### The project statistics file

The generated detailed project statistics file includes the following quantitative data:

**Teamwork Cloud projects**

- Teamwork Cloud project local cache size on disk
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache latest blob size
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob size by revision
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob count by revision
- Teamwork Cloud project history depth
- Teamwork Cloud local project binary cache size
- Teamwork Cloud local project cache dir size
- Root object count by type
- Used projects count

**OS & Hardware**

- Hardware (RAM, OS, disk size and type) and Java info (heap)
- Restricted dir size

**Diagrams**

- Total size of diagrams binaries
- Diagram count by type
- Diagram count by kind

**Elements**

- Total element count
- Total size of strings
- Elements by meta-type
- Features by size
- String features by string length
- KerML based Elements statistics
  - Non-Standard Library Elements
  - Standard Library Elements

**Metadata**

- Diagram statistics
- Element statistics

**Standard Libraries**

- Diagram statistics
- Element statistics

**Symbol Styles**

- Diagram statistics
- Element statistics

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The exported statistical report will help solve performance problems you may be experiencing. Project statistics are calculated in the main project and, if selected, in used projects. The generated detailed project statistics file includes quantitative data about diagrams, elements, Project/Environment options, hardware &amp; system, Teamwork Cloud projects, and performance.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="811f00dd-6f47-4a6a-99ee-daa68a80650c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Confidential Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The exported file contains the names of the main project, used projects (if selected), and used elements. If they are confidential, you should select <strong>Confidential </strong>to keep their names hidden.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_statistics_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Project Statistics" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h3><h3>Exporting detailed statistics</h3><p>To export detailed statistics</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Tools </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Statistics</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Statistics</strong> dialog, click the <strong>Export Detailed Statistics</strong> button. The <strong>Export Detailed Statistics Options </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Select data to export along with the OS and hardware, diagrams/elements by types, and project options statistics that are included by default.</li><li>Specify other export options:<ul><li>Select <strong>Include Used Project Statistics</strong> to generate and export used project statistics as well.</li><li>Select <strong>Confidential </strong>to keep the project element names hidden in the exported statistics report. </li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Export</strong>. The <strong>Select Location </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Select the location where the file should be exported and click <strong>Save</strong>.</li><li>When the statistics file is generated, click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>The project statistics file</h3><p>The generated detailed project statistics file includes the following quantitative data:</p><p><strong>Teamwork Cloud projects</strong></p><ul><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache size on disk</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache latest blob size</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob size by revision</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob count by revision</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project history depth</li><li>Teamwork Cloud local project binary cache size</li><li>Teamwork Cloud local project cache dir size</li><li>Root object count by type</li><li>Used projects count</li></ul><p><strong>OS &amp; Hardware </strong></p><ul><li>Hardware (RAM, OS, disk size and type) and Java info (heap)</li><li>Restricted dir size</li></ul><p><strong>Diagrams</strong></p><ul><li>Total size of diagrams binaries</li><li>Diagram count by type </li><li>Diagram count by kind</li></ul><p><strong>Elements</strong></p><ul><li>Total element count</li><li>Total size of strings</li><li>Elements by meta-type</li><li>Features by size</li><li>String features by string length</li><li>KerML based Elements statistics<ul><li>Non-Standard Library Elements</li><li>Standard Library Elements</li></ul></li></ul><p><strong>Metadata</strong></p><ul><li>Diagram statistics</li><li>Element statistics</li></ul><p><strong>Standard Libraries</strong></p><ul><li>Diagram statistics</li><li>Element statistics</li></ul><p><strong>Symbol Styles</strong></p><ul><li>Diagram statistics</li><li>Element statistics</li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093010 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00129: (2026x) Quick find

- page_id: `298093010`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093010/2026x+Quick+find
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:29:55.023+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the Quick Find functionality to search for specific elements in the model. The simple and easy-to-use dialog is designed to locate the needed element by its declared or short name.

##### Opening the Quick Find dialog

To open the Quick Find dialog, do one of the following

- On the Containment tab toolbar click the [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_find_button.png'] Quick Find button. **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**
- On the main menu, click Edit > Quick Find .
- On the keyboard, press Ctrl+Alt+F or Cmd+Opt+F .

Search results are displayed in the drop-down list, which opens when you start typing the search phrase. Click the element you need in this list or correct the search query to perform a new search. The element will be selected in the Containment tree and on the active view if the element has symbols on the active view.

##### Quick Find dialog elements

The following table provides descriptions of the **Quick Find** dialog elements.

| Dialog element name/image | Dialog element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Search for | Options group | Click Any Element to search in all elements of your project.Click View to search only in views. |
| Search By Name | Text box with a drop-down list | Type a phrase to search for in element names. See the search results in the drop-down list.The following elements are excluded from the search results: (e.g., in, abstract, derived, etc.)Element metaclasses (e.g., part, attribute, package)Feature values (owned expressions) (except for Association and Connector)Expressions (except for ConstraintUsage and CalculationUsage)Features owned by MetadataFeature (e.g., Documentation, Comment, TextualRepresentation)Special actions (e.g., ControlNodes, AssignActionUsage, SendActionUsage)Features owned by special actions (parameters) |
|  | Menu button | Click the button and customize search options. Match Text AnywhereTo search for matching phrase in any part of the element name, click the command to add the check mark.To explicitly search from the beginning of element name, click the command to remove the check mark.Use Camel CaseTo search for matching phrase typed in camel case, click the command to add the check mark.To search for matching phrase typed in regular case, click the command to remove the check mark.You can use camel case as follows:Type only the first letters (either capitalized or not) of each word, for example, "and" or "AND" to find "Analysis and Design".Type skipping spacers, for example, "cv3" to find "CV-3". |
| More | Menu button | Click the button to load the next 100 matches. By default, the first 100 matches are shown in the dialog. |
| Expand | Menu button | Click the button to load all model elements. |
| Apply Filter (Ctrl+Space) | Check box | Click to clear the check box, if you need to remove the search scope filter. If you clear the Apply Filter check box once, your selection is remembered for the next time. |
| Filter Options | Menu button | Click the button and customize the search scope filter options. Ensure the Apply Filter check box is selectedHide Standard/System LibrariesReduces the search results list by hiding elements that belong to the Standard/System Libraries. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298092874 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00130: (2026x) Quick Properties Panel

- page_id: `298092874`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298092874/2026x+Quick+Properties+Panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T14:27:54.101+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Modeling tool environment > (2026x) User interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

TheQuick properties panelhas two tabs, as shown in the figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Quick properties panel

- The**Zoom**tab zooms in on the current view.
- The**Change Sets**tab shows the latest changes compared against the most recent project version saved in the local repository

##### Zoom tab

The Zoom tab allows you to preview and zoom in or out of the active view.

###### Opening the Zoom tab

To open the Zoom tab

- Do one of the following:
  - In the Model Browser, click the Zoom tab.
  - In the main menu, click Window > Zoom .

###### Displaying a view in the Zoom tab

To display a view in the Zoom tab

- Do one of the following:
  - Click the view in the Containment tree.
  - Click the view tab.

###### Zooming in or out of the active view

To zoom in or out of the active view

- In the Zoom tab, drag the corners of the blue square.

###### Quickly accessing any part of the view

To quickly access any part of the view

- In the Zoom tab, drag the blue square to the desired part of the view. The selected view part will be displayed in the view pane.

###### Fitting the view to the window

To fit the view to the window

- In the Zoom tab, on the blue square [ATTACHMENT filename='fit_window_button.png'] , click the Fit in Window button or press CTRL+W .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The<span> </span>Quick properties panel<span> </span>has two tabs, as shown in the figure below.</span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Quick Properties Panel.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Quick Properties Panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Quick properties panel<br /></span></h6><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="46fd7003-a8ed-4568-ad0d-9e8c4ab6be83"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The<strong><span> </span>Zoom</strong><span> </span>tab zooms in on the current view.</span></li><li data-uuid="c78a0d05-2041-48e5-b870-ba338c539b38"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The<strong><span> </span>Change Sets</strong><span> </span>tab shows the latest changes compared against the most recent project version saved in the local repository</span><br /></span></li></ul><h3>Zoom tab</h3><p>The Zoom tab allows you to preview and zoom in or out of the active view.</p><h4>Opening the Zoom tab</h4><p>To open the Zoom tab</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="6cf87d6d-7eb0-4d62-b46d-132f671b127b">Do one of the following:<ul><li data-uuid="2c019ebd-4651-4261-95ac-5174af036d50">In the Model Browser, click the <strong>Zoom</strong> tab.</li><li data-uuid="2883cdd8-740d-4568-a3f8-c5dc5916d0bc">In the main menu, click <strong>Window</strong> &gt; <strong>Zoom</strong>.</li></ul></li></ul><h4>Displaying a view in the Zoom tab</h4><p>To display a view in the Zoom tab</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="c6496948-19a1-4644-9c22-ee7c84719d49">Do one of the following:<ul><li data-uuid="3c7d0f43-6b07-45d0-a40f-f928409b1a02">Click the view in the Containment tree.</li><li data-uuid="220fab6f-5a55-4ee3-9d81-ad56b8d04635">Click the view tab.</li></ul></li></ul><h4>Zooming in or out of the active view</h4><p>To zoom in or out of the active view</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="66cde2a1-d593-4f63-a420-bb08b9176757">In the Zoom tab, drag the corners of the blue square.</li></ul><h4>Quickly accessing any part of the view</h4><p>To quickly access any part of the view</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="47f27ae9-f94a-4cbc-9e1d-c5a67592a11b">In the Zoom tab, drag the blue square to the desired part of the view. The selected view part will be displayed in the view pane.</li></ul><h4>Fitting the view to the window</h4><p>To fit the view to the window</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="8c24fcec-4bbc-4800-9d61-b93e782f9c9b">In the Zoom tab, on the blue square <ac:image ac:height="28" ac:width="26"><ri:attachment ri:filename="fit_window_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Quick Properties Panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, click the <strong>Fit in Window</strong> button or press <strong>CTRL+W</strong>.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093566 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00131: (2026x) Redefinition

- page_id: `298093566`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093566/2026x+Redefinition
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:51:38.019+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Relations > (2026x) Specializations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The redefinition relationshipis a kind of subsetting used to replace an inherited feature by redefining.

##### Redefinition

###### Redefining a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view

To redefine a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view

- Right-click an inherited usage element symbol in a view and in the shortcut menu select**Refactor**, then**Redefine**.

###### Redefining a usage via the Textual Editor

To redefine a usage via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the usage element that you want to be the redefining one.
2. Follow the element name with either one of the following:
  1. keyword redefines
  2. textual symbol :>>
3. Follow the keyword with the name of the redefined usage element.
4. Click the Synchronize button. partdefEngine{ 
partcylinders[4..8] :Cylinders; 
}partdieselEngine:Engine{ 
partdieselEngineCylinders[4] :>>cylinders; 
/* or */ 
partdieselEngineCylinders[4] redefinescylinders; 
}

###### Redefining a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To redefine a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P'] on an element symbol executing the Set Redefined Feature command.

###### Redefining a usage via the view palette

To redefine a usage via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, select redefinition .
2. Draw the line with the arrowhead going from the redefining element to the inherited one. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Redefining a usage via the Create Relationship command

To redefine a usagevia the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click the element that you want to be the redefining one.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click Outgoing , and then in the submenu, click redefinition . The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to redefine. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to redefine and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to redefine from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK .

###### Redefining a usage via the Specification panel

To redefine a usage via the Specification panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] page.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093562 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00132: (2026x) Reference subsetting

- page_id: `298093562`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093562/2026x+Reference+subsetting
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:51:26.255+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Relations > (2026x) Specializations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A reference subsettingis a kind of subsetting relationship. While a subsetting usage can have multiple subsetted usages, a reference subsetting usage can only have one specific subsetted usage.

##### Reference subsetting

###### Reference subsetting a usage via the Textual Editor

To reference subset a usage via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the usage element that you want to be the referencing one.
2. Follow the element name with either one of the following:
  1. keyword references
  2. textual symbol ::>
3. Follow the keyword with the name of the referenced usage element.
4. Click the Synchronize button. part'drone in delivery mission'{ 
perform action ::>'deliver goods'; 
/* or */ perform action references'deliver goods'; 
} 
action'deliver goods'{ 
action'start mission';action'fly to destination'; 
first'start mission'then'fly to destination'; 
}

###### Reference subsetting a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To reference subset a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P'] on an element symbol executing the Set Referenced Feature command.

###### Reference subsetting a usage via the view palette

To reference subset a usage via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, select reference subsetting .
2. Draw the line with the arrowhead going from the referencing usage to the referenced one. [ATTACHMENT filename='Reference subsetting.png']

###### Reference subsetting a usage via the Create Relationship command

To reference subset a usagevia the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click the element that you want to be the subsetting one.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click Outgoing , and then in the submenu, click reference subsetting . The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to subset. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to subset and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to subset from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Hold Shift to select multiple elements. </li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Reference subsetting" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>button. ## PAGE 00134: (2026x) Rendering basics

- page_id: `298094055`
- space_key: `UAF2P`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the basic procedures for working with renderings and rendering definitions.

##### Creating rendering usage and definition elements

###### Creating rendering usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create rendering usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *rendering def*for a rendering definition.
  2. *rendering* for a rendering usage.
2. Specify the element name.
3. (Optional) Specify the rendering's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

renderingdefGraphicalRendering;

renderingasTreeDiagram : GraphicalRendering;

##### Rendering views

###### Rendering views via the Textual Editor

To render views via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the curly braces of the view's body, declare the keyword render followed by the name of the rendering usage you want to use .
2. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Part Structure View'{ 
renderasTreeDiagram; // references the rendering usage *asTreeDiagram* elsewhere in the model 
}

## PAGE 00135: (2026x) Rendering views

- page_id: `298094053`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094053/2026x+Rendering+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:30:33.636+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A rendering feature specifies how a particular view and its exposed elements are supposed to be rendered, i.e., displayed for viewing. Once you have[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating views' space='UAF2P'], specified the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exposing elements for views' space='UAF2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Filtering elements for views' space='UAF2P'] the exposed elements, you can specify the renderer for the view.

##### Renderings, rendering styles, and view definitions

The following table maps renderings and rendering styles available in the modeling tool to corresponding predefined view definitions.

| View rendering kind | Rendering style | Renderings | Subsets of the renderings | Rendering definitions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Graphical | Nested-view | Views::asInterconnectionDiagramView definitions using the renderer:Standard predefined symbolic view definitions:'General View' (through inheritance from 'Symbolic View' view def) 'Interconnection View' (through inheritance from 'Symbolic View' view def) 'Action Flow View' (through inheritance from 'Symbolic View' view def)'State Transition View' (through inheritance from 'Symbolic View' view def)Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions:NestedViewUsagesNestedView (by subclassifying NestedView)PartsNestedView (by subclassifying UsagesNestedView)Parts&PortsNestedView (by subclassifying UsagesNestedView)ActionsNestedView (by subclassifying UsagesNestedView)StatesNestedView (by subclassifying UsagesNestedView) | - | Views::GraphicalRendering |
| Tree-view | Views::asTreeDiagramView definitions using the renderer:Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions:TreeViewPartsTreeView (by subclassifying TreeView)ActionsTreeView (by subclassifying TreeView)RequirementTreeView (by subclassifying TreeView) | - | Views::GraphicalRendering |  |
| Tabular | - | Views::asElementTable | DS_Views::CoreViews::asTableView definitions using the renderer:Standard predefined tabular view definitions:'Generic Table' (subsets asTable inherited from 'Base Table' via subclassification)'Requirements Table' (subsets asTable inherited from 'Base Table' via subclassification) | Views::TabularRendering |

##### Rendering views

Ways to render views:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Rendering views' space='UAF2P'] . You can render views through inheritance, e.g., if the view is defined by a view definition that contains a renderer, the renderer is inherited by and applied to the defined view.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Rendering views' space='UAF2P'] . You can specify a renderer after the expose and filter declarations directly for the specific view, using the keyword render .

###### Inheriting a renderer

Generally, renderings are specified for view definitions, which are then used to define specific view usages, the latter inheriting the renderer specified for its definition.

See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Rendering views' space='UAF2P'] table for all renderings available in the modeling tool and the predefined view definitions that use them. Choose the view definition based on the renderer specified for it that would be the most suitable for your view, then define the view by the view definition. However, many predefined view definitions carry specific filters and other customizations that will be inherited in addition to the renderer, thus choose accordingly. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular views' space='UAF2P'] pages.

The corresponding procedures are:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] page.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] page.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space='UAF2P'] page.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space='UAF2P'] page.

#### PANEL: Explanation in textual notation

Explanation in textual notation

// explanation and example of a symbolic view *General View*inheriting a renderer through subclassification

abstractviewdef<sv>'Symbolic View':>Visualization{ 
//... 
renderViews::asInterconnectionDiagram; //view definition has the *Views::asInterconnectionDiagram***renderer specified for it 
}

abstractviewdef<bsv>'Base Symbolic View':>sv{ // view definition 'Base Symbolic View' inherits the *Views::asInterconnectionDiagram***renderer through subclassification of 'Symbolic View' 
partbaseViewPalette:ViewPalettes::BasePalette:>>palette; 
}

viewdef<gv>'General View':>bsv,StandardViewDefinitions::gv{// view definition 'General View' inherits the*Views::asInterconnectionDiagram***renderer through subclassification of 'Base Symbolic View' 
partgeneralViewPalette:GeneralPalette:>>baseViewPalette; // view definition also contains a specific view palette 
}

view'General View':DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv; // the specific view is defined by the view definition 'General View', inheriting its renderer and view palette

// explanation and example of a tabular view *Generic Table* inheriting a renderer through subclassification

abstractviewdef<bt>'Base Table':>bgv{ // view definition *'Base Table'* has the *asTable*renderer specified for it 
renderasTable; 
}

viewdef<gt>'Generic Table':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bt{ // view definition 'Generic Table' subsets the *asTable*renderer inherited through subclassification of *'Base Table'* 
renderrenderingasTable:>>asTable; 
}

view'Generic Table':DS_Views::TabularViews::gt;// the specific view is defined by the view definition 'Generic Table', inheriting its renderer

###### Specifying a renderer

Instead of creating views defined by view definitions that already have specified renderers, you can specify therenderer directly for the new view.

The corresponding procedures are:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] page.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space='UAF2P'] page.

#### PANEL: Explanation in textual notation

Explanation in textual notation

viewnewView{ // new view element is not defined by any view definition 
renderViews::asInterconnectionDiagram; // the view has a renderer *asInterconnectionDiagram*, rendering it as a 'General View' symbolic view with nested-view rendering style 
}

style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> &lt;gv&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'General View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">bsv</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">, </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StandardViewDefinitions::gv</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view definition 'General View' inherits the </span></span><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Views::asInterconnectionDiagram</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><em> </em>renderer through subclassification of 'Base Symbolic View'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">generalViewPalette</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">GeneralPalette</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">baseViewPalette</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view definition also contains a specific view palette </span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'General View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::SymbolicViews</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::gv</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;       <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the specific view is defined by the view definition 'General View', inheriting its renderer and view palette </span></span> </p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// explanation and example of a tabular view <em>Generic Table</em> inheriting a renderer through subclassification</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">abstract</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> &lt;bt&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Base Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">bgv</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view definition <em>'Base Table'</em> has the <em>asTable </em>renderer specified for it</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> &lt;gt&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Generic Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::CoreViews::bt</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view definition 'Generic Table' subsets the <em>asTable </em>renderer inherited through subclassification of <em>'Base Table'</em></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Generic Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews::gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the specific view is defined by the view definition 'Generic Table', inheriting its renderer </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Specifying a renderer</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Instead of creating views defined by view definitions that already have specified renderers, you can specify the<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">renderer directly for the new view.<br /></span></p><p>The corresponding procedures are:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating undefined symbolic views using renderers via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating undefined symbolic views using renderers via the Textual Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating symbolic views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating undefined tabular views using renderers via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating undefined tabular views using renderers via the Textual Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating tabular views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b729039d-3f68-43b7-a173-7378442669ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Explanation in textual notation</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">newView</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {   <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new view element is not defined by any view definition</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">Views::asInterconnectionDiagram</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the view has a renderer <em>asInterconnectionDiagram</em>, rendering it as a 'General View' symbolic view with nested-view rendering style</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094058 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00136: (2026x) Rendering views basics

- page_id: `298094058`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094058/2026x+Rendering+views+basics
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-11T20:10:40.675+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Rendering views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two ways a rendering usage can be specified for a view:

50%

- by referencing the rendering usage defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the render keyword (unnamed element) or the rendering keyword (named element) . [ATTACHMENT filename='rendering.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Rendering views basics' space='UAF2P'] :
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Rendering views basics' space='UAF2P']

50%

- by containing an owned rendering usage, i.e., the view renderer is the owned rendering usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the rendering keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='rendering2.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Rendering views basics' space='UAF2P'] :
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Rendering views basics' space='UAF2P']

##### Rendering views by referencing renderings defined elsewhere in the model

###### Rendering views via the Textual Editor

To render views via the Textual Editor

1. In the body of a view for which you want to reference a rendering, declare one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed rendering, declare the render keyword, then follow with the name of the rendering you want to reference.
  2. For a named rendering, declare the rendering keyword, name the new usage, and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space='UAF2P'] the rendering you want to reference.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

viewvehicleModel{ 
renderasTreeDiagram;// unnamed rendering 
renderingasNewTreeDiagram::>asTreeDiagram;// named rendering 
}

##### Rendering views via the owned rendering usage itself as the renderer

###### Rendering views via the Textual Editor2Rendering views via the Textual Editor

To render views via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the view's body, declare the rendering keyword, specify the element name, and then the rendering's body.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

viewvehicleModel{renderingasCustomTreeDiagram:GraphicalRendering;// // new view-owned rendering usage}
